"Ow!" he manages, still conscious! Damn he's getting good at this! "Next time I'm wearing something more comfortable."

Carefully, and with a silly grin on his face, Andrew stands up. He's a little wobbly but OK.
The new Andy looks slightly shorter, maybe Nineteen or Twenty by looks, short cropped dark hair, brown eyes and fairly ordinary looking features. He probably looks a little better than you to be honest... which you rarely are.

"Odd, this feels stiffer than the other Mask." He says wriggling his legs a little.

"Heck, Robert is hyperactive, what can you expect! I felt weird in his body too."

You both glance at each other after you realize what you just said.

"Fag!" Andy says laughing at you, but is too focused on his working legs to make much fun of you. "Hmm, I think it might be more than that actually, maybe merging me and you was a bad idea? I think it got part of my paralysis into the image, just not fully."

You slap you hand against your head. Why didn't you think of that?

"Damn I'm sorry, but the legs work?"

He flexes his toes and smiles.

"Yep, good enough for government work. Hmm, maybe if I get the cash together we can make a mask up without me as a donor?"

He's offering to buy another Mask? Oh perfect! Got to make sure he stays interested then.

"OK, boy or girl?" you say nonchalantly.

Andrew's eyes go wide.

"What did you say?" he says, and you can't help but let a grin split your face.

"Do you want a Mask of a boy or a girl? What, you telling me you never thought about that this whole time I was explaining it? Anyone this scans is stored, then you wear the Mask and you ARE that person. Doesn't have to be the same sex!"

"Oooh shit! That's kind of freaky." He looks as if he's not as into the idea as you are. Hmm, maybe not a good way to keep him interested.

"Look, you can decide that later, for now this should do fine. I ought to head out and start buying supplies. Some of the stores I need close at ten on Sunday."

As you stand Andrew grabs you and hugs tight.

"I am NOT going to forget this Will. Thanks for thinking of me first."

You smile and hug back. "No problem Andy."

Waving, you leave him with his new Mask, taking the Robert Mask with you. It's getting late so you dash out to try and get the supplies you need. It's a pinch but you manage to get enough to make two more masks and head home. Crashing down on your bed you look at the table where you made the last two Masks. Not good enough. You need somewhere private to do this.

Then the obvious strikes you. The clubhouse! OK, you haven't been there in months but the locks should still be good. You stumbled across the Clubhouse a few years ago now, a basement in an abandoned Elementary school, but even though some of the upper building has been renovated and turned into a community center no-one thought to do anything with the basement. You put a lock on to be sure no-one could spoil your fun... or at least you'd know when they bolt cut the padlock, and have used it to hang out at with a few friends for years. It'd make the perfect place to make foul stenches and burn stuff and no-one would find out!

Unwilling to wait till tomorrow night you keep an eye out for your parents and brother and once they've all gone to bed you head out to the Clubhouse with bags full of supplies. You've got the instructions down pretty good now and manage to do the operation without referring to the book and the horrible job of translating. When you return to your house at 1AM you have two new gray Masks.

You get an idea and use a couple of old belts to strap the Masks, one to each knee. Then you use the proscribed cloth and buff them, one with each hand. You'll have to shift the belts to burnish those parts of it, but this'll cut your time in half... though after a few minutes of this you find it's more tiring than before as well. For tonight you give in, put the masks away and head to bed, but soon you'll have another two blank Masks to play with. Well, next week you will anyway. Maybe you can get some polishing done over the weekend?
1111221111.title: Fix the Mask and try it out.

choices: Try to keep to your lie with Andy.
Maybe you should tell him about the book?
11112211111.content:
(Text by Gimlet )

The next day you even wonder if you can get away with being sick and stay home to polish masks, but decide against it. Your mom is rather over protective and would be coming in and out with chicken soup and pills all day. The possibility that she'll interrupt you while you're polishing would be high, and you want to keep the whole thing as secret as possible.

At school you manage to avoid the Molester, a small miracle in itself, and remember to ask Caleb about Lisa. Again he hasn't talked to Eva but you seem less angry at him now. Are you letting her go or just getting way to involved with the Masks to think about much else? Or are you burying your emotions on the subject? You don't think so, but how would you know? You're not used to thinking about your emotions like this.

That evening as you reach your house you see Andrew's door open and he waves you in. Smiling you head over. He's still in his old body, in the wheelchair.

"So given up on the new body already? Come on, you can't have worn it out that quick?"

"Har har!" he says, and pulls the mask from under the wheelchair as he pushes the front door shut. "Right now I'm keeping Freddy to myself. I still haven't got all the details of how to 'inherit' Andrew Gary's fortune down pat and don't want to do something to mess things up."

"Fortune?" you snort. "Since when are you loaded! You make a hell of a lot more than me, but still, you ain't loaded."

You and Andrew head into the living room as he peers into his Mask.

"Maybe not now, but if I can figure out a way to get the life insurance money... Who boy!"

You raise an eyebrow at him.

"Dang, you think big. How can you pull that off without a body?"

"Like I said, a few details aren't there yet." You shake your head laughing "Hey I'll get it, don't you worry! Hey want a beer?" You nod and he wheels into the kitchen, but comes back running in his new body. "Catch!"

"You're doing that change damn fast!" you comment, a little acidly but take a sip from your beer and try to relax. "I'm just glad that it's working for you."

"Oh it's better than just 'working'. It's incredible. Still feels a little stiff but I've managed to work out enough to run and jump a bit. Feels a bit like arthritis or something, but minimal. I'll definitely need another mask later."

As you take another sip of beer something lands in your lap with a slap.

"Seven hundred, as promised, in twenties. I figured if your buying chemicals and stuff from stores you don't want it to be in too large a bills."

You pick up the thick wedge of cash and grin wolfishly.

"Thanks Andrew, this will really help. Damn!" you say, as you realize you didn't check the materials for the new item. Will what you bought work for that? You glanced at the instructions for the new metal strips earlier in the week, but with all the polishing you didn't want to distract from what you were doing. Well, that and the damn text didn't describe what the thing was supposed to do! You'd rather make Masks that you're sure of than leaping into the unknown, well for the moment.

"What is it?" Andrew asks.

"I was just realizing I'd forgotten about something I wanted to try," you say lamely, trying to sound casually interested only.

"Ooooh, what? The next item after the Mask? What is it?" he asks eagerly. Damn, you should have kept your mouth shut!
11112211111.title: Andy gives you remaining cash he owes.

choices: You have to try it out. No matter how weird it is.
Not now. Too freaky! Finish this mask and start on another two.
111122111111.content:
(Text by Gimlet )

"I don't know really!" you say, trying not to give any details. Frankly it's not far from the truth. Who knows what the strip does? "Look, you're really getting into your new body, I need to sort some things out, so do you mind if I head out?"

Andrew looks a little disappointed but waves goodbye good naturedly from the living-room, unwilling to change back to let you out. You lock the door and close it and head home. Well at least Andrew is getting some immediate use out of these things! You have a useless mask (well, nearly useless), two blank ones that still need a lot of polishing... and a wad of cash? OK, so not a total loss!

Grinning you bound upstairs, giving only a cursory "I'm home!" to your mom before dashing upstairs.

That evening you get next to no polishing done. If it isn't Robert annoying you it's your mom wanting to get you to be involved with the church more, or your dad demanding you do your chores. Luckily no-one sees what you're doing, but it's still frustrating. In the end you go to bed early and set your alarm for 1AM. Dashing out in the dead of night you unlock the Clubhouse and start polishing in private.

You almost stay too long, but manage to get home before everyone gets up in the morning. Tired and irritated you spend Saturday morning doing more chores (did this household really make this much trash in a day?) but by mid-day you manage to head out and again go to the Clubhouse to polish. Watching TV while polishing the two Masks on your knees is very tiring. You can't alternate between arms and they ache like mad. Plus, nice as it is in here it is a little claustrophobic. Though it's tiring you get both masks mostly done by the afternoon, but are way too tired to continue. You rest a little before heading home, making sure to lock up.

On Sunday (after church) you head back and pick up where you left off, but it's way too stuffy for you in here right now. The masks look mostly done. Only two days of work, but very intensive two days. Maybe you can take them in public? It'd be nice to get some fresh air, but would it be safe? Well, who knows about them anyway? It's not as if they'll work until their polished fully.

With a smile you head to the park, leaving the belts behind and deciding to focus on one at a time for the last little bit. It's a sunny day at least, a little too hot for the clothes your wearing actually, but you don't want to go back and change now. Sitting at a bench you set one Mask on the table and start burnishing the other Before mid-day you have one finished and swap over, watching wispy clouds drift across the sky as you polish.

"ARROOOOooo-up!" comes the plaintiff bark, cut short and you turn in time to see the mask you just finished melt into the forehead of a large Doberman!

"Nooo!" you leap over to the dog as it lays unconscious on the ground. "That's not possible, is it?"

It must have leaped up at the shiny mask and knocked it off the table and onto its face.
Glancing around you don't see anyone searching for their dog so wait in silence for the time to be up. What if it's longer for a dog? What if the thing can't scan a dog and stays like this forever! What if...

Slowly you see the Mask return, is that slower than before? You think so. There was absolutely NO mention of animals in the book. If this isn't what the mask was designed for, who knows what the results will be?

After a minute of slowly emerging it is free and you grab the mask and run, not wanting to be around the dog when it wakes.

Your heart is pounding when you get home, collapsing on your bed. You put the nearly polished mask away and stare at the dog mask. Yep, in the crystal finish you can see the image of that Doberman. It's recorded the body of a dog! Wait, could that actually work? Could you BECOME a dog? Oh this is getting weirder and weirder.
111122111111.title: Try to keep to your lie with Andy.

choices: You head to the Reptile house and end up looking at an Anaconda.
You head to the Aquarium and peer in at a big Octopus.
1111221111111.content:
(Text by Gimlet )

OK, it's dangerous, but you have to try it out. It's just too tempting!

You strip this time, not wanting to risk damage to your clothes or you in the process. Laying on on your side on the bed you bring the mask up to your face and slowly take a deep breath and press it home before you can second guess yourself.

*PAIN!*

You writhe around on the bed in panic, searing bolts of white hot lead pouring through your face and into your body. Luckily the mask covers your mouth so muffles your screams of terror, otherwise your mom or dad might have burst in while you were changing! You aren't thinking about that though, the mind numbing agony is high on your list of things to be concentrating on, but it doesn't last. Your body seems to take much longer, as much as a minute or more before the mask fully melts into you and your body succumbs to unconsciousness but eventually you do. While you sleep your body distorts and shifts till a Doberman is laying on your bed.

Consciousness returns in a painful haze, but thankfully it's receding. Will you have to go through that again when you take the mask off? Suddenly panic fills you. TAKE THE MASK OFF! How can you say the words that remove the mask? You're a DOG!

With a thump you roll off the bed and land on the floor. Oh this is weird. You feel like you're in a monster suit, twisted like a pretzel. Rear legs are... tricky. Like standing on your toes the whole time, but also the front legs; balanced on your fingers... er paws. Twice you fall over to your side but eventually to stay upright, if a bit wobbly.

Now that you can stand pretty well... you collapse to the floor! Struggling you fumble your paws up to your face and press, trying to grip your face.

"Rrrroo Ruff!" you bark, trying to say the words, desperately. You repeat it twice more and...

*Pain!*

This time it's not so bad. Perhaps, like Andrew, you're getting used to this. It's just worse when it's not a human body! You still writhe around on the floor in pain, but surprisingly don't faint.
"Will?" comes your mother's voice as you shakily pull the mask from your, now human, face. "Are you OK?"

"Y.. Yes mom," you manage, and struggle under the covers to hide your nakedness, but she doesn't come in. Relaxing you try to let the memory of that swell of agony subside. Would you do that again? It was easier taking it off, hopefully next time to put it on it wouldn't hurt so much.

A while later you wake, realizing you fell asleep without noticing! The clock says 4PM, you've slept three hours. Still a little of the day left. You hide the dog mask next to Robert's and take the nearly polished mask out. You head out with the intention of going to the Clubhouse, but instead head into town. You aren't sure where you're going at first, then realize what it is. The Zoo is nearby.

Smiling you idly polish the mask while you head in. Do you really want to waste another mask on an animal? There are all sorts of things you could be doing. At very least get a girl body to fondle at night. Still you find yourself wandering around the Zoo trying to see if any animal sparks your imagination.
1111221111111.title: You have to try it out. No matter how weird it is.

choices: Fix the Snake Mask and try it out now!
Maybe you should merge it with a human? A hybrid animal Mask?
11112211111111.content:
(Text by Gimlet )

Dark slitted eyes stare back at you, forked tongue slipping out. The head of this huge snake is not as big as you'd have thought given it's bulky body. Would the mask work if it can't fit on the creature's face? Hmm, well this one is the biggest snake here and it's face is maybe the size of a toddler's.

You were only passing by the Reptile house but suddenly had a thought. Snakes are incredibly strong. Thinking back to Lester you wondered if you could deal with him. Walking in you thought better of it, after all after you do attack him, having him talk about being squeezed by a snake might get people into checking for weird things at the school, if anyone believed him that is. You don't want that much attention. As you looked around at all the various snakes in here you imagined squeezing the hell out of him, then realized it would do you little good. After all he wouldn't know it was you doing it, and if he DID know... you'd be in even worse trouble.

If he lived.

The thought brings you to an abrupt halt. Could you really kill Lester? OK he has given so much pain to you and seems to revel in it, you know he is going to go on to being evil outside of school... but kill him?

You look in at a large puff adder, this species venom could kill. Could you really do that? But how would you dispose of the body?

That's when you saw the Anaconda.

Maybe you could get a mask on it. If it works it would be child's play to... deal with Lester, and then... eat him? Would that work? It would sure do away with the evidence.

The more you peer in at the snake the more you like the idea. You've never liked snakes much, but not really disliked them either, and now with your new perspective you can imagine being inside a snake's body and the thought of sinuously moving around like that is definitely interesting. It's face is dark, greens and yellow-green mottled patterns, and honestly you kind of like it. It looks... serene somehow. Unfortunately it's in a large tank with a sealed hatch. You can see a lock from here. Caleb is better at this than you, but... You get out your tool kit, originally for electrical repairs but you've added some finer tools and picks, and peer around. The room is dark and you are fairly sure no-one is looking. Wait, what about cameras? Again you peer around and can't see any. The Zoo is seriously under funded but would they really have no cameras?

One final check and you step up onto the ledge, reaching up to the high hatch. Why is it so high up? Hmm, maybe so the snake doesn't get out as easily when it's opened? The lock is simple and snicks open seconds later. Easier than the tumblers to the girl's locker rooms! You get out the mask that you finished polishing as you were wandering around the Zoo and hold it over the snake's head. If you drop it and miss, or the snake dodges away you won't get it back! Quickly you pull out a power cord you were going to use at the Clubhouse and loop it loosely over. Easily you lower it down, planning on swinging it over the...

*Snap!* the snake's head shoots up, snaps at the lip of the Mask and pulls it free of the cord!

"Shit!" you whisper, as the snake nibbles it, then drops it. Wasted! How are you going to get it now! You're not climbing in there with...

You stare unbelieving as the snake's head pokes around the mask, then wriggles under it. It must have covered something it wanted. With a jerk the Mask sticks, it works! The snake starts thrashing around, then slows, finally stopping at the mask melts in.

You let the door pull-to, but not lock, and step down from the ledge. You've got ten minutes or so, maybe longer. You think the dog took a bit more than normal.

It's incredible, and you really can't believe that it worked, well you haven't got the mask to try out yet, but so far it's working.

A couple wander through, peering in at the snakes and you nervously look at your watch, then at the Anaconda. The time's up and the Mask hasn't reappeared! They leave and you dash to the tank. Nope, still no Mask. Fretfully you cast about, hoping something hasn't gone wrong. Another ten minutes go by and you are about to bail out of here when a slight blue glow comes from the snake's head. Quickly you get on the ledge and open the hatch and lean in. You reach down and grab the Mask just as it's finished emerging, the snake still asleep. Pulling back you hear the hatch click as the lock engages and you look at your prize. Yep, the image of a snake head is there, body trailing below out of sight. God damn it worked!

So far, you keep reminding yourself. Can't get your hopes up yet!

It's with great trepidation that you head out, almost heading for the clubhouse, but you are tired after all the tension back there and there aren't any beds in that basement. It'd be private but really uncomfortable. When you get home your Dad is out late at 'The Company' but your mom greets you pleasantly. She's still watching her soaps on the living room TV and doesn't really seem interested in chatting. Thankfully. You just want to sleep, but are intoxicated with the idea of the snake Mask.
11112211111111.title: You head to the Reptile house and end up lookin...

choices: Take the Mask off and hope you finish changing before Dad gets to the Kitchen.
Make a break for the kitchen window.
111122111111111.content:
(Text by Gimlet )

Collapsing on your bed you stare at the mask. This is so freaky! A snake. What's next, a Shark? An Eagle? Now there's a thought! Wait, are there any bird's heads that would fit the mask? Even closely? Hmmm probably not. Bummer, it'd cool to fly like a bird, or rather BE a bird.

Idly you pick up the fixing solution and nervously paint the inside.

It's done. Now to test it. You strip and hop on the bed, holding the Mask.

Um, yeah. Test it right now.

A few minutes later you're still staring at the thing, wondering if you'll ever have the courage to put it on. Have you wasted yet another Mask? Laying the Mask carefully on your bed you go back to the book and leaf through what you can open. No matter how well you search, how carefully you translate, there is no mention of animals at all. Just 'people', or 'corpus' which does mean person or body, or several other things but not human. It could have said 'hominis' or something close which WOULD mean human or body or person, but it uses 'corpus' Is that on purpose? Being generalized to make the magic work? Who knows how this stuff works.

With a sigh you close the book and turn back to the Mask. You can't put this off... OK, you can, but you aren't going to. This is just too fun an idea. OK, you don't want to, but you want to. It's gonna hurt, but it will be fun, and at least you know you can get back to human again. For the next few minutes your mind whirls, thinking contrary thoughts on the whole thing. Finally you shake your head and try to concentrate.

Again you pause, staring at the Mask's surface, the image stored within. Can you really...? Yes you can do this!

With a gritting of teeth you lay on the floor (the bed isn't nearly big enough for the Anaconda!) and bring the Mask to your face... and you pause. Last time it was so painful you thrashed around. If you were still thrashing as an Anaconda the piles of stuff around you room would go flying.

You know it's an excuse, but use it to delay things as you tidy up (first time in months!), pushing things under the bed, up on the top of cabinets etc. Eventually you have a nine foot space around you, probably enough.

Once more you lay on the ground and nervously bring the Mask up. This is it! With your heart racing and your hands shaking you bring it up to your face and press it on.

For seconds nothing happens and you begin to wonder if it worked after all.

"P***A***I***N!!!"

This is worse! MUCH worse! Agony sears your veins, your head threatening to explode, and thankfully you pass out quickly this time.

Eyes plop open blearily. Damn what were you dreaming again? Something funky but you can't quite place it. It was a snake you think but... You try to shift and feel hardness beneath you, but when you try with more effort to move, something feels... wrong. Your arms are numb, and your legs! Twisting in shock you look down and... YOU'RE A SNAKE!

It comes back to you, the dog, the Zoo and your final test, along with the blinding agony. Blinking you stare at yourself. It worked! Shit!

For minutes you just stare at yourself in amazement, not sure whether to be happy or not. It does feel cool, but not what you expected. Hmm, maybe try to move? Snakes have that whole rippling movement thing, their belly's shifting in ripples, dragging them along. Can you do that?

You try to slither, undulating your belly, but it's hard just to move. After a while you can slowly and carefully move your body, but that slithering thing seems beyond you. Instead you move in a kind of press, lift maneuver. Is that how sidewinders move? You don't think so. Maybe you don't have snake instincts? This is more the way an engineer might think of for a snake should move.

It feels crude, but you manage and shift and crawl your way about your room. Your vision is in black and white and your sense of smell seems mostly based on your tongue. Very weird! OK, maybe it's not your tongue but something in your mouth that the tongue brushes against. After checking your body is much longer than your room is wide, maybe twice as much. That makes you a little over seventeen foot long, maybe twenty tops. Big certainly, but big enough to eat Lester? Well shows you've seen on TV picture Anaconda's eating animals bigger than humans, but you'd have to test this. How?

Wait, Mom has a 20lb turkey in the kitchen on the counter. It's bigger than your snake head, not as big as person but it would be a start.

You nudge the door open with your muzzle and try to hear if your mom is still in the living room. It's tricky, you can't really hear very well. Do snakes have ears? It seems easier to hear, at least deeper notes, when your head is on the ground. Maybe it's the vibrations through the paneling?

When you're sure she's still watching TV you wriggle out, awkwardly without slithering, and try to get down the stairs. This is a challenge! You could change back, walk down, then change again, but the pain when you change would be hard to hide in the kitchen, even though there is more space there. No, just head down and deal with this now.

Several times you feel coils slipping and almost tumble down the stairs. This is hard! Why couldn't the Mask record the snake's instincts? You should be able to do this effortlessly! You've seen snakes navigate far more awkward conditions than this.

Peering up the hall at the bottom of the stairs you see the light of the TV going, she's still there, and you turn to the Kitchen. Up on the counter is the turkey. Your head is smaller so it looks totally huge from your perspective. It's out of the wrapping thankfully, sitting in a pan. You position coils beneath it around a table leg to catch the pan and use your tail to nudge it off the table, positioning your head, wide open, beneath it.

There is a little clunk as the pan hits the table leg but it seems quiet. The Turkey slams into your jaws and you desperately try to swallow. Halfway in you feel a deep cold seep into you and realize it wasn't long ago she took it out of the freezer! It's still partially frozen! Damn it! You're too far gone now though and swallow heavily, feeling lots of little teeth fold out to grip it. Some times they don't fold out and it's awkward. You haven't quite got the hang of this yet. The turkey doesn't seem a stretch for your new body, and soon is working it's way easily down your throat. That feels kind of cool, though Lester? You imagine someone struggling while you do this and wince. You'd have to deal with him first. Are you really a killer though?

As the melting meat oozes nicely down into your bulkier body you ponder that... but are interrupted by a slam. DAD'S HOME! OH SHIT!

You glance around in panic and try to figure a way out of this. The front door is too close to the stairs, he'd notice if your dashed that way. You could try the Kitchen window, but that might make a lot of noise. You don't want him coming in and seeing a giant snake wriggling out the window!

Or you could take the Mask off! You don't have much to lose now!
111122111111111.title: Fix the Snake Mask and try it out now!

choices: You bump into him on the landing.
There are odd noises coming from Roberts bedroom!
1111221111111111.content:
(Text by Gimlet )

You hear a deep rumble. Is that your dad calling to your mom? It's now or never, he'll probably be in here soon!

You wrap your tail around your face and pull, hissing the words, but nothing happens. You try again, levering against your lower jaws and nose. It doesn't work! Twisting and wriggling you try for something closer to the 'brow and nose' grip that the book proscribes and heave, yet again hissing the words. Finally you feel it give and you tense waiting for the pain explosion.

You aren't disappointed.

Again you faint dead away, and when you wake up Dad is leaning in over you.

"Son Son! Will are you OK?"

Groaning you sit up. Yes, you're human, but where is the Mask?

"I... I think so Dad. What happened?"

"You tell me, I found you naked in the Kitchen. I don't think your mother would appreciate it so I hauled you into my office. What were you doing in there naked?"

You use the echoes of the pain to try and fake expressions of anguish to cover your nervousness. You don't want him to know about the Masks.

"I don't know. I went to bed and... Oooh, I don't feel so good." This is true. You have a heavy cold lump in your stomach. The turkey! Glancing down you expect a huge bulge in your belly, but it's flat. What the heck?

"You've never sleep walked before son. Is this on the up and up?"

"You think I'd *gnnn* make this up? Oooh god!"

Dad carries your groaning body upstairs quietly, and as he goes through the downstairs hall you glance towards the kitchen. There is the Mask! Under kitchen table! Oh god, you can't tell your dad about it, but to leave it there... You'll have to risk it.

He lays you in bed and tucks you in.

"Lets not mention the sleep walking to your mom hmm? I'll tell her you don't feel good. I think we've got some Nyquil somewhere."

Laying in bed you ponder if this could have gone much worse! You've got a lump of ice in your stomach, your dad thinks you are sleep walking, you've lost the snake Mask downstairs and are coming down with a cold or something from the turkey (you guess) and don't have the energy to go grab it. What if someone puts it on! Oh god!
There is nothing you can do about it though, and you just shiver and cough through the next few days, fading in and out of consciousness, your mom being as overprotective as ever. You get pretty sick of chicken soup until by Thursday morning you start to feel a little better. The lump of the turkey is still there but it's long since melted. Will you need to eat again soon? Who knows? That's one hell of a meal!

By night time you feel a little better but fake still being ill. Your mom insists you are starting to come out of it so you guess you aren't as good at faking it as you thought, but she says you might be OK by Saturday.

One AM comes and the alarm you set goes off. Everyone is asleep and you creep out of bed, stiff and sniffling, but OK. Dressing in slacks and a t-shirt you tip toe downstairs and go into the kitchen with trepidation. Peering under the table you curse voluminously, but quietly. The Mask is gone. Honestly you didn't have much hope it would still be there, but you just wanted it to be. Couldn't life be straight forwards once?

OK, who has it? Mom? Naww, she'd have asked you about it. She'd have asked everyone in the house about it. Dad? Doubtful. Why would he root around under the kitchen table. Robert? Oh god Robert!

He's a pain, but he doesn't deserve to be turned into a snake! At least not without knowing how to change back! You creep upstairs again to see if you can figure out if Robert did find it.
1111221111111111.title: Take the Mask off and hope you finish changing ...

choices: She recovers and... likes it? Aparantly she LOVES snakes, always has!
Umeko hates what happened but is fascinated by the masks. What else do they do?
11112211111111111.content:
(Text by Gimlet )

"Robert!" you gasp, at least quietly.

"Dick!" he says pleasantly in reply "Dude I knew you were faking it!"

"Robert!" you say, seriously "I wasn't, but that isn't important. Did you find a mask downstairs?"

He grins evilly.

"Aww, did Willsy loose his itsy bitsy masky?"

You guess he still thought you were pretty out of it as he doesn't react quick enough when you pin him hard against the wall by his shoulders.

"Fuck off Robert! This is serious. You could be in danger! You didn't try the mask did you?"

He looks like he thinks your nuts, but with a twinge of nervousness. It's obvious you're serious.

"No you dick, but you won't get it back now. I sold it to Umeko today." he says with a smirk.

Your heart skips a beat and you step back. The shocked look on your face gets to the little punk somehow and his expression falters.

"Y... you sold it... to Umeko?" you manage weakly. "Oh shit. Oh shit shit shit!"

"Hey, it's a fucking mask! What the hell's up with you?" He manages weakly, but your terror at the situation is obviously infectious, he just doesn't know why the thing is dangerous, and you'd like to keep it that way!

"Robert, go back to bed, if mom asks I went out for some fresh air cos I started feeling better, OK? And for you own good you shouldn't mention the mask."

For a moment he looks belligerent, but your expression of seriousness at the last bit seems to get to him.

"Maybe..." he says weakly.

"Trust me Robert, you don't want anything to do with the damn things!" and you grab a coat and shoes and run out into the night. Umeko's family lives quite a ways off so it takes a while, but you run the whole way. Gasping for breath you lean against a streetlight and peer up the driveway to Umeko's house with terror. Police cars are there, lights flashing in the night and the family is up and seems panicked. And there is no sign of Umeko in the group there in front of the house!

"Fuck!" you gasp, trying to get your breath back "Umeko? Why did you have give it to Umeko you little shit!" you whisper to yourself vehemently. Robert would pay for this! "Umeko? Do have any idea how to get me out?" a tail shake; 'no' "Damn! Well, maybe you could head to the clubhouse. I'm pretty sure I've got some antacids there. It's not far, but as a snake? I'm not sure how long it'll take to get there." You pause, considering the other phone calls you were considering. "There is another option. We could call someone for help." you feel her jerk at that. "I know, but... there is someone else who knows something, though not much, about the masks. You see I sold a mask to Andrew."

She starts scribbling.

'you what?'

"Early on I needed money for materials, and he needed legs. I merged our faces to make a mask of a new person for him. With it he can walk again."

'ok, I understand. why didnt you tell me'

"um, I guess I didn't think of it?" you manage weakly as you get a few spasmodic squeezes from her, you guess this wouldn't be good place to piss off Umeko from! "He doesn't know much about them. I told him I found out about how to make them online, but the website is gone."

'he believ that?'

"Um, yes?" you say awkwardly. You guess it does sound kind of stupid like that. "He seemed to. Look he's already grateful to me for giving him the ability to walk back, he hasn't pushed about if I know about anything else other than masks."

'what else?'

"Well, I haven't read ahead yet much, actually the book won't LET you read ahead much, but I haven't tried the next spell. I'm actually kinda scared. These masks are powerful enough. What else is in there?" you shake your head "Anyway, Andrew knows the masks exist even if not about the book. We can tell him I made a snake mask and you put it on ... and..."

'I ate you accidetly?'

You could swear she hissed a laugh there.

"OK... awkward I know, but... we need to do something! If we get Andrew's help he's going to have to know what's happened. Can you stand him knowing you ate me?" Oh that sounds so weird on so many levels... this could give you a permanent head ache just thinking about it!
1111221111111111111111111121111111111.title: Umeko can't cough you up! You're stuck!

choices: He suggests syrup of ipecac to make her throw up. He's got some at home.
Right now he's got nothing. Just take antacids till you figure this out.
11112211111111111111111111211111111111.content:
(Text by Gimlet )

Umeko doesn't write anything for quite a few seconds, her tail idly tapping the dirt slowly. Just as you are about to speak up she starts scribbling.

'I dont like him knowing, but ok. we need help'

"Thanks for understanding Umeko, don't worry Andrew is a great guy, he'll be considerate I'm sure." another snake sigh at that. You wonder how many 'considerate guys' Umeko has been close to, unable to tell them why she wasn't interested in them.

You flip your phone out again, glancing at the illuminated stomach as you do... very distracting, and dial Andrew's number.

"Hello? Gray residence." For a moment that voice on the other end of the line floors you, then you realise it IS Andrew, wearing his mask.

"Er, Fred? I didn't think you were going to be... around." you say, lamely.

"Freddy. And no I'm not around much, I'm helping Andrew out. You can help him too, you know how difficult it can be around here, what he thinks about phone lines." oh yes, Andrew and his conspiracy theories. Well at least he's not a nut about it.

"Sure... Freddy, it's just we need Andrew's help."

"We?" he says curiously "You and Caleb been breaking things again?"

You chuckle to yourself and feel Umeko squirm. Can she hear Andrew's side of this? Maybe it's easier to hear things inside her. Echoing perhaps? Who knows.

"Say," 'Freddy' says before you can answer "What's that gurgling sound?"

"Oh, that. Nothing, just a little indigestion. Um, that is something you can help with though. When you come could you bring some antacids? A lot of them?"

"Where do you want ANDREW to come?" he says frostily. Oh yeah, this is 'Freddy' not Andrew.

"Out by the Zoo on 17th Street, East exit, outside the gate. Um... look for something weird and... don't be surprised and... think of what you know and some odd possibilities... OK?" hey, you can be cryptic too! Serve him right... though it might just make it more difficult for him to help you. *sigh*

"Um, sure. I'll tell Andrew all that." he says more quietly, obviously wondering what you're talking about. "Anything else?"

"Yeah, make sure the back of... his 'truck' is cleared out. We'll need the space. Oh, and bring a big tarp."

"Hmm, OK. Andrew'll be along soon I'm sure. Take care Will." and he hangs up.

"Well," you say closing the phone, "He'll be here soon." Umeko seems to collapse a little "Hey, it'll be OK, I'll be here for you. Heh I'm sure not going any place!" No laugh from that lame joke. Maybe you should stop trying to cheer her up?



It's not that long a wait. You're guessing he took longer to get the mask off, get his wheelchair into his SUV (Which he insists on calling a truck for some reason) than actually driving here. His Blue 'truck' pulls up across the street and he gets out on the lift. Soon you can see him as Umeko tracks him, framing his wheeling form in the view from inside her maw. When he gets close you ring him again.

"Hey Andrew, go up to those bushes up ahead, and try to relax. There is NOTHING dangerous there, OK? Promise!"

He doesn't even answer, just nods and wheels in your direction. This is it!

When he gets within a few feet Umeko gently lifts her head from the foliage and opens wide, your hand sticking out and waving calmly.

"JEEE... I mean, oh god! What have yo... Who...?" He sighs and collects himself for a moment. "OK, which one of you is Will?"

"See Umeko, told you he was smart?" you say, she just hisses around your arm.

Pulling it back inside you settle back while Umeko slides back into cover, though Andrew can still see her from where he is you think. Tricky to tell from in here.

"OK," you say up Umeko's throat "It's like this. The... snake here is Umeko, and yes, she ate me. But she can't seem to throw me back up. Can you think of a way to get me out of here?"
11112211111111111111111111211111111111.title: Call Andrew for help.

choices: Do some serious research online about snakes. Find out how to make her throw up!
Work on Umeko, try to ease her conscience and want to throw you up.
111122111111111111111111112111111111112.content:
(Text by Gimlet )

"Say what?" Andrew says. "Oh hey I didn't sign up for that! I mean, look I like Freddy but you? Um, could it be someone else?" Umeko shakes her head emphatically and then writes.

'fewer people that know better'

"Well, I guess I agree with that... but... being a girl? That sounds kinda freaky."

"It's actually pretty cool." you say, chuckling.

"Yeah, well I'd expect that from you Will." He pauses for a few minutes, thinking. Neither you nor Umeko interrupt him. "OK... I guess I can do that, but for how long?"

"As long as it takes I guess?" You say "She's got to figure out a way to cough me up."

'My parents will want me there at dinner, then to bed.' she writes, shakes the tray then goes on 'morning, breakfast. Then come back here. Thats all'

"That's all huh?" Andrew mutters. "If I do you two have to try to work out your differences and get Will out of there. I'm not doing this for more than one night. This is going to be weird enough as it is!"

Umeko starts going over what Andrew should do to make her parents believe it's her, but she closes her mouth and you are unable to read it. Was that on purpose? Andrew makes a few comments, but nothing beyond 'un huh' or 'if you say so' so that doesn't help. Eventually he sighs, says he needs a drink and heads out of the room.

"Umeko. We're alone, right?" She nods "Are you sure about this?"

Her mouth opens again looking down on the tray.

'yes. you trust him. right?'

"Yeah, but that's not what I meant. This is a lot to hand out. You're asking someone to BE you! Take over your life for a time. That's some pretty heavy shit!"

She chuckle/hisses around you for a few moments.

'yes. heavy. could you put my mask on for a bit Will?'

You blink. The mask?

"Um, OK Umeko. Sure." You fumble for it in the bag then pause. "You like the idea of a girl in your stomach? Rather than me?"

'its better. but both are nice' she writes, a bit hesitantly. This is obviously hard for her.

"I know. And I know you wanted me as a girl in here. I shouldn't have asked, I just... this whole thing is weird."

Without saying anything else you put the mask back on, shifting and squirming inside as you turn into her alter ego again.

'thank you Will'

"Hey," you say in her voice "Anytime."

For several minutes she just lays there, twisting a little, moving around you and squeezing, feeling you inside her. Eventually you hear Andrew return and hastily take off the mask. The shock of it makes you miss part of what he says, but it's obvious he's ready. You think he asked for the mask so without replying you push your arm up Umeko's throat slowly holding the mask. It's tight, but after a few minutes with Umeko helping it plops free and Andrew takes if from you.

"This is a loaner Andrew. You understand that right? That's Umeko's mask, not yours."

"Oh trust me kid," Andrew says "She's welcome to it when this stuff is all sorted out!"

You hear a pregnant pause, a sharp intake of breath, then a thump. A few moments later you hear Umeko's voice.

"Oh my god!" Andrew says as Umeko. "Quick, her clothes. This is too weird!"

Ahh right. You wriggle and squirm, pushing the bag upwards. Now that you're male again it isn't hard to get it up past your chest into her throat, but coughing it up is tricky. At least the bag squishes down as her throat muscles work on it and after a few minutes it plops up onto the sofa, a bit wet on the outside but none the worse for wear.

"Hmm, wrinkled, but dry at least." Andrew mutters to himself as you hear him pull out the clothes.

"I've still got my phone in here" You say, though where you'll put it to make sure you don't lose it is beyond you. "So you can call me if you need help from us."

Rustling sounds come from outside but Umeko's jaw resolutely stay shut. Hey, it's not as if you haven't seen her naked... though you guess it IS Andrew as her... yeah, that would be a bit icky.

"OK," comes Andrew in Umeko's voice "I can do this, I can do this! Just be angsty."

Umeko opens her maw at last onto the tray.

'Im not ansty! just not close to parents!'

That's odd, from what you've seen she had a loving, caring family? She was always nice, polite and friendly to them when you were around.

"I'll do my best Umeko, just don't answer the door for anyone, OK?"

Both of you chuckle at that.

"Not a problem Andy. Take care of yourself!"

You see him/Umeko wave as he leaves the house. Now what do you do?
111122111111111111111111112111111111112.title: Right now he's got nothing. Just take antac...

choices: Do some serious research online about snakes. Find out how to make her throw up!
Work on Umeko, try to ease her conscience and want to throw you up.

Both of you chuckle at that.

"Not a problem Andy. Take care of yourself!"

You see him/Umeko wave as he leaves the house. Now what do you do?
1111221111111111111111111121111111111122.title: Work on Umeko, try to ease her conscience and w...

1111221111111111111111111121111111111122.content:
(Text by Gimlet )

You've always wanted to spend a Saturday night with Umeko, but not IN her. You have to admit though it's a lot nicer than you would have expected before today!

'what you want to do?' she scribbles 'scrabble?'

You laugh.

"I was more thinking of talking Umeko." You say, and feel her tense. "I know it's hard but we have to work this out."

You stare out her mouth as she looks up at the clock. 8PM, hopefully Andrew is having Dinner with Umeko's parents now. It's a little late for dinner in your book, your family usually eats around 6:30. You wonder about it idly, and then ask Umeko about that.

'Dad insists. he work late often and want family together for meal.'

"Well that sounds cool Uncle Scott, I mean your Dad, sounds like he wants a good family. Why don't you get along?"

'oh, this my fault?' she says, a little erratically.

"I'm sorry, I didn't mean..." you say, awkwardly.

'sorry, old argument.' she scribbles, then shakes the tray blank. 'Dad wants family bad, mom too, but Dad forces it.'

"Forces?"

'he doesn't raise voice, doesn't hit, he just insists. a lot' she shakes the tray again 'he doesnt like to not have his way. doesnt listen'

"Ahhh. Yeah Dad can be a little like that too sometimes, but generally he's OK."

'I know. My Dad talk about uncle Harris a lot.' another tray shake 'he envy you big family.' she pauses 'god I sound like caveman writing here!'

"Hey it's tricky I know. You'll get your voice back soon enough Umeko. Don't worry."



Umeko is obviously feeling awkward right now so you let it drop and you have a drink of water, and she has a drink, thus bathing you in the process.

A little later you spend a couple of hours watching a soppy movie that you wouldn't have touched with a 10ft bargepole before, but Umeko recommends. It's actually better than you expected and you actually find you're enjoying yourself, more for knowing you're watching it with Umeko than for the movie itself. You think at the end that if Umeko had tear ducts she'd be crying.

You find both of you not saying/writing anything for a while, just a kind of awkward silence. She manages to swallow a bottle of antacids (you have to open the bottle inside!) and she curls up in front of the radiator in the living room.

You try to think of something to say, something that might make her feel better, but the day has been really complicated, and find yourself drifting off to sleep again.
1111221111111111111111111121111111111122.title: Work on Umeko, try to ease her conscience and w...

choices: He's grounded! What did he do as Umeko?
He's relieved you got out. Can you switch back now?
1111221111111111111111111121111111111122.content:
(Text by Gimlet )

You drift awake before Umeko and find yourself content. No panic, no fear. Weird. For maybe half an hour you rest, listening to her heart beat around you, her moist insides pressing against your skin... Hmm, you wonder why you don't have bed sores or a rash or something? You've been in her for a long time... well, partly in the Umeko mask, maybe that lessened things, switching back and forth. Still all night in a (admittedly comfy) very wet place probably should have left you a little worse for wear. You aren't even itching.

Slowly you feel Umeko start to shift, twitch, then finally raise her head.

"Good morning." you say in a gentle, tender voice.

Your world jerks around you and shudders, ripples going over you. Whoah, you thought you were being gentle about that, but you must have caught her unawares.

"Mmm, Uh, you OK Umeko?"

A little shifting, the sound of the salt tray shaking and her mouth yawning before you, light streaming in.

'Im fine. Good morning.'

You realise that wasn't just surprise. Again it was her reacting to the thought of you inside her. Maybe Andrew was right, maybe the reason you couldn't get out was because unconsciously she doesn't want to let go of you.

"Umeko? I know these last few days have been weird, but I'd like to ask you a serious question." You pause, trying to arrange your thoughts. "You know how I feel about you. From all our time together I was already close and... OK, yeah I wanted to be with you anyway, but the last couple of days have made me want more. As I said, I love you Umeko. I know, me as a guy you can't love, but I think I've made my mind up. If you want me as a girl, as a snake, or anything else you've got me. I'll make any mask you want and... honestly there is one last thing. I know I have to get out of here to avoid getting turned into lunch, but can I come back? Please? Being inside you has been fantastic. You have no idea what it's like in here when you slither around. You're going to have to show me how you slither just so I can show you what that feels like!"

Through your speech Umeko was still. Now she shakes the tray and shakily writes.

'Will. you dont know how much means to me.' shake 'I wish there was way you could sleep in me every night'

"I'd love that!" you say chuckling, and she shivers around you again. "But your parents wouldn't want you out at night and mine would start to wonder. I might make it for a couple of nights a week tops, but doubtful more than that!"

'deal!' she writes, and her tail slides into her own mouth, coming to rest in front of you.

At first you don't know what she wants, then you realize she has no hands. This is her version of a handshake! Smiling you wriggle an arm up and take her tail lightly and shake on it. After her tail leaves you wriggle up a little.

'what you want for brekfast?' she scribbles.

"Hmm, how about we just do it simple. Bacon would be nice but I don't think even with my arms sticking out we could do breakfast properly. OJ would be fine. But would you mind just having water? I really don't feel like an orange juice bath!

For a few moments she pauses, just laying there not answering.

"Umeko? What's up?" no answer. You're just about to ask again when she breaths in and *SQUEEEEZE!* "OOOOHH GOD!" You grunt as muscles heave down on you. It's not as fun in your body as it is in Umeko's, but still it's nice, even though the massage this time is a little rough. Again you hit a ring of muscle as you head up, but it seems to be twitching. And as you hold your breath she squeezes you against it again and again and... *Pop!* and your shoulders are oozing up Umeko's throat! You're heading out! It takes quite a while and you try not to shift too much, not wanting to make her swallow again. The light gets stronger, her jaws yawn past you and you finally see her tongue brushing your face as her lips move down.

"Oh, Umeko!" you gasp feeling the pressure all around your lower body. As your hips reach her jaws she studiously avoids the rigid pole with her tongue, but soon you are easing your legs from her throat and laying on the floor, panting. As soon as you get your breath back you turn and lean over to her. "Thank you, and I meant what I said. Any time you want a snack I'm available!" then you lift her snake head up and kiss her on the lips.

For a while she goes with it, then suddenly she pulls away, her tail coming up to her face, gripping her eyebrow and nose and... oh! She hisses three times, pulls and... nothing. She blinks, tries again.

"Maybe you're saying it wrong?" you say and lean in, grip her face the appropriate way and say the phrase and pull.

"HIIISSSSESEEEAAAARGGGGHHH!" she screams, her changing body falling into your arms as she writhes. Yes you have a hard on as she becomes female once more, but you try to ignore it, holding and soothing her as she undergoes the painful transition. Finally she is panting in your arms, human once more.

"Welcome home." you say softly, and kiss her. She doesn't respond as enthusiastically as you'd like, but she doesn't stop you either. She even smiles at you with a very tired looking face.

"I'm sure Andrew has some clothes here somewhere for us." you say, helping her to her feet.

It takes a while to find something that fits her, but you do. At least she doesn't seem to be nervous being naked around you any more, and you aren't as all fired up to see her naked now. You need her, but not in that way. At least not only in that way! You still love the way she looks but it kind of feels... natural now.

"Will, thank you. For... so many things." she says as she finishes pulling on a t-shirt and slacks, about the only things that will fit her. As you finish dressing she comes over and hugs you "I wish we had my mask right about now."

You chuckle

"That would be a fitting start to the day! But I could always use the snake mask if you want."

It's obvious she's tempted, but...

"I think we'll give him a rest for a while. Besides I'm famished, haven't had a meal... a meal I could digest, in ages. lets get breakfast then try to find out how Andrew made out last night."
1111221111111111111111111121111111111122.title: Wake and have breakfast.

choices: "Oh no! By myself! Eep no, just... no, sorry!"
"That is the general idea Will... yeah. If you don't mind?"
1111221111111111111111111121111111111122.content:
(Text by Gimlet )

"Please tell me you got out of... Umeko?" Andrew says after he answers the phone, in Umeko's voice of course. His attempt at whispering that last word makes you think he isn't really alone over there.

"For the moment, yeah, I'm out." You say, and see Umeko suppress a giggle. Andrew sighs in relief "Then I need to get out of here! Oh god I hate being young again! This isn't like the movies, I like my freedom for god's sake. How do kids make it with all the rules?"

You pause, trying to avoid bursting out laughing. Andrew probably would not appreciate that.

"Look, I'll hand you to, Umeko," You say, imitating his silly whisper, "and you can sort out the details of how you extricate yourself from the living hell over there. Bring me back a pitchfork OK?" Andrew just blows you a raspberry before you hand the phone to Umeko. As she takes the phone you get the impression she wants to say this in private so before she can 'look meaningfully' at you you head out, saying over your shoulder... "So, I'll get you that OJ for you Umeko, now I'm not in the way down there."

By the time you head back in with her drink, and one for yourself, the phone is hung up.

"He'll be over in about an hour." Umeko says, though you raise an eyebrow at this. The trip would only take fifteen minutes. "He's got to persuade my Dad to let him out for the day. That'll take a little while."

"Fair enough. Enough time for breakfast anyways." and the two of your head to the kitchen to make some bacon and toast... quite a lot of it for Umeko. Apparently she was REALLY hungry!



"Here, take it, keep it, burn it!" Andrew says as he comes in, as Freddy, shoving the Umeko mask into your hands. "I don't care, just keep it away from me! Oh god, teenage girls! Bah!" and he storms off upstairs.

"Um, welcome back?" Umeko manages weakly by your side to his retreating back.

"It's OK, I'm sure he'll calm down later." You say, almost going to put an arm around her waist, then pause and think better of it. "Here, this is yours I believe?" you say, handing her the mask.

Umeko stares down at it, peering into the holographic image of herself in the mask, glinting in the light.

"No... it's yours Will." she says quietly "But can I get one of you in return?"

This floors you for a moment, then a grin slowly spreads across your face.

"One of... me? Is this going where my, admittedly smutty, mind thinks it's going?" you say, and see her blush.

She just nods, still blushing. You gently lift her face to look her in the eye.

"Is this for... well, you by yourself or... with me and..." and you wave the Umeko mask idly.
1111221111111111111111111121111111111122.title: He's relieved you got out. Can you switch b...

choices: Go fix the snake mask now. Umeko wants to try it out!
Start gathering more materials for another mask or two.
1111221111111111111111111121111111111122.content:
(Text by Gimlet )

Umeko peers deep into your eyes and her blush deepens.

"Yeah, that is the general idea Will... if you don't mind?"

You almost just agree instantly, just out of principle, but pause to consider. You know it felt weird with Umeko in the Robert mask before. How much more weird is it going to be as her, with her as YOU!... She bites her lip as she sees you seriously considering it.

"I think I can handle it Umeko, if you're gentle with me." and that at least gets a giggle out of her. "But first we need to get to the Clubhouse and 'fix' the Python mask."

Umeko's eyes go wide at that and you suspect she'd actually forgotten about it! She nods enthusiastically and is about to just head out. You grab her arm and shake your head.

"First lets get our stuff back. Andrew still has my bag with our old clothes and stuff... and the unfixed mask. Plus I want to thank him for the diabolical living hell he's just walked through. I mean, living as a kid again! Shock horror!" and you both chuckle.

The two of you head upstairs and knock on his bedroom door and hear him tell you to come in. Inside he is out of his Freddy mask and in his wheelchair again, in front of a computer.

"I just wanted our bag of clothes back..." and Andrew sighed, grabbed it from the back of his wheelchair. It was up here the whole time? *sigh* He tossed the bag to you and you nodded back to him. "Thanks. Oh and also to say thank you Andrew, we owe you big time for all this! Saving us from the Zoo and what you just went through" You say he stares at you with a strong frown and nods slowly.

"Oh boy do you ever owe me!" He says, and melodramatically shivers. "If I ever go near that house again I'll probably break out in hives!"

You suppress a grin and try to remain calm.

"How does a blank mask sound? As recompense?" you say calmly, waving good bye to a thousand bucks. Hey, Umeko is worth it! "I'll give you a pot of fixer and you can make it of whoever you want, a mix I'd suggest, like before. You go as someone else and... well, identity theft has nothing on this!"

Andrew's face starts to calm, the irritation draining out of him, but leaving him looking at you oddly.

"Will, this last week or so, have you been on drugs or something?" You peer at him and he waves his hand. "I'm serious! You aren't exactly known for... well, how can I put this. Generosity? Forethought? Thinking with something other than your..." Andrew pauses and his gaze slips to Umeko who bites her lip. "...hormones?" he finishes lamely.

"I... well, you know. This is big stuff Andy." you say, beginning to realize you have begun to think bigger lately. When did that start? With the book? Why? "I... I just want to do something right, I guess. Hey, what're you worried about, you'd be down another grand otherwise!"

"I ain't complaining Will. Thanks, just if you see William Prescott say hi to him for me."

You chuckle and mutter something about making him a blank mask this week and out of his room. You dash into the bathroom while Umeko goes into the spare room and dress in your own clothes again, leaving the 'loaners' from Andrew here. Going back out on the landing you meet up with Umeko and head downstairs. Half way down the stairs you stop and stare at Umeko.

"Have I changed these last few weeks Umeko?" you say carefully.

She turns and looks at you seriously and pauses for quite a few seconds.

"Honestly... yeah." She says, some nerves showing in her face "You've kind of grown up."

"Oh come on! I've been grown up! I ain't a kid!" you say, but manage to remain somewhat calm.

"That's what I mean. Before you'd have really blown your top over anything I said like that. Now, you seem more able to deal with stuff. Not as over emotional as you used to be." you raise an eyebrow and she smiles.

"I thought that was the line guys said to girls about emotional outbursts or something?"

"Well, get me a Will mask and we'll see if it holds true there huh?" she says and heads downstairs, then calls over her shoulder "Before, during and after!"

Oh boy!

1111221111111111111111111121111111111122.content:
(Text by Gimlet )

Another stroll across town (You gotta get a car someday soon!) and you head down into the old school basement again. Everything looks good, seems Blackwell didn't follow you here before or something. You pat the book, but right now really don't want to go into something new. There are other things on your mind right now, and something specific on Umeko's! A gleam is in her eye as she gets the Python mask out and uses the pot of fixative on it carefully, though you do notice a slightly shaky hand with the brush. With a lick of her lips she puts the pot down and lifts the finished mask up before her, inside facing herself, and gazes at it.

"OK... a little privacy Will?" she says.

You turn away then hear the rustle of clothes, then the squeak of a chair that she moves out of the way so she can lie down. A brief sigh, then a stifled scream when she puts the mask on. Wincing you keep away while she changes, the sound of thrashing limbs, then thrashing tail, coming from behind you, grating teeth sounds blending into hisses before finally subsiding. About twenty seconds later there is a tap on your shoulder and you turn around.

The new Umeko is... different. The anaconda was bulky, thick around and with a fairly large head for a snake, about 8 or 9 inches long. It was also only maybe twenty foot long. The Python is thinner, a fair bit longer (25, 30 feet?) but the head is thinner and a little shorter, maybe 6 or 7 inches long. You're surprised the mask worked on it actually.

"Hey Umeko." You say, smiling at 'her' "So, the new suit fit well?"

She can't grin of course, but makes a fair imitation of hissing a laugh. You wonder if Pythons can hear better? She has her head lifted up level with yours as her body slowly curls around you... and then you see a mask in her coils, which then hoist it up from the floor in front of you. Ah, she wants you to change too huh? You take the mask and look into Umeko's eyes. For some reason this snake seems... more sensuous than the other. It's a lighter color, a mottled mid and light green mix all over rather than with dark green, light and yellow stripes, but it's also the shape, longer thinner, and the head is also somehow more feminine. Perhaps that is more to do with the species than the gender, but what ever it is it works. A coil loops lightly up, across your back and over your shoulders lightly, rippling slightly across them. She taps the mask before you with her snout.

"He, OK Umeko, I get the message. Hurry up and change huh?" She hisses as you bring the mask up before you.

You gaze into the mask and see the holographic face of Umeko. Oh, somehow you'd figured she'd want the snake? Well, this is more your thing certainly. Sitting down (though Umeko doesn't uncoil from you, just flows with you) you just unbutton your shirt, undo your trousers (and slip your underwear down a little inside to be sure) and take a deep breath before putting the mask on.

The usual discomfort, and something more, flows over you as you change. A few seconds later, while you are still recovering, you feel Umeko draw the last of your clothes off and realize that is why it felt odd. Umeko had lifted you off the ground in her coils and stripped you while you were changing!

"Whoah! Um, thanks." you say in her voice. Her slender head bobs into view over you and nods back, then settles comfortably between your breasts as her coils wrap around your limbs, flexing and rippling over you. There is even a tight coil behind your head, supporting you like a pillow. It's actually rather comforting. She seems insistent on wrapping around you, but lets you pull your right arm free and you reach up and stroke the side of her head, just poking out of your cleavage. That in itself is an odd thought, but you're starting to get used to the idea of having cleavage. She wriggles contentedly in there as you stroke her, you moaning in sinc with her motions. That feels good!

For what seems like hours you literally float on a sea of Umeko Python, her body massaging you all over, she even slips her long tongue into your mouth at one point, though snake kisses aren't all they could be from your point of view. Not mobile enough lips. Stroking her head, now back in it's cleavage abode again, you smile down at her.

"Mmmmmm," you moan as a coil wriggles around your rump slowly. Her motions are getting a little more insistent and you think she is getting a little horny from the rubbing of her head in there and the way a length of her tail is pressed between your legs. You just like the feel of her all around you and let her go as she pleases for now, and the massage continues, a couple of coils lifting up from the side and looping over your breasts gently and starting to ripple, as if she were slithering, kneading the supple flesh tightly. "Oh now THAT is good!" you moan, then moan deeper as the length between your legs presses harder and ripples too.

111122111111111111111111112111112.content:
(Text by Nirro )

Umeko, changes her position, instead of laying peacefully on you, she sits up, looking at you, with a strong and meaningful flare in her eyes. You sit up and meet her eyes with your's.

"I want a mask of you."

Is all that she asks for, it's not a request you can refuse, as you take a look at your body, you smile at her, she laughs, and you know immediately that it's what she wants. The laugh after she asks for something is her way of blowing it off, trying to sweep it under the rug, you've know her for a long time, and you are her, you hold her hand gently, she stops laughing, almost shocked by your sudden change.

"Of course you can, I mean you let me make one of you, so it's only fair!" You tell her, shattering the odd wall of laughter she placed between you. She smiles an incandescent smile, latching on to you. Kissing you and squealing as she does this.

"THANK YOU, THANK YOU, THANK YOU, THANK YOU, THANK YOU SOOOOOO MUCH WILL!!!!!!" Umeko screams in joy! Literally trying to kiss her face off your body. "I mean I felt so weird not being able to like girls, and then you became one! Or well, you became me and then I got to expirence it finally! Now if I become you we can finally understand each other! I mean am not going to ask for sex as us! But you know maybe we can find a girl we can get a mask of to at least see what it's like!!" She speaks, slowly getting more and more excited. As the planning and utter delight reach a critical mass, your phone goes off, cathing both of your attentions, you pick it up, quickly anwering it.

"Hello?" You say, suddenly remembering who is speaking your words.

"Umeko?" Your mother questions, sounding slightly relieved. "I was worried about Will. Thank god he's with you, can you hand the phone to him?" She asks. Umeko is giggling at your predicament, the akward conversation of playing your cousin with your own mother now under way.

"Sorry auntie, he is helping my friend moving some stuff, I asked if he could help us without sending you a message!" You quickly say, Umeko suddenly sending daggers at you with her eyes. "They went to get some pizza while I cleaned up some of the furniture. They should be back any mintue." You finish quickly.

"Oh, tell him his supper will be waiting for him when he gets back if he is still hungry." She says joyfully, relieved that you're safe, strange as it seems to hear from another person's perception. Your mother talks to you for quite a bit about love and other things, but soon you begin to understand, maybe she treats Umeko like a distant daughter. Not as strict your relatives treat her under their household rules, but like she isn't a stranger to being an outcast at all. The conversation hits a powerful note when she says the final set of words. "Umeko, I know you're different than what your parents want, but if you need anything am here for you."she speaks with a strong sense of motherly love, a little shocking to hear from her, then it hits you.

"My mom knows Umeko isn't in to guys!!!"

This revaluation shocks you, to the point where Umeko takes the phone and sighs off.

"What the hell Will!!!" She says looking concerned for a moment, her face trying to not show the concern for your sudden change of attitude.

"Ume, my mom knows your a lesbian....." You tell her, not trying to be blunt, by using a nick name she hated. She even pauses, sort of taken back by this, she looks at you, blankly staring at her own face, until the words she puts out are a simple

"Oh.... Shit!"

*****************************************************

The return home is greeted by your father not being to happy about you being home after nine, yet happy you helped family. Your mother sits with you at the dinner table as you eat asking about Umeko and her friend.

"Is she tall?" She asks, being quieter than normal, you know how to deal with it, but you need to ask her straight to her face. It's not easy, but it has to be done, or else it would be weird to dance around the subject, so you choose to says it bluntly.

"Umeko isn't into her friend, she just hangs out. Plus she isn't into girls like Umeko." You say bluntly, just like you were told to do by Umeko. Your mother smiles, she stands up to approach you, embracing you with a very tender hug.

"Am glad you're able to be her family and friend William. I know your dad's part of the family is a bit more religious than most, especially his brother. He was the most out spoken about our relationship when we started getting serious." She tells you as she lets you go and gets a glass of water.

"Wait, what do you mean?" You ask, the curiosity of your mother now being on the rise.

"I never told anyone this, but my hometown is filled with people dedicated to a very isolated belief. It praises illusion's, truth, lies, understanding one another. My mother, your grandmother was very much into it, it's part of the reason you don't see them too often." She relentlessly tells you, looking down at the cup of water. "I was the priestess' daughter, so it was expected of me to go into the faith, but I wanted to see the world, then I met your father." She looks happier, beaming fondly over the memories going through her mind, the look on her face being on of pure satisfaction. "I was made to do a lot of things against people who were different. Not racism, but just for being different, I learned later about how amazing people can be, especially when your father snuck me out of the town. All the way to Texas, it's where we made it official. Point is I didn't look back once I left that prison. It's okay for people to be different, and am glad you don't hate Umeko for liking what she likes." She says with a brighter smile, looking at with proud eyes. "Even if you had the biggest crush on her, more than Lisa." She teases as she leaves, leaving you with a strong blush on your face as you finish the meal.

*****************************************************

You're up till one in the morning preparing a mask, not the full treatment, just the basics. You can't risk starting a house fire when your this sleepy. Still as you look on, the way your mother spoke about the place she grew up in, you can't recall it's name, but you know of it. It's a very bothersome feeling, but you push it aside and finish the mask before it gets it's final treatment for your face. The thought of you being so close to Umeko like this, maybe there is something about these masks that you don't fully understand? Maybe it's worth going deeper into the book? You're unsure of it, there is an eerie feeling encroaching from it, like the book is somehow, deceiving you. You hold it in your hand trying to turn the pages past the other pages, some are blank, others are gone, some are scribbled with barely readable words. The leather almost feels like it's breathing, like a child in your hands, or a shark. You hold above your head, curiosity gets the better of you and you begin to type in some of the words scribbled on it, and look at the strange markings. The search is long into the night, it's sunrise when are able to stop, but you have discovered a lot more about the book, more than you wanted. The ruins on the leather praise gods of deception, trickery, cunning, silver tongued devils, assassin's, druids, sorcerers, and, all manner of spirits. The book from what you can tell originated in Europe, somewhere before the dark ages, the runes are from the oldest of alphabets used by shamans and druids. You print everything you can find about it. The list of god associated with anything of it to who my have had it, at some point, you begin to think Alistair Crowley may have used it, or have knowledge of it. It's past seven when your obsession ends, the mania leading you to run your hands over the computer screen and papers of many names, but only a few stand out. It's too perfect not to match it the runes have it traced back into Gaulish, the ancient druidic letters lead you to Taliesin and Gwyyddion, Shapeshifting gods. Yet in runes, Loki is seen, even more confusing, the words are more than insane ramblings, they are incantations, of some sort, the words don't flow right. They created a blend of ancient celtic and elder runes, some are even from Greek and Angelo Saxon times. You don't even have time to process it, but as you barely chant, the starnge words, the book vanishes into thin air! You jump back falling onto your bed, knocking the chair spilling papers off your desk, a cup breaking in the process.

"WILL? Is everything all right??" Your father calls, a little confused by how early you're up.

"Yeah am fine, just rolled out of bed!!! Literally....." You finish as he opens the door, looking confused by the mess.

"Okay.... Get dressed, go have breakfast, normally you aren't even up this early. I'll take your brother too school, your mother is going to see my sister today." He sighs in disbelief. " I swear she makes more money by keeping that stupid fortune teller gig, and it goes directly to her head." He mutter as he walks out. When he's gone, you examine you hand, something feels off, like there is more weight to it now, or it's being held by something...... "CLEAN YOUR ROOM BEFORE YOU GO ANYWHERE TODAY!!!" your father shouts from the staircase. You say nothing, but recall the letters in your head, trying to word them out, in a flash, dull and a strange ghost flame, the book appears. You place it down, trying the words spell again, the book disappears, or hides itself.... Now you know a little actual spells........ You get into the morning routine of a shower and have breakfast, you aren't speaking much, but processing the information, the things you can barely translate about the book, maybe it's more than just masks, maybe your on to something. As you leave the house, searing sensation attacks your right hand! You check your hand, to see a small mark, it's a mask tattoo almost branded perfectly to your palm.
111122111111111111111111112111112.title: I'd like a mask of you, Will.
