She pats the table, and puts on the biggest pair of puppy dog eyes you've ever seen.

So you flop down opposite her.

Her smile turns knowing. "I really shouldn't yell at you. I'm sorry. You're doing an amazing job, and I haven't told you how much I appreciate it."

Bullshit.

"I'm sorry I got mad back there. I was thinking about Andrea, but I wasn't thinking about you, and about how rough you're having it. Believe me -- " She can't keep the smile from turning into a smirk. "I know how rough it is."

"Gee, thanks. I love it when a pretty girl blows perfume up my ass. It gives me a hard-on."

You'd get up again, but her next statement shuts you up: "It is hard for you to get laid, isn't it? Would you like to do something about that?"

A large stone materializes in your throat. "You're offering to help me get laid?" you thickly demand. She nods. "With who?"

Her eyes are bright. "Who do you want?"
1112111311222221222222122121211212112211.title: A First Day at New Business

1112111311222221222222122121211212112211.content:

Tuesday morning you wake to a text from Andrea. She's demanding an early meeting in the parking lot of the same CVS where you had your first meeting with her. You dress and race out the door without even showering.

That catches your attention. "Yeah?"

"Shut up. Some of your friends busted in on them. Some of my friends, actually." A slightly stricken look crosses her face. "Call and Spencer and Rennerhoff. Oh, and Kirkham."

Your "Chen" personality only listens, but you flinch. The idea of what that crew of degenerates could get up to at the Warehouse doesn't bear thinking about. "What happened?"

"Nothing. Well, nothing like what could have happened." Now she definitely looks guilty. "There were too many people around, more than they were counting on being there, so they just did a lot of mouthing off."

"Well, for a start, it's going to put a kink in the scene back at my house if those guys are going to start spending more time with me."

"No, you fuck. Jelena and them. They don't like the direction I'm taking things."

"So tell them to fuck off. If they're not cool enough for you -- "

"I thought I was supposed to make it sexy so you could sell a lot more product to my friends. Andrea's friends, I mean."

You notice the shift in pronouns. Is that what the "guilty" look is for? Thomason is starting to identify more with Andrea, the way you worry you've been identifying with Chen?

Her eyes narrow when you only answer with a "Huh?" "I said, don't you want me making it a sexy scene at -- ?"

"Oh, sure. Exactly."

"Because I can't if they're going to be sitting around my house and frowning like a lot of church ladies."

"You're not getting it, Chen! They're a big part of what makes it sexy at my place. You do think Jelena is sexy, don't you?"

That guilty look -- which had disappeared briefly -- comes back. "You know, Call thinks Jelena is sexy too. Have you ever heard him talk about her?"

"It sounds familiar, but -- "

The penny drops. Actually, the clang inside your brain is so loud it's more like a manhole cover dropping. "You want me to put Joshua Call into Jelena Petrovich's body?"

"It means Jelena and Sienna and Fatima stop giving me shit, and they start helping out at my place. Or at the Warehouse, if we start -- "

"We do the same thing to them. With Spencer and Rennerhoff."

"You don't like the idea." She sounds hurt.

In fact, she sounds hurt in just the way that a girl would sound hurt if you refused to do her a really big favor after you'd promised her you'd do it. The guilt is gone from Andrea's face, and now she just looks reproachful. You have to fight the instinct -- where does it come from, that instinct? -- to give in and promise her to do everything she asks.

"Okay, I don't not like it, let's put it like that," you say, and shove three fingertips under your knitted cap to wipe at the tickling sweat that's broken out on your brow. "It's a hard thing to do, you know, and maybe Call likes Jelena, but that doesn't mean he wants to be her. And the other two -- "

"I think any guy -- "

"And who says they'd be interested in helping you out with the party scene?"

"I think I can talk them into that," says Andrea, and she shifts a little in the car seat.

Your cock, already turgid, instantly starts to throb at high alert. Yeah, you just bet she can talk them into anything. The sudden fantasy is as vivid as a movie: Andrea and Jelena, Andrea and Sienna, Andrea and Fatima -- in pairs or in a quartet -- on that futon, or in a bed, gripping and gnawing at each other and pulling their clothes off. "I'll have to think about it," you mumble, and that means you'll have to think about the fantasy and about Thomason's suggestion, both. "Anything else?"

Kirkham is leaning next to your locker when you check in there before first period. "You fucking miss me last night?" you ask.

"The fuck does that mean, asshole?"

"Means what it sounds like. I heard you tried to have some fun without me."

His silence is sulfurous, and you look over to find him staring at you. His eyes are hidden behind those shades, and the muscles of his face show nothing, but the angle of his toothpick somehow suggests unhappiness.

"Not till Friday. Saturday at the latest."

As you ponder things, you see one additional upside to Thomason's request: You could go along with his idea in exchange for getting him to try out for the cheerleader squad. And after a full day of thinking about it while ignoring most of your classes, you reach a decision.

"Yo, que pasa?" Drew Jeffries calls out as you swagger into the bar area of the Warehouse. "Or whatever it is you and your guys say."

"Me and my guys?" The desire to paint the walls with Jeffries' face rises up your spine like an express elevator, and is barely restrained by the desire to prudently keep on his good side. "Which guys would those be?"

"I dunno, man." He shrugs. "Who's in that army you think's following you in here, and how many are in it?"

You show him a very stiff middle finger, long enough for it to register with him, then flick your index and ring fingers into a regiment around it. "Josh Call and two other guys. They get here yet?"

Jeffries' face has turned stony. "Who's asking?"

"Your mother. She's got her bed all warmed up for us."

He drops the cell phone he's been nursing, and it clatters on the bar. "You wanna fuck with me instead, chinaboy, 'cos -- "

She's in short-shorts and canvas shoes, so there's nothing but long, bare leg from her ankles up to just below her ass. Andrea leans across the bar at an angle that gives you and Jeffries both a good view of her swelling breasts. "You don't want to fuck with Gary," she tells Drew in a very quiet voice. "Everyone at Westside is scared of him."

Jeffries' eyes flick over to yours. They betray a deep skepticism overlaying an even deeper loathing. "Yeah?"

"Yeah." Andrea's breathy voice is almost a whisper. "And you know the ones who are most scared of him? The ones he taught to be scared of him."

She puts her arm in yours, and now another feeling goes up your spine: one of intense desire -- a haggard, starved-mongrel kind of desire. Your legs aren't the only things that are stiff as she pulls you over to a half-open door leading onto a bleak staircase. "I've got things set up for us upstairs," she says. You catch her casting a backward look at Jeffries just before you disappear up those stairs, but what expression she showed him, and what expression he showed in response, you don't catch.

"You know, you didn't have to save me or anything back there," you growl. Lust has been (mostly) conquered by your knowledge of who exactly is taking you upstairs, and it has been completely smothered by humiliation. "That fucker -- "

"That fucker's tougher than he looks, same as you," she says in that same quiet voice. "He has to be, if he's gonna hang out at a place like this on weeknights. His name's Drew Jeffries, by the way. I don't think you know him."

"No." Strictly speaking, that's not true. You knew him quite well when you visited the Warehouse last Saturday in the guise of Adam Karter, and as soon as you saw him a few moments ago you remembered him and even his name.

"Well, if you ever decide to start hanging out here more, you better get to know him and to like him. He's the one that sells the alcohol and weed."

"Only him?"

"He has a few guys helping out." You've reached the top of the stairs now. A long, dark, musty hallway plunges along under the rafters of the Warehouse. The outer wall is of brick and cinderblock, punctuated at regular intervals by broken windows. The other wall is of rude plywood, same as the floor, which sags and groans under your feet. But Andrea is still talking. "Mostly those other guys help out by promising to fuck over anyone who makes life hard for Drew," she says. She stops halfway down the hall. "Jesus, Chen, do you have to be a fucking prick everywhere you go?"

"Keeps life simple if I don't have to keep track who I gotta be nice too and who I don't. Anyway, that gimped-up fag who let me in at the front door didn't give me any trouble."

"How did you know he's -- ?" She breaks off and sighs. "Beats me why Lane liked you enough to let you in."

"I'da liked to have seen that bondage queen keep me out."

Andrea sighs again. "I'm just saying -- "

"You don't have to say anything, Thomason. 'Cept I kinda like it when you do. You sound sexy." You take a step back, and drink her from the soles of her feet up to her long, slim neck. It's too dark upstairs to make out details, but the shape is mighty, mighty fine. "You look it, too."

It's an easy promise to make. "Sure."

And clearly Call senses it's an easy promise for you to make, for he's got a follow up. "I'll tell you who next week. And if you don't set it up -- if you don't find a way to set it up foolproof for me and my friends -- I'll blow the whistle on you. I'm sure the fucking government would love to see what you've got going on here, get their hands on it."
1112111311222221222222122121211212112211.title: Negotiations with a Devil

1112111311222221222222122121211212112211.content:
It's still an easy promise to make. Who cares what Joshua Call wants or demands? Even if he got the government involved, all you'd have to do is rip the Chelsea mask off Chen and leave him to take the blame while you disappeared behind some other mask. Like maybe a celebrity the way Rennerhoff was talking about. Or maybe -- and wouldn't this be funny -- as one of the agents sent to catch you.

You don't want it to come to that, though. You tell Call you're happy to work with him, and seal the deal by crushing his hand in yours while he tries to crush yours in his. "So how are we going to do this?" he asks.

"I'll get 'Andrea' to call her friends. One at a time, get 'em out here so we can replace 'em without making a scene. While we're waiting for them to show up, I'll need to make a mold of each of you for your robots."

"A mold?" asks Call.

"Yeah, don't worry, you won't feel a thing. Will they, Rennerhoff?" The girl on the mattress doesn't answer. "Yo, Rennerhoff, you listening? You in there?"

"What?" Sienna jumps a little.

"I asked, are you there? What'sa matter? Starting to get some memories? Getting distracted?"

"I don't know?" Her brow crinkles. "I guess I was thinking about something else."

"See, she doesn't remember a damn thing," you tell Call. "You think she'd remember if it hurt."

"Yeah, but how will these robots work?" Call looks very suspicious.

"What do you care? They'll work just like that Thomason 'bot you were hanging around with last week. He seemed normal, right?"

"Normal for Thomason, I guess."

"Well, your 'bots will seem normal for you. They'll do the same shit stuff you'd'a done in their place. Sit still, guys, trust your lingxiong."

But they've still got issues. Call says, "Who's gonna be which girl?" to which Spencer sullenly says, "I wanna be Sienna."

But you're already through the doorway. "Figure it out amongst yourselves, guys," you call over your shoulder. "I don't care who's who, only it's gonna be Jelena, Sienna and Fatima. Flip for it, rock-paper-scissors it, I don't give a fuck."

In the other room, you're pleased to find that Andrea Varnsworth has returned and is awake; you're slightly less pleased to see that she'd dressed again. "Okay, we're ready for your friends," you tell her. "Start with -- Oh, start with Jelena. Those assholes are going to fight over the other two, I think."

Andrea takes out her phone. "What do I tell her?"

"She's your friend, tell whatever you think will get her out here. But get her alone." You snatch up your knapsack. "Is she gonna think it's funny when you lead her up to one of these rooms?"

"I don't know what she'll think, but I'll get her to do it."

"Make it this room. I'll be waiting inside. Let her come in first, then grab her from behind while I get the mask onto her."

"Okay. Hey, are those guys going to do like we want them to?"

You stop in a doorway again. "You mean Call and them? It's a little late to be wondering that, isn't it?"

* * * * *

Shit and questions. Everyone is full of shit and questions. Call still wants to know how the robots work, and what's going to happen to the real girls. You tell him it's not his business to know, that he shouldn't worry, and that the girls will be okay. "They won't remember a thing when we let them loose into the real world again," you tell him. Your lack of responsiveness makes Call angry, you can tell, but he doesn't change his mind about going along with your scheme, and submits to having a blank mask put on his face -- though only after watching it happen to Spencer first.

As for body assignments, Rennerhoff doesn't make trouble and lets Spencer claim Sienna, so while Spencer is being copied you pry Sienna's mask off Rennerhoff's face. Call would want to talk some more, but you put him off by returning to the bar to get some beers. Jeffries hands them over with a glower. "All you guys having fun back there?"

"Regular carnival. We got a campfire going. Got any marshmallows to go with the hops?"

Twenty minutes later, all three of your new conspirators have their faces and brains copied into three of the blank masks you'd brought. "Now I gotta take a piss," you announce once they are collected.

"Is that part of the magic?" Spencer asks. He's become more vocal as the night's progressed.

"A kind of magic," you reply. "The magic of bodily secretions and the removal of toxins. I gotta full bladder," you add when his heavy blinking tell you he needs a translation.

"You always take your shit with you to the john?" asks Rennerhoff as you sweep your bag onto your shoulder.

"Take a shit, leave a shit. It's a form of inventory control. Christ, you need to stop skipping Peters's classes."

The head is downstairs, behind the booths, and it's a dark, foul place with a trough-like common urinal and busted chamber pots that might as well open directly into the sewer pipes. The concrete floor and walls are besmirched with browns and reds that you hope are mineral deposits and not bodily effluvia. Along one wall are some makeshift stalls of plywood for the use of the ladies -- though only a girl who is high or drunk would try to use one -- and you hide in one of them. For you came here not to take a shit or a piss, but to seal up the masks.

Probably you could do it in front of the three idiots, but you prefer to keep as many of your secrets as you can, as Call is being super nosy. You make a fast job of it, for you'd mixed up the paste earlier and brought it out in a small plastic tub.

But it's not only sealant. It's a rockbot paste.

You've learned from your trouble with Andrea, and you're pretty sure that Jelena and Sienna, at the very least, wouldn't go along with being trapped inside the bodies of Josh Call and Jeff Spencer. So you mixed in a bit of your hair -- the same kind of hair you put in the Chenbot and the Thomasonbot -- into this paste, so that the Callbot, the Spencerbot, and the Rennerhoffbot will be perfect duplicates and under your control.

That could prove useful.

But even as you apply the paste, you have the nagging worry that you're doing something foolish. If it is so easy to make a 'bot that replaces a person, why hasn't someone used the magic book and the magic masks to conquer the world with an army of duplicates? There must be a catch somewhere.

* * * * *

You get finished just in time, too. A few minutes later, as you're on your way back up the stairs, Thomason signals that Jelena has just arrived. You stick your head into the room where the three psychos are waiting, and one glance tells you they're starting to get a little stir crazy from waiting. "Keep still and keep quiet," you growl at them. "First girl will be up in ninety seconds. If she sees or hears you, she'll run off and then I don't know what we'll do." In reply, you get a grimace from Call, a sullen glare from Spencer, and a blank, slack-jawed stare from Rennerhoff.

In the next room, you wait in the blind corner near the door with a blank mask cradled in your hand. The target seems very long in coming, and you flinch angrily when you hear the clink and clatter of bottles next door, and a muffled bark, probably from Call hitting someone. Silence follows on that.

Then the floor in the hallway creaks, and you hear soft voices. You can't make out the words, but it's two girls talking. You twist off the light bulb, plunging the room in darkness.

The door creaks open, showing a paler darkness through the doorway. "Oh Christ, it stinks up here," says someone. "I'm going back -- " Her voice breaks off with a muffled gasp.

You jump forward. There's a tangle of bodies in the doorway, and with one hand you grope at them. "The fuck?" a girl shouts, and the bodies twist about. You feel arms crossed against a chest, then a neck, and finally a face that twists away from your open palm.

You jam the mask in that direction. There's a soft kind of a crunching noise, and a shuddering blow races up your arm to your shoulder. The mask melts under your fingers, and then your fingertips are pinching and grasping at flesh: nose, eyes, cheeks. They sink away and Andrea says, "Chen?" in a low, desperate voice. Something heavy hits the floor.

You hop over to feel for the light bulb, and twist it back on. Two bodies are in the hallway. One of them is wearing dark jeans and a dark blouse. The other, underneath the first -- Well, you recognize the bare legs of Andrea Varnsworth.

You seize the unconscious Jelena Petrovich by the ankles and drag her into the room. She is pale -- but she always is -- and her long brunette hair is disordered. You roll her over as best you can -- she's like six sacks of wet cement bundled up inside a larger sack -- onto the mattress. The whole thing has quite taken your breath away, and you're panting heavily.

But the first one is down. You look up at Andrea with a grin. "Ready to call a second friend?"
1112111311222221222222122121211212112211.title: Here Come the Coeds, Part 1

choices: Just wait for Sienna to come into the room
1112111311222221222222122121211212112211.content:
"I'll leave it to you to get her naked," you tell Andrea as she squats next to the unconscious Jelena Petrovich. "I got a feeling you'll be getting lots of practice at it."

She looks up sharply. "What's that supposed to mean?"

"Nothing. I don't know." You go out into the hall and pull the door closed behind you.

But you don't move. Where could you go?

There's no place you'd feel comfortable landing. Inside that room as Andrea pulls Jelena's clothes off? That would feel like a violation -- a funny scruple to have, since you're about to do a lot worse to Jelena. Or maybe that's what you meant when you cracked wise about Andrea getting naked with Jelena. It's the obvious, leering comment, and you've thought often enough that that's the kind of thing that'll be happening with Andrea and her friends after you get these three new victims swapped.

But you've also told Thomason and the others that this business is theirs, that you're just setting up the swap for them, and that the only thing you expect is that they'll use their new identities to help you move more product through Westside. Is that why it feels like a violation of privacy to watch Andrea peel Jelena's pants and top from her? Because you'd be intruding on something that you've told everyone doesn't concern you?

Nor do you want to go in to see Call and his friends again. They'll just badger you with a lot of questions. Jeffries is manning things downstairs in the bar, and you don't want to wander far off into the dance room or outside for a smoke.

So you end up leaning against the end of the corridor and gazing out the windows. You don't see much, for the hazy glare of the mercury vapor lamps obliterates the night shadows without revealing anything behind them.

A minute or so passes, and the other door opens a crack. Call looks out. He blinks, and chucks an inquiring chin at you. "Waiting for Jelena's mold to set," you tell him. "I can bring you her panties in a minute, if you wanna sniff them." He shuts the door.

A few more minutes, and Andrea opens her door long enough to hand you Jelena's things. They're neatly folded too. You take them with a sigh, and glance around helplessly after Andrea shuts the door again. Christ, it's like being at a department store and your girlfriend gives you a bunch of clothes while she rushes off to try on some others.

Maybe that's your problem: You still haven't got laid, and here you are surrounded by acres of succulent female flesh belonging to people who are beholden to you. Except that you know what the real faces look like under the fake ones, and that kills your interest.

No, it doesn't kill your interest. That's the problem. It only hobbles it. Looking at Andrea Varnsworth kills your potency without killing your intense desire. The fucking masks are like a chastity belt for your cock.

You feel your pockets for a cigarette, then think better of it. This place could probably burn down real easily if you let a cigarette get loose.

Oh hell, there's probably been millions smoked in this place since the city's teenagers took it over. You light the cigarette and take a deep, relaxing breath. It tastes wonderful, and a slight numbness steals over you. Maybe that's all you've been missing tonight, and that's what's gotten your nerves jangly. You've been missing the nicotine.

You bang on the door hiding the three dipshits. "Here," you tell Call around the cigarette and shove Jelena's clothes in at him. "Yo," you call over his shoulder at Rennerhoff and Spencer. "How about you two faggots go hang out in the next room over, give your buddy here some privacy for when he changes." To Call, even though the others don't move. "It'll be ready for you in a minute or two. You might as well get out of your things. Your robot'll need 'em anyway." You suck in some smoke and blow a thin stream into his face. "Wouldn't recommend you getting into these, though, not until you're tricked out like a certain guitar-straddling dyke." To the other two: "I thought I told you two dumbasses to move."

So they move. You put them in the next room over, so that your company is spread into a trio of cubbyholes. Good thing it's a weeknight, and the place is dead.

You've just got them shut away when Andrea's door opens and she peers out. She gestures excitedly when she spots you, and pulls you inside. It's the mask that's got her antsy. It's reappeared on Jelena's face.

"I'll take it from here, sweetheart," you tell her. "Go downstairs and call Sienna."

"Bring her up here?"

"Yeah, we'll be out of your way by the time you're back." She nods, and leaves you alone.

You take a moment to look Jelena over. She's well shaped, though her boobs aren't the biggest, and she's covered in a layer of baby fat all over. Again, you feel a twinge of loneliness and longing, but the cigarette has taken the edge off. You paint the inside of her mask while studying her out of the corner of your eye, then take it over to Call.

"Christ, you don't have to give me an eyeful," you tell him when you open the door and he turns his hideous, full-frontal form on you.

"You told me to take my -- "

"Your butt crack'd be enough for me. Here's Jelena's mask. Just sit down and put it to your face, like you did with that other one. I'll get your things in to the robot."

Back in the other room, you don't waste any time, and drop Call's sealed-up mask onto Jelena's face. Joshua Call -- naked, hairy, ropey-muscled Joshua Call -- materializes and opens his eyes. He sees you, blinks once, and rears back.

"Quiet," you tell the 'bot in a low voice. "You have to do what I say, right?" The 'bot swallows heavily, and nods. Pain and confusion fills its eyes. "Get dressed, go downstairs, and wait in my Jeep. You know what my Jeep looks like, right?" Again, it nods. "Good. Then wait there for me. Don't touch anything and don't call anyone. I'll be sending some friends out to wait with you, and you can talk to them, but to no one else. Wait there until I come find you."

Yes, you're nervous about sending this thing out. It's bad enough having to manage the Chenbot when you've got it operational. But the Callbot, maybe because it's disoriented by the changes, seems more docile. It keeps its mouth shut and reaches for his clothes.

You briefly check on the real Call --

Only now it's Jelena Petrovich in there, resting limply on the mattress. Somehow she looks a lot more vulnerable than she had in the other room, even though now she's got Joshua Call under her face.

-- then look in on Spencer and Rennerhoff. Both are looking nervous, which is a real trick for Spencer to manage, since his face most naturally rests in a dead-eyed glower. "Only about another hour before we're done with you two," you promise them. "Sienna should be on her way. Try to look happy about it, Spencer." He doesn't look like he's going to reply, so you leave him, and return to your station at the end of the corridor. The Callbot comes out, gives you a quick, frightened glance, and hustles down the corridor to the stairway.

A quarter hour passes with no noise or movement from Call. You're about to check on him when your phone chimes with a text: Andrea, telling you that Sienna has just arrived. You knock twice on Call's door, then burst in.

Jelena Petrovich whirls around. She's in her pants, but her blouse is only halfway done up. She's still in her stocking feet.

For a moment you can't think of anything to say. Despite being ready for this sight, you're taken aback by the change.

Then words form. "The fuck is taking you so long?"

Her mouth works. Then, peevishly, she retorts. "I'm not used to this shit, okay? The buttons on this shirt are all wrong."

"It's a fucking blouse, not a shirt, and didn't you ever notice the buttons on a girl's things? But sit still and keep quiet, okay? Sienna just got here."

Jelena pales. "Shit. Really?"

"Yes, really. Why, what's wrong?"

"I'm not ready, that's the fucking problem."

"So get ready. You've got lots of time. Just do it quietly." You shut the door on the ugly face she's making at you, and retreat into the other room.

It's another long wait, and feels even longer than the wait for Jelena. You start tapping your foot and flexing your fists, then force yourself to stop. You're almost on the point of looking out the door to see what the hold up is when you finally hear voices. One of them is loud enough that you can recognize it as Sienna Goldman's. That girl has a voice like a foghorn when she wants to use it.

And she suddenly she does use it. From the corridor comes her loud cry, "Jesus Fuck!"
1112111311222221222222122121211212112211.title: Here Come the Coeds, Part 2

choices: Yes, you've got a job for them
1112111311222221222222122121211212112211.content:
You hesitate for only a few seconds before deciding that Sienna's yell means something's gone wrong outside. You lunge for the door.

Your eyes have grown accustomed to the dark, so even in the gloomy hallway you can dimly make out the two figures a few feet down. Andrea would be the taller one, and Sienna the shorter one. The third figure --

Christ. There's definitely a third figure leaning halfway out of one the rooms. Judging by the lumpy shape of the skull, it must be Spencer. Fucking moron must've put his head out when he heard Sienna's voice.

In seven huge steps you're down the hallway to the group. Sienna twists and yelps. But Andrea grabs her and you press up close with the mask. Again, Andrea and her victim tumble the floor, but this time they take you with them.

Fists, elbows and someone's forehead collide as two bodies try pulling themselves out of a three-body wreck. "Oh, Jesus Christ, Chen!" Andrea yells. "Figure out another fucking way of doing this!"

"We got her, didn't we? No thanks to that fucker in the -- " You kick savagely at Spencer, but miss him so completely that you doubt he even knew you were trying to strike him.

Thomason's still yelling, though. "I'm gonna break something! Like, your fucking head next time, if I don't break -- "

"Quiet! We don't want Jeffries up here. Come on, help me get her -- This is your fucking fault, Spencer! Get out here and get one of her ankles!"

He finally moves, so that puts four bodies in the narrow corridor, and then you bump into a fifth: the newly minted Jelena Petrovich. "What the fuck happened?" she demands.

"Your fuck buddy almost blew it for us." You grimace as you haul Sienna along the corridor. "He put his head in the hall just Sienna was going by. No wonder she screamed."

"I thought it was Josh," Spencer says truculently. You can't be sure, but it sounds like someone hit someone else -- probably Call punching Spencer and discovering he hasn't got the same upper-body muscle mass as he had before.

"It all worked out," Andrea says, though her tone is angry. "Except for me nearly twisting my ankle."

"Want me to suck on it and make it better?"

"Bite me, Chen."

You get Sienna into the makeshift work room and twist on the light. Her face is blotched all over with red spots. Andrea sags against one wall, and you fall back in a corner to catch your breath. Jelena stares at you doubtfully from doorway. "So, are you just gonna sit there and watch," she asks.

"We need to get her clothes off her," you say. "You wanna do the honors, since me and Thomason did the work of getting her in here?"

Jelena shrugs, and squats down next to Sienna. You clamber past, to crawl back into the hallway.

A minute later, Andrea joins you. "I'm serious about us figuring out another way of doing it when Fatima gets here," she says. "I went down funny on my ankle."

"Did you sprain it or strain it?"

"I don't think so, but I could have."

"Okay. I'll tell Che -- I'll tell Chelsea -- " You break off and bite your lip.

"You'll tell Chelsea what?"

"Nothing. I didn't say 'Chelsea'."

"Sure you did, you said, I'll tell Chelsea -- "

"I was thinking about those cheerleader tryouts. Now you have an excuse not to go to them."

Andrea keeps quiet a moment. "Why do you have to tell Chelsea I won't be going to the tryouts?"

"I don't, obviously, since I don't have anything to do with her." You rub your eyes. "I'm just exhausted. You said you sprained your ankle, I thought of the tryouts, I thought of Chelsea."

"I didn't sprain my ankle."

"Fucking brilliant, we'll have a party to celebrate."

There's a much longer pause. You hold your breath. You've about decided that Thomason has dropped the matter. But he hasn't.

"Is Chelsea someone else in a mask?"

"No!"

"Because if you got me being Andrea -- "

"I don't have anybody playing Chelsea."

"It'd be sweet if you did."

"Chelsea doesn't do anything for us. Couldn't, not the way Andrea could. Chelsea's just a psychotic, stuck-up, duck-faced bitch."

"What's the deal with Gordon lately?"

"What deal?" She's really starting to piss you off.

"You know what deal. He's turned into a total pothead, got kicked off the squad, got dumped by Chelsea. All kinds of stuff that should leave him pissed off, but he's walking around like he's blissed out or something."

"Maybe he had a religious experience."

"Was he an early experiment of yours? Before you got all this figured out?"

"I don't know what's going on with Black, and I don't care. My brain is a little more than fried from having to deal with Call and Spencer and them, so don't make the night harder for me than it has to be, okay? I think there's still a couple of beers in that room where Call was -- next door down, I mean. Look in there and see if there's one or two in there we can split."

After regarding you dubiously for a few seconds, Thomason slides away to check. While he's gone, the door opens and Jelena comes out. "I got her shit here. Do I give it to Spencer, like we did with me?"

"Sure. And kick him in the balls while you're in there. Rennerhoff, too, so he knows not to pull a fucked up thing like that when Fatima gets here.

She bumps into Andrea on her way to Spencer's room, and you hear them talking in low tones for a minute, then Andrea sits down next to you with a rapidly warming beer. You split it, and Thomason's smart enough not to return to the previous subject. Lacking any other subject, though, you keep silent.

* * * * *

You're sorely tempted to cancel the rest of the night after the near-disaster with Sienna, but you decide you'd rather just get things over with. You press Spencer's mask onto her face, and give the new Spencerbot the same set of orders you gave to the Callbot. You let Jelena carry Sienna's mask in to Spencer, and pull Andrea aside for a brief conference. "Okay, let's handle Fatima this way. You call her, bring her up, take her to the end of the hall like before, but instead of opening the door, knock on it. Then turn her around to talk to her. I'll open the door and get the mask on her from behind. How's that sound?"

"And she'll fall forward into me."

"No, I'll catch her. I'll break my ankle."

"I hope so. Fine, we'll try it."

But the plan is for naught. You're just separating when you see a shadowy figure at the head of the stairs. It takes a few tentative steps toward you, and a girlish voice calls, "Sienna?"

Thomason sucks in a hissed breath. "Fatima," she says in a whisper, and grabs your forearm.

"You already called her?"

"No, I -- "

"Sienna? Andrea?" Fatima draws closer.

Andrea's grip on you tightens. "Yeah, it's me," she calls back. "Fatima? What are you doing here?"

"Sienna texted me, said something about coming out here." The girls sounds very frightened. "The guy downstairs said you were up here." There's a hanging pause. "With a bunch of guys."

"I'll get the mask," you murmur to Andrea. She doesn't let go of you, and you have to pry her fingers off.

"We're just hanging out," Andrea says. "There were some guys here earlier, but now it's just us."

Sienna's muffled reply is inaudible as you hustle into the work room and pull out the last of your blank masks. You hop back into the corridor and run forward.

Fatima draws back from you, and quavers out, "Who's that? What's going on?"

Before you reach her, a door creaks open, and another shadow joins those in the hall. "Oh, hey there," says Jelena in a husky voice. "I thought I heard you."

"Jelena? What's going on?" Fatima asks again.

"Nothing's going on," says Andrea. "We're just hanging out."

"Where's Sienna?"

"She's around," says Jelena. She puts her hand on Fatima's arm. "Come on, let's go in here. We're sitting around tell ghost stories."

Fatima squirms, and squirms harder when Jelena doesn't let go. "I've changed my mind, I don't wanna -- "

You take that as a signal and jump forward. You think you make out Fatima Zahedi's huge black eyes as they widen fearfully before you cover her face with a spectrally white mask.

* * * * *

"So we're all set?" you ask. You wish you sounded more cheerful, as you ought to sound after a difficult mission successfully accomplished.

"I think so," says Jelena Petrovich. "I wish I knew where I lived, though."

Sienna Goldman says nothing. Fatima Zahedi just giggles and gasps.

"It's alright," says Andrea. "I know where you guys live. But it doesn't matter. Jelena and Sienna can spend the night with me, their families and roommates won't mind. Fatima can do a sleepover with me too."

"That'd be awesome," Fatima says breathlessly. "Like, a real fucking slumber party, and we can paint each other's toenails and crank call cute guys -- " Jelena thumps her on the head.

"That sounds great, I'll leave it to you," you say. In Andrea's ear, you add, "Get them to concentrate on remembering how to be, you know -- " She nods. "I'll see you around at school tomorrow," you add to the others, and half-walk, half-run downstairs. You don't even glance at Jeffries as you race past.

In the parking lot you climb into your Jeep, but you don't say anything to the three shadow-veiled figures waiting there for you, not until four girls have come out, piled into some cars, and driven away. Then you address your new 'bots: "You assholes are my slaves, you got that?" Guttural grunts of assent come back. "Good. I might have a job for you."
1112111311222221222222122121211212112211.title: Here Come the Coeds, Part 3

choices: You're in too deep. Keep going
1112111311222221222222122121211212112211.content:
"But I haven't got anything for you yet," you continue after a long and pregnant pause. You had a couple of schemes flit through your brain while you were working on Fatima's mask for Rennerhoff, but you lay them aside.

So you tell the 'bots to go home. "Act normal, whatever that is for you guys. Only stay away from Jelena and Sienna and Fatima. And Andrea. Don't go near them, don't hassle them, don't talk about them. Understand?"

More of those guttural agreements.

"That's it, then. Get out of here."

You think you catch some under-the-breath comments from the Call- and Spencerbots, but nothing distinct. As soon as they're clear of the Jeep, you turn the motor over and race home.

You get it in the neck for being out so late without permission, but you've a few sheets of math homework with which to impress Mrs. Chen.

But you are falling behind in your schoolwork. The last few days have been brutally preoccupied with mask stuff.

* * * * *

The next morning brings a text from Chelsea, reminding you that "Dane" is supposed to deliver the month's supply to school this morning. on it, you text back. andrea not trying out, ankle problm.

bullshit, she replies, but you ignore it.

Dane actually called you yesterday afternoon shortly after his meeting with Macauly. You put him off, though, until this morning -- a Friday -- and you meet him at a little before eight in the school parking lot. "Don't flash that thing," you tell him as with a desperate desire to please he yanks the new case out of his car. "Other than that, good job. Talk to you next month." You half turn away, then turn back when you catch him eyeing the case with open curiosity. "I said, see you next month, fuckface." He hops away without waiting for another warning. You crawl under your Jeep and lock the weed in the special strong box.

You -- or Chen -- hates the days when he has a full load of product sitting around. It makes you -- or Chen -- think about all the disasters that could happen. Scarcely more fun is contacting people to tell them that you're ready for delivery. The first such talk occurs on the soccer field, when you manage to sidle up to Garner when there's no one else around. "Got your order in," you tell him. "You got the money?"

"What? Oh. Sure." He sounds embarrassed.

"You don't wanna do this?"

"No, I do. I just -- Uh -- "

"Don't know how to handle it? Gardinhire's done it before, he can do it again for you. Tonight, at the country club. That's where used to do the hand-off, we can do it again tonight."

"I thought I was taking over for -- "

"You are. He's just the mechanic, swapping one bag of shit for another. He's done it before. You just make sure he's got the money. All of it."

"Sure. Anything else?" Garner's eyes narrow, and you realize you were regarding him speculatively. You shake your head, and return to your position on the field. But still the thought is there. Maybe that's a new spot for Mendoza. Get the AP fucks well and truly screwed into the scheme.

The only other people to contact at school are Evans and Mendoza and Thomason -- the fakes, of course -- telling them that you'll be over Saturday to give them their month's inventory. Evans whines that he hasn't got enough money to pay for the increased load; you tell him you'll carry him this month, but no further, for the extra charges. You also tell Kirkham you'll be over on Saturday with something for him. "About fucking time," he snarls softly.

You keep an eye out for your newest recruits, and their replacements. But the 'bots of Call and Spencer and Rennerhoff -- as the originals should be -- are scarce, and you don't spot the new girls in the hall. You put away the temptation to go looking for Andrea.

You do catch up with the Rennerhoff-bot toward the end of lunch, though, as you're making a circuit of the campus. It's out by the portables, naturally, and it looks and moves just like him. Head up, eyes alert for mischief, a nasty smile pulling at the corners of its mouth. It freezes when it sees you, and that smile starts jerking spasmodically when you approach. Must be a nervous tic.

"Yo, where's your friends Call and Spencer?" you ask.

"Dunno." The 'bot whinnies a little. "Skipping, I guess."

"Yeah? Dumbasses gotta be dumbasses, I suppose."

"I suppose."

"If that's so, then what the fuck are you doing here?"

The 'bot laughs, and swallows.

"What do those guys do when they're skipping?"

"Stuff." It shrugs stiffly. "Josh picks up some extra work in places. I think Jeff gets drunk."

"Figures. But Call's a workaholic? I didn't picture that."

"He wants money. Or needs it. I don't know." Again, the shoulders twitch.

"And what do you do when you're not in school?"

"Oh, just party, hang around." He laughs, whinnies, gulps. "Like, with Horner, sometimes Evans and Thomason." A distant look of slight confusion comes into his eye. The thing is probably remembering that you told Rennerhoff that Thomason is now a fake, and is probably thinking how weird it would be to hang out with him. The 'bot shakes itself, and that smile comes back. "Probably go up to the Warehouse again tonight. It was fun last night, you know -- " Breathy laughter whistles through its nose. "But tonight's Friday, you know, so -- " It hops on the balls of its feet. "Band's playing."

"Oh?" You're not really interested. "Which one?"

"Slow Fast Hazel, for one. That'll be funny, seeing as how we saw Jelena up at the Warehouse last -- " It trails off with a click in its throat, and a look of terror comes into its eye. "Except I'm not supposed to -- "

"That's right, you're supposed to stay away from them. Well, I guess there's no harm if you go see her play."

"Yeah?" It leaps up and punches the air when you give it permission. Fucking dillweed. You wonder how the others can put up with him.

* * * * *

On the way out of school when the bell rings, you get another blast from the past: Seth Javits texts, asking if you can meet with him. Caleb's been keeping his distance lately, and you wonder what this meeting is about. Is he finally maybe getting tired of the charade? After last night, you might go along with him if he says he wants to wind things down. You tell him to meet you at the elementary school basement at four-thirty.

You've got Chinese leftover takeout and a couple of pounds of weed sitting out when the door flies open and Javits bounds inside. "Yeah!" he roars, and pumps his fists in the air. Halfway down the stairs, though, he freezes when he sees what you've got in front of you. "Jesus, man!"

"Yeah, can you believe it's been a month since I went into this racket? Time for a new distribution. Close the door, okay?"

He complies, then comes back down to peer over your shoulder as you weigh out some more weed. "Was there that much last time?"

"No. I've been growing the business."

"Jesus!"

"You don't know the half of it. Literally. Don't tell me that's why you wanted to see me, so you could catch up on my -- "

"No. Well, yeah. We just haven't talked in forever." He perches on a desk.

You give him a sidelong stare. His whole demeanor and body language is perfectly "Javits like". Head up, shoulders back, punchy movements, arrogant stare. "How's Chelsea? Still putting out?"

"Oh yeah. Well, mostly. She's fucking psycho, though. You know that. Way more of a handful than Cindy was."

"Is the pussy worth it?"

"Yeah, sure." He scratches under a pit. "You know, I guess I kinda wanted to say, uh -- "

"Yeah?"

"Ah, fuck it." He hops to the ground and heads to the stairs.

"Come on, man, I need someone to keep me company. What is it you wanted to say?"

He stares at the floor, kicks at it. "You'll get pissed."

"No I won't. I can't afford to. If I turn over this table, there goes all of this month's income."

He looks at you sharply from under his furrowed brow. Then he shrugs. "I guess I just wanted to say I'm sorry I made such a fucking scene about the way you were acting after you got inside Chen here."

"Yeah? Apology accepted, I guess. Why are you making it?"

"I just -- " He wheels in place, dances a little, mimes a free throw and gives himself a silent cheer. "Patterson's been working me real hard since Gordon fucked himself all up. Really bringing up my game, he's got me at a whole new level, and it's fucking awesome. Chelsea's pussy is great, but -- " He smooches the back of his hand. "I'm just rockin' it these days, you know?" He hops onto another table and drums it excitedly. "You haven't noticed?"

"Noticed how you've been rocking it? No. What's this got to do with me?"

"Oh, nothing. I guess I'm saying I'm really into this new, like, life for me. Me and Seth, we really understand each other now." He bangs the heel of his hand against his head. "We're all like one now, like the way you and Chen were instantly one at the start, I guess. I thought you were just being an asshole, but this merger, it's -- " He clasps his hands together and sighs happily. "Anyway, I guess I'm saying you don't have to worry about me."

"Great. Thanks. I'll tell that fucking ulcer you gave me it can go away now."

He laughs at that, slaps you on the shoulder, tells you he'll see you later. Up and out and away he goes.

You lean back. Shit. Was that the last you saw of the real Caleb Johansson?
1112111311222221222222122121211212112211.title: Send Offs, Expected and Otherwise

1112111311222221222222122121211212112211.content:
You're not willing to call it quits on your new life, but you need to get out of Chen's head for a little while, if for no other reason than because you want to send the Chenbot to the country club to handle the night's work.

But you don't want to be stuck down in the school basement all evening with nothing to do. After ten minutes of back-and-forthing with yourself, weighing whether you're desperate enough for the respite, you decide that you are.

You call the Jamie Rennerhoff robot and give it directions to the old Acheson Elementary School.

* * * * *

It's a very shy robot, and doesn't even knock when it gets to the school. You send it a text when you lose patience waiting, and it replies by telling you it's just outside the door. "Get the fuck in here," you tell it when you find it standing stupidly on the stoop.

It's a dumb-looking thing, and with Chen's eyes you can hardly believe that Jamie Rennerhoff ever frightened you. He's half a head taller than you, it's true, but he's very lanky. He looks especially lanky now, having changed into a pair of knee-length shorts and a v-neck t-shirt that shows how skinny and bony his torso is. He's very pale, too, and hairless. He blinks and grins nervously at you. Why did someone who looks like this -- who looks even less threatening than Caleb or Keith -- ever unnerve you? Because of who he hangs out, you suppose.

Also, there's that Joker-like smile and laugh, but these don't hold any terror for Gary Chen.

"Take your clothes off," you tell the 'bot.

It looks startled, but only for a moment does it delay. Then it peels off the canvas shoes and ankle socks, the shorts and underwear, and the t-shirt. All very neat and fast. You avoid looking at it, and tell it to lie on a dirty desk and close its eyes. "Keep 'em shut," you tell it. "Try to go to sleep if you can." Even though the 'bot is under your control, you don't want it seeing that you're not really Gary Chen.

And just in case it does open its eyes and look around, you hide yourself behind a clutter of bookcases before taking off your own mask.

* * * * *

"You're fucked in the head, you know that, Prescott?" Gary Chen mutters. He slouches behind the briefcase, weighing and parceling out weed. It's the Chenbot, naturally, but it's the Chenbot resting on Fatima Zahedi's unconscious body; the original mudbot is still in the corner. He looks you up and down. "Christ, but you're a sad sight."

You hold up your flaccid penis -- a long and lanky thing, like the rest of Rennerhoff. "Wanna closer look?" You can't help thinking it's a funny thought, and the whinnying laugh blows noisily through your nose. Your cheeks ache with a wide grin.

"Fuck you, man. You got all the fucking world to pretend to be, and you pick this -- " The Chenbot can't even find words to convey the depth of its disgust with Jamie Rennerhoff, and gives up with a grimace.

"I need a break from being you, asshole. Don't you ever wanna take a break from being yourself?"

The Chenbot glowers at you, and sweeps a small pile of weed into a tiny plastic baggie.

"And I can have some fun like this, I guess. More fun than as Call or Spencer."

Yeah, Rennerhoff has seen them up close. Spencer's got nothing to do but drink, and Call tries to avoid the drinking by finding crap to do at his uncle's body shop when he's not working at Wal-Mart. He could make quite a sum of money at all the part-time work he's picked up, except he blows it all on poker games.

But Rennerhoff, you're pleased to discover as you stretch your skinny arms, is more your kind of guy, into videogames, TV, music, goofing off with friends and trash-talking with them. In fact, if it weren't for the kind of friends he had, the stuff he does would look a lot like the kind of stuff you and Caleb and Keith do.

You pause after pulling on the underwear. It startles you, of a sudden, to have this kind of insight into a person whose mask you woke into less than a minute ago.

Oh, it's probably on account of the 'bot's being on its way to see Nicholas Horner and Rich Austin to do some target shooting when Chen called. Will Prescott has never held a gun in his life -- FPS games don't count -- but there's not a lot of difference, you feel now with Rennerhoff's brain -- between playing with a real pistol and playing with a fake one.

And that's because, unlike Call or Spencer or Kirkham or Chen, Rennerhoff would never use a pistol anywhere except as target practice.

So Rennerhoff and his extracurricular activities were at the forefront of the 'bot's brain when you slipped into the mask, and they instantly felt familiar. In fact, the more you wriggle into Rennerhoff's history and life, the more it feels like your own.

Jesus, you think with a chill. Did I narrowly miss turning out like Rennerhoff? Did he narrowly miss turning out like me?

"If you're gonna put that shit on, put it on," says the Chenbot, and its words pull you back to the present. So you resume dressing. The shorts, the t-shirt, the socks and shoes on the bony, canoe-like feet. "How do I look," you ask when you're done.

"Like a fucking doorknob."

"Awesome." You inhale and exhale shakily around a stifled giggle. The laughter feels like a nervous tic. "Okay, I'm gone for the night. You know you have to make the sale to Gardinhire up at work, right?"

"Yes," the Chenbot says through gritted teeth.

"And finish sorting things out."

"I know what I'm doing, Prescott."

"Just making sure. When you're done at work, send me a text so we can arrange to, you know." You point to his face, then to yours. The Chenbot bares its teeth, and the hatred in its stare nearly burns a hole in your chest. "And you know, right, that you don't tell anyone where I am or what I'm doing."

"I'd put my fucking face in a buzz saw before I even admitted to knowing you," it hisses.

"Good. Okay, see you around." You turn and trip and bang your knees on the stairs. Got into the brain faster than I got into the body, you reflect, and embarrassment burns your face as you get back up. You don't turn around to catch the Chenbot's expression, and mercifully it doesn't chase you out the door with a taunt.

Outside, you get in the old minivan and double check you've got your driver's license. Rennerhoff's picture looks like a mug shot of a deranged teenager, or one strung out on goofballs. You paste an imitation onto your face and check yourself out in the rearview mirror. The reflection's bangs and nose are hooked in the wrong direction, but the grin is perfectly manic. You don't find the eyes, though -- wetly gleaming with gleeful malice -- until, out of habit, it feels like, the image of a hot, swollen pussy flashes into your mind.

Then you've got Rennerhoff's face perfectly. With an asthmatic giggle, you start the engine.

* * * * *

"What fucking kept you?" Rich Austin calls. He pulls off the shooting muffs and drops them around his neck. The protective goggles glint in the failing afternoon light.

"Toldja, had to make a stop first," you yell back. Austin lives on a farm a couple of miles west of town, and you have to hike through a field of tall grass and over a ditch to reach the makeshift shooting range he and his dad have erected. The grass scratches and nicks at your bare calves. Picking your way carefully along on your long legs, you feel like a stork.

Clouds are coming up from the south, and it looks like there will be some evening showers. That might be why Austin looks unhappy: even if you weren't late, the weather looks like it will spoil his evening fun.

Krack! Horner, though, is getting in as much time as he can. That's Nicholas Horner, the closest thing Rennerhoff has to a best friend. Like Rennerhoff, he's a kid of slight build who plays remora to the gang of Call of Spencer and Thomason and the others. He holds a pistol stiffly in front of him and bends his concentration on the distant target. He squeezes the trigger and gets off another shot. Then a third before he lowers the pistol and mutters some bad words.

Austin grabs him by the shoulder and massages it. "You don't gotta be perfect to have fun," he hollers so Horner can hear him through his own muffs. Back to you: "Here, got a new one for you to try." He raises the lid of the nearby gun case. "The one on the end." He grins and waggles his eyebrows.

You pick it up. It's heavier than Will Prescott would have expected a gun to be, but about as heavy as Jamie Rennerhoff would have expected. But neither you nor your alias see anything special about it. "It's an H&K USP Tactical," Austin exclaims, and follows up the identification with a lot of technical language that you could never follow and Rennerhoff barely can. "Come on, try it out." He pushes Horner to the side and you to the fore. Horner passes over the muffs and goggles while Austin preps the pistol.

And when you have it in your hand --

For a moment it feels very hot, for you're conscious of what it is, and what it can do, and that you've never done anything with anything like it.

But Rennerhoff has. You close your eyes, settle your feet, inhale deeply. It's what Rennerhoff would do, and that settles you comfortably into his psyche, so that when you open your eyes again you are completely calm. You find the target, raise the pistol, sight along it. and gently squeeze the trigger.

Your heart leaps joyously at the crack and recoil. "Awesome!" you exclaim, and laugh enthusiastically.
1112111311222221222222122121211212112211.title: A Night Out as Jamie Rennerhoff

1112111311222221222222122121211212112211.content:
It's bracing, that first shot, even though you only wing the target. It welds you and Rennerhoff together.

It gives you an immense feeling of power and of peace to grip a pistol, to caress it and squeeze it and send a hot bolt of metal screaming instantly across space to fatally puncture a target. You -- Jamie Rennerhoff -- could never aim a pistol at anyone, let alone put a bullet into them -- but it pleasures all your senses and, in truth, stokes your libido, to hold and release such power. After you've sent off two more shots, your grin is so wide it's practically tickling your ear lobes.

Austin tells you a little more about what you've been holding, and you enthusiastically describe how it felt, and you pass it along to Horner so he can try it. The three of you are soon talking, in a rough and rude way, about Austin's new H&K the way wine connoisseurs might talk about a bottle of Cabarnet.

And the smell and the heat and the heft of the pieces do intoxicate you, a little, and you flap your hands and gesture excitedly about the merits of one kind of pistol against another. Austin snorts derisively, because you've only got web learning to guide you: Rennerhoff's parents don't believe in guns and won't intentionally let their kids near one, so all of Jamie's exposure to them has been through Austin. Once Rennerhoff's on his own, though, he's determined to start assembling a collection like Rich and his dad have got.

A gray sheet of rain comes sweeping in after about an hour of shooting, but your trio had foreseen its coming and packed things away and repaired to the barn before even the first drops began to spatter on the grass. In the ranch house kitchen, Mrs. Austin surrenders to the impetuous hunger of teenage boys and lets you and Rich and Nicholas prep baloney sandwiches and chips for supper. You sit at TV trays in a small parlor, within earshot of Rich's dad, who's watching TV in the living room. So you keep the conversation neutral and your voices low.

You don't have much to contribute, for Rich and Nicholas keep the talk to family matters. They're cousins, actually: the youngest son and only child of an older brother and younger sister in the extended Austin family. Rich graduated from Westside last May, where he used his hard muscles and steel-tipped boots to dominate Call's gang and terrorize those portions of the junior class that weren't already traumatized by the basketball and football squads. He's the one who pulled Horner into that crowd, and through Horner pulled Rennerhoff in. Maybe it's on account of his graduating, and needing to make it in the larger world, but he seems to have mellowed, or at least turned his raging need to kick shit around in a more productive direction. His plans for the future involve working for a year on his dad's farm while taking community college courses to improve his brain before enrolling in the military. He's already got the buzz cut and the bark.

After cleaning up from supper, he takes you and Horner out onto the back porch, where the rain -- now tailing off -- has turned the cool autumn air chilly; you clasp your arms about you as you lean against the wooden railing. "So it's Friday night, you bitches," Austin says with an evil grin. "What are our plans?"

"A bar?" says Horner hopefully. "I brought my ID. My fake one, I mean."

"I hope you fucking meant it, but you got any better ideas?"

"Do you?"

"We could go to the Warehouse," you blurt out. Rennerhoff hasn't been there that often, and it still carries the excitement of danger for him.

"I can't," Austin says. "Graduated."

"But you worked security out there," you say. "I bet Call would let you in."

"He'd kick my ass to the curb, and he knows me and he knows I graduated."

"Couldn't we sneak you in?" asks Horner.

"I bet I could talk Call into letting you in," you insist even as you know it's an unwise thing to say.

"Nah. It was fun when I was in school, but it's pathetic to try to go back. Anyway, I was actually thinking about -- " He breaks off with a grin and pulls out his wallet. It's brick-thick, and he has to rifle through it carefully before he can pull out the bent business card. He hands it to Horner. "What do you say to that?"

Horner stares at it in puzzlement. You sidle over to see it, but with a gasp he drops his arm to his side before you can make out more than a smear of fancy black graphics. "No shit! Is this a -- "

"Sh! Yeah!" Austin winks wolfishly. "Escort service."

"Dude!" Horner grabs you by the front of your shirt and almost tears the thin fabric. "Fucking escorts!" he hisses excitedly.

You tense all over and go up on tiptoes. Whores. Expert whores. Clean, beautiful, expert whores, open for business. You twine your own fingers around the folds of Horner's shirt and grin back at him.

"Of course, you'll have to pay for your own."

Austin's words land with a thud. Those tense muscles in your calves and fingers turn stiff. Horner does a double take at his cousin. "Pay for our own?"

"They're not cheap, Nick. I can't carry all three of you. I can barely pay for one myself. I'm only in a mood to celebrate 'cos I aced my first calculus test this week."

"How much are they," asks Horner. His voice is very tight.

Austin looks between him and you. Please say less than twenty, you plead silently. Please say less than twenty.

The number he cites is ridiculously higher than twenty. Your grin freezes into a rictus, and you swallow.

Then Horner says, "Yeah, I could swing that. I'll have to save up all over again for -- "

"What about me," you exclaim. "Can you guys split my cost?"

They both swivel in surprise. "Can't you pay for yourself," demands Austin.

"Fuck, no! Not at that price! I can throw in twenty dollars -- "

"Twenty? You couldn't pick up a whore at the bus station for that!" Rich turns back to his cousin. "We need to go by an ATM fo ryou?"

"Can't we do something all three of us can do?" you plead.

"Like go to the Warehouse?" Austin says derisively.

"No, but -- " You pluck at Horner's shirt.

He licks his lips and glances anxiously between you and his cousin. Austin's face is hard. "We can go to the Warehouse next weekend. Or tomorrow night," Horner tells you. "This is -- "

And when Austin's face relaxes, Horner's does too. At that moment you recognize you've been cast into the cold.

It's worse than that you haven't got the money. It's that you're not family.

"Okay, you guys have fun," you tell them. You say it very neutrally, and you hope that they'll notice that you're saying it in a very neutral way, so that they'll feel guilty about tossing you overboard so they can have a night with some overpriced hookers. "Thanks for the time on the range," you tell Austin.

"Sure," he says vaguely. "You should come back out, I dunno, like next week sometime. Tuesday, maybe." His brighten again as he turns back to Horner.

"Call Thomason, see if he's free," Horner tells you. "I bet he'd go to the Warehouse with you, if you're wanting to go."

You want to retort that you don't need anyone to go with you if you want to go out there, but just smile back while biting your lower lip. "I'll text you, like, tomorrow or something," you reply. He nods at you, and pulls Austin close so they can talk more confidentially about their plans for the night. So you don't even get a "Good night" as you return into the house.

Rich's parents are more polite, though, and do give you a pleasant send off. You tug at a t-shirt that feels like it's shrunk in the humidity as you half-trot back to the minivan, and inside the cab rub and scratch at bare legs that feel like they've broken out in a rash. You cuss at Horner under your breath as you head back to town.

* * * * *

What a sad little self-pitying douchebag you are, you tell yourself. You even have to blink back some wetness, and when you look in the mirror, to see if your eyes are red or bruised with suppressed tears you see that Rennerhoff's perpetual grin has acquired a brittle and trembling gleam. You try wiping it away with the back of your hand, but your face muscles naturally spring back into the shape of a desperate smile.

So you giggle, because you're feeling fucking sorry for Jamie Fucking Rennerhoff, who hasn't got any friends worth calling friends, just a lot of shits who put up with him because no one else will. Jesus! You wipe at your eye again, and if the giggle that bubbles in your throat didn't have a note of madness in it, it would sound like a sob.

But what does it matter to you? Really, who the fuck are you to feel sorry for Jamie Rennerhoff? You're not him, you've got your own life. Okay, it's not your own life, it's Gary Chen's life, seeing as how Gordon Black is inhabiting yours --

You steer sharply into the parking lot of a strip center and brake hard into a parking space. You've got too many thoughts flying through your head -- not all of them yours -- to handle while driving.

You've no reason to give a fuck about Jamie Rennerhoff. You're in his mask only for the night, so you'd have something to do except sit in the elementary school basement. The obvious thing to do is to go to the Warehouse, for shits and grins if nothing else.

But at the thought of Gordon Black and Will Prescott you had another idea. Why not use this temporary identity to go see how your first victim in this chain of cock-ups is handling the life you left behind?
1112111311222221222222122121211212112211.title: Family Over Friends

1112111311222221222222122121211212112211.content:
The idea is loopy, but not lunatic. Jamie Rennerhoff is definitely not a friend of Will Prescott's, and it would be far out of the ordinary if he showed up at Will's house and suggested doing something together.

But Gordon Black doesn't know anything about Will Prescott -- that's the main reason you want to see what's up with him -- and so wouldn't know that Prescott and Rennerhoff have nothing to do with each other.

But after entertaining the notion, you decide it was just a bit of brain fluff, not an idea, and toss it away. You put on Jamie Rennerhoff's mask so you could be Jamie Rennerhoff for the evening, and that means doing what Jamie Rennerhoff would do.

Which after the evening at Rich Austin's would probably involve going home and gaming the evening away, but you're in a dangerous mood. And when Jamie Rennerhoff is in a dangerous mood --

Okay, the first thing is that Jamie Rennerhoff gets a partial erection when he's feeling puffed up with adrenaline. But going to the Warehouse without being part of a group, that would definitely be something Jamie Rennerhoff would want to do when he's feeling dangerous.

Still, you have to consciously stop yourself when you pull out the cell phone to text Thomason to see if he's up for an evening there.

* * * * *

"The fuck you think you're going," says Jonas Martin. His buzz cut visibly bristles as he looks you up and down contemptuously.

"Inside. To see Slow Fast Hazel," you retort. You're actually taller than the basketball player, but he's unimpressed at the way you raise yourself up straight and put out your chest. He's probably even less impressed with your sneering grin.

"You think so?" says Martin. "I don't."

You're at the front door to the Warehouse, having barely managed to squeeze the minivan into a parking place at the far end of the crowded lot. It's not a big lot, and there's no parking allowed on the streets, so most people carpool in. That earned you a dirty shout from a crowd of Eastman students when they saw you getting out of the van alone. You ignored them.

It's almost nine o'clock now. You killed some time at home doing some easy homework, then changed into torn jeans and a silver-colored long-sleeve shirt you rescued from a thrift shop. You think you look nice, certainly nicer than the guys that Martin is letting into the Warehouse with only a cursory nod.

"What's the fucking matter," you shrilly demand of him. "There's not a fucking cover charge, there's not a -- "

"There's not a fucking prayer for you," says Martin. He shoves you in the chest. "So step back."

You're about to fly at him when a strong hand grabs you. You look over. It's Josh Call. A startled look of recognition shows in his eyes when you lock yours with his. "What's going on?" he asks while gazing intently at you.

"Your dumbass friends wants in," says Martin.

"Tell him to let me in," you tell Call.

"Let him in," Call says dully to Martin, his eyes still fastened on yours.

"You're just the parking valet," Martin sneers. "I get to decide -- "

"Make him let me in," you mutter at Call. It comes to you in a flash that the 'bot recognizes you as its master, even with a change of masks.

Call wheels stiffly on Martin. He balls up his fists and gets in his face. "Let him in, cocksucker," he says.

Martin resists the challenge for only a few seconds, then relents with a casual shrug. "Fine. Glad I'm only the doorman and not an actual customer." As you pass him, he puts a hand on your back and shoves you in through the door.

* * * * *

The percussive beat of the music had been loud enough outside. Inside the Warehouse, where it has four walls to echo off of, it's assaultive. And you're not even on the dance floor, the door to which is on your left. Above the drums and the harsh guitars a female voice rises in a wail.

That would be Jelena Petrovich. The new one. It's a thrill of anticipation to imagine seeing what you've made in action.

But first you duck into the bar. Jeffries is behind the counter, with Lane Overstreet and another guy you don't recognize. From Jeffries' expression you can tell he recognizes you from last night, but he says nothing when you ask for a beer, and give him five ones in exchange. You crack it open and drink while looking around. There's only a few people in the bar area, and only a few of them -- like Laurent Delacroix, the captain of the wrestling team -- do you recognize. None of them are people you'd hang out with.

So you look into the next room, the one with the makeshift tables and booths. It's darker and more crowded, which makes it a lot harder to make out any familiar faces or forms. At the far end of the room is the staircase leading up to the fuck rooms. It's guarded tonight by a hulking brute in track pants and a knitted hat, looking like a steroid-engorged version of Gary Chen.

Maybe you should text Thomason or one of those guys, now that you're inside. If you don't find someone to stand and laugh with, it's gonna be a real boring night.

But you haven't scoped out the entire place yet. There's still the dance floor, which is where most people will be.

When you step into that room, it's like piercing the sound barrier. It's so loud you think your ears will crack.

It's a wide and open space of hard surfaces, taking up half of the building all the way up to the rafters. At the far end is a makeshift stage on which four people are performing. They've got their amplifiers turned up way past eleven.

Three of them you don't recognize. "Sure is," Jeffries says.

"I mean, I know there's a charge for using them at night. Like, last night." God, that hideous smile is back on your face. What Jeffries must think of you for grinning at the topic. "What about in the afternoons? This afternoons?"

He stares at you a moment, then gives you a reflection of your own grin. "Why, got plans for today?"

"No, I just -- "

"Who with?"

"Fuck you." You blush when he raises his eyebrows and laughs. "I was just wondering what the rules are."

"The rules are, there's a charge when Cantu's around. You know who he is, right?"

You can guess, and you mumble your gratitude to Jeffries.

But why were you asking? You don't realize the answer to that question until you're back in the dance hall, and your eyes fall on Karter -- and the girl he is fondling.
1112111311222221222222122121211212112211.title: A Return to the Scene of the Grime

choices: This is too much; yell at her
1112111311222221222222122121211212112211.content:
It feels pathetic to use a mask to get laid. Oh sure, you used one last night, but it was scarcely your idea. You just happened to be in the right place, at the right time, under the right mask, when the right person grabbed you. Even then, it was mostly instinct and lack of willpower that carried you upstairs and through the spasmodic motions.

So you drift back into the dance hall and drink your beer and keep your mouth shut as Karter and the others talk. After you're done with your drink, you wait a bit until it won't seem pathetic, then excuse yourself with a sigh.

Jesus, I'm bored, you think when you're out in the minivan again. You'll say this for the life of Gary Chen, it's not boring. And since you're supposed to be responsible for it -- and because it's been yours for the last month -- you are nervous about being away from it. What kind of trouble might the Chenbot have gotten up to in the last twenty-four hours?

You call it, but instead of meeting at the elementary school, you tell it to come to Rennerhoff's place. "Try not to act like Genghis Khan when you get here," you tell it. "My folks are here."

You can practically hear it sneering at you across the ether.

* * * * *

it works out okay, since Rennerhoff's folks are running errands when you get home. The switch off happens without a hitch, and you don't linger with the Rennerhoffbot. The Chenbot got a lot of work done in your absence, including distributions to Chen's school friends, but there's one other place you have to go. You're glad the 'bot saved this stop for the end, so you can enjoy it.

* * * * *

There are three cars in front of the house when you arrive, and you eye them warily before parking behind them and going up the walk to the front door. You'd called ahead to see if the coast was clear, and you're not sure it is. "It's open," a voice calls from within when you knock, and you push it open.

Four girls are splayed out in the living room. Andrea and Jelena are sitting on a futon, close to each other but not touching. Fatima is lying on a sleeping bag, flat on her back, staring at the ceiling. Sienna is hunched over a cell phone, tapping furiously at it. "Is it just you four," you ask cautiously.

Andrea nods. "I had to get rid of Semple and Winkler. And you shouldn't stay long, I don't know who will turn up."

"What about your mom?"

"She's at work."

"Jesus, is she always at work?"

"Usually."

"'Cos I got the money I borrowed." You toss a fat envelope to Andrea. "Tell everyone it was just a mistake, nothing got stole from your house."

Fatima giggles. "No one will believe it." She's turned her head to grin at you. Her big, coal-dark eyes shine with malicious amusement.

"They will if Andrea swears to it. How are the rest of you guys doing? Getting into it? Anyone have a gig at the Warehouse last night?"

"I did," says Jelena. She's been staring at the wall opposite without looking at you, and she's still staring at it.

"How'd it go?"

"It was fun. Everyone said I did good. It felt good." She slowly drags her ring finger across her lower lip, which is stained with purple lipstick, and licks the residue off. "I cut loose, gave 'em a really good show."

"She was amazing," says Andrea. "I always had the impression Yell was holding back. But last night -- " She shuts her eyes and draws in a deep breath.

"What about you two?" you ask Rennerhoff and Spencer.

Sienna remains preoccupied with the cell phone. Fatima giggles again. "I really like it. I'm having fun." She lets her knees fall open in a most unladylike way. "But poor Sienna, her home life -- "

"Fuck," says Sienna. She flings the cell away and pounces onto Fatima. They kiss, long and loudly and intensely. Fatima gropes her own bosom.

"I guess they've figured out how to make the best of it," you observe to the others. "How about you two?"

"We're not giving you fireworks, Chen," says Jelena. "So fuck off."

"Well, that's gratitude for you. But I don't want fireworks, I just want to make sure you're settling down, that you can be in character."

Jelena turns slowly until she's looking at you. "You want me to be in character, Chen? I can be in character." She gets up slowly, and walks toward you slowly, and leans back with one hand on her hip as she stares at you from dark eyes under dark bangs. "I can kick your ass. Would that be enough in character for you?"

You return her stare evenly. "Is this being in character, or do you have a serious problem with me?"

She wavers and breaks off first. "Nah, I don't got a problem," she says with a stiff shrug. "Except I was having a lot of fun fucking around with that pussy Winkler until you called and fucked it up for me."

"Really? What were you doing to him?" You are trying desperately to keep your eyes on her and not on the girls in the floor, for Sienna has climbed onto Fatima and is straddling her and put a hand up her shirt. They are making moaning noises.

"Oh, she was having fun," Andrea sniggers. "She was getting him all twisted up, playing nice and playing mean. It's too bad I'm not going to let you hang out here, or you could have fun watching her in action."

"So lemme see you in action at school." You jerk your head at Sienna and Fatima. "Those two weren't doing that, were they?"

"What's wrong with that?" Andrea asks with a smirk.

"Hey, I don't got a problem with it, so long as it doesn't screw things up for you."

"We're making it sexy for people," says Jelena with careful insolence. "Isn't that what you wanted? That's what Thomason says you want. For us to make it sexy." She steps back and raises her chin and tucks her fingers into her back pockets.

Yes, she's sexy alright, and you ignore your phone when it tweets at you. Jelena is wearing a black t-shirt with the slogan LIKE ME FUCK YOU stenciled across its front, and tight jeans with big rips, and boots. Her mane of dark hair is wild and unkempt. You'd like to peel her clothes off, like the bark off a tree.

You cover your lust by flicking a finger at her chest. "Your shirt needs a proofreader. It's missing a question mark after 'me'."

Her lip curls. "Hey Sienna, am I missing a question mark up here? Hey, I'm talking to you, bitch." She kicks Sienna in the side.

"What? I'm busy!" Sienna raises up long enough to snarl that, then plunges back in to Fatima.

"It's her shirt," Jelena tells you. "We've been swapping clothes around, seeing what works for each of us."

"Well, make yourself visible at school, if you don't mind," you say. "I'm curious to see what it looks like."

"Yeah, this is all for your pleasure, isn't it, Chen?" Jelena says. She pulls up her shirt and shows you a boob. "That's a little picture you can take home with you for tonight."

* * * * *

"Why don't we take it up to your bedroom, have some real fun?" you suggest to Chelsea after you're seated. "It's gettin' too cold to keep meeting back here."

"Here" is the back patio, as it has been for most of your other meetings with the real Gary Chen. That cold front has dropped the temperatures, and instead of lounging in the sun in shorts and a skimpy top, she is bundled up against the cold, hugging herself and trying to keep her teeth from chattering. The collar of her jacket is up around her cheeks, she has a scarf over her chin, and the giant, fluffy ski cap is pulled down almost to her eyes.
