"You wanna meet at the fucking library or something, make it a study date?" she snarls. You were late replying to her text, and maybe that's why she's in a bad mood. "So what's this bullshit about Andrea spraining her ankle?"

"She twisted it or something. Not enough to put her in a cast, but enough that she shouldn't be stressing it. She's gonna have to miss the cheerleader tryouts."

"They're tomorrow!"

"Like I say, she's gonna have to miss."

"This is bullshit, Prescott! I told you I wanted her -- "

"Life sucks, man. Deal with it. You're gettin' it good everywhere else."

"Am I?"

"You look good from where I'm sitting. Except you got too many clothes on. Let's go inside and let me warm you up a little."

She offered you something along those lines the other day, but you turned her down, and she's obviously not in the mood to renew the offer. "Did Dane at least get you the stuff yesterday?"

"Yeah, I got it, and I distributed it. All except the stuff for Trantham. I'm changing things up there."

"How? Why?" She looks alarmed.

"Trantham was a loser. I found you a new contact, one who can grow things at Eastman. I gotta get his stuff to him, was gonna do that but then you yanked me out here."

"Is he the one buying all that extra stuff you paid for?"

"Some of it. Rest of it's like you asked me to do, give more to the three fucketeers, find a sales point down in the junior class, that stuff."

"So give me numbers and names."

"Why?" You grin hard. "You thinkin' of comin' out of retirement, takin' the business back? I'll happily take over from Chelsea for you."

She leans forward with a glare. "I don't trust you, Prescott. I don't trust you not to fuck things up." She jabs a finger at you. "You've got nothing up there I didn't have, but when you add your brain to mine I figure you're lopping off at least half my smarts."
1112111311222221222222122121211212112211.title: Girls Behaving Badly

choices: Tell him the ultimatum still stands
1112111311222221222222122121211212112211.content:
You get to your feet. You don't even decide to, you just instinctively stand up.

"You put me in charge of your life, motherfucker, so I'm in charge of it now. Anything I screw up, I pay the price. Anything I get right, I get the reward."

And suddenly it's like the top has come off a well-shaken bottle of cola. Sudsy anger fizzes up the sides and back of your neck.

"You wanted to be Chelsea Cooper, asshole, you wanted to suck basketball cock and finger cheerleader pussy, and you wanted to stick me with all the shit that you wanted to get away from. Well, now you're Chelsea Cooper, and that means you got fuck all to say or do with Gary Chen. You wanna manage your old life? You take it back for yourself, you cunt, you do it yourself in your own fucking skin. Otherwise, you sit back, enjoy the ride Seth Javits is giving you, and stay the fuck out of my way."

"You don't -- !"

You bend over her. "I mean it, you dripping whore. You stick to what you got now, and forget you ever were this poor son of a bitch -- " You point to your face. "Or you take it back and keep it. Because I'm sick of you yanking my chain like I'm your fucking dog. I didn't ask to be you, not permanently, but I'll take a permanent change of address if that's what it takes to get you to fuck off and leave me alone."

You straighten back up again as she turns a deep scarlet. "You think you got a hold over me, but you don't," you continue. "But I'll play fair with you this far. I'll give you till midnight to decide. Do you ever wanna be yourself again? If you do, text me, and I'll give it all back to you tomorrow. Otherwise, don't count on ever getting your life back again. And if you don't want it back, if you want to stay as Chelsea Cooper for the rest of your life, letting squirmy wormies crawl up your cunny and squirting out babies like they was cookies at the Keebler factory, then just get the fuck out of my face, get the fuck off my back, and get the fuck outta my life!"

You fix her with one hard glare, then stalk off. "Outta my way, shit head," you mutter at Jordan when he tries stopping you at the sliding glass door. For a moment you think he's going to try grabbing you, but he lets you leave unmolested.

* * * * *

You're still driving down the road when the shakes -- which you knew were coming -- finally hit you, and you pull into the parking lot of a strip center for a smoke.

Fuck. You just gave Gary Chen an ultimatum. And whichever choice he makes, you are screwed.

Because if he takes his life back, you make yourself completely vulnerable to him and his friends.

And if he doesn't, you'll be stuck being Gary Chen.

You get out and lean against the Jeep and suck hard on the cigarette, filling your lungs with stinging -- but soothing -- tobacco smoke, and stare at the asphalt.

Actually, you reflect, you were screwed from the moment you put Chen's mask on. Or from the moment he figured out what was going on. You've just been delaying things, hoping that something would turn up or change so that you could get out of this mess without having to face the consequences of what you've done. And even now it's not like you're going to have to pay a price. Not unless you want to.

Because that rockbot paste means you don't ever have to face Chen's vengeance, not if you don't want to, and you don't have to stick it out as him if you ever get fed up with it. If Chen says he wants his life back, you can always just replace him with a Chenbot, and can continue to manage his affairs remotely, maybe even from under the mask of Chelsea Cooper, if that's what you decide you want.

And if he decides to let you keep playing Gary Chen? You can do like you did with Rennerhoff last night, and find yourself a nicer situation while letting the Chenbot keep things going in its mindlessly vicious way.

It's a gambit that occurred to you a long time ago -- one even Caleb has been mildly lobbying for -- and you've not faced it because you've not wanted to face it. The past few weeks has just been one desperate dodge away from the only solution that makes sense.

But now you've forced the issue, and you're going to have to figure out what to do about Chen when he texts you a decision.

You finish the cigarette and finish another one without really deciding for yourself what you're going to do. You're just trying to steady yourself so that you won't get into a wreck while driving.

* * * * *

But you can't delay the day for long. You've got errands to run, and after burning through half of it, you toss a third cigarette to the curb and get back in the Jeep.

The first errand takes you to the Warehouse, where you meet the Karterbot and give it a supply of weed. It has no money for you, obviously, but the thing is completely under your control and will simply pass on the cash it collects from selling.

Not that you're short on cash at the moment. The strongbox under the chassis is heavy with money from the bulk sales the Chenbot made last night and this morning. You hardly lighten it any when you swing by some supply stores to get the makings for more masks. You have no idea what you'll do with them, but it's best to have them on hand so you'll be prepared for whatever Chen might throw at you.

At the elementary school you get three rough masks made, and one of them polished, before you have to break off and return home for supper. Grandfather has been very docile since you used that spell on him, and maybe that's contributed to a better atmosphere at home, for Mrs. Chen hasn't been riding on your homework and viola practice. But she comes down on you hard, just the way she used to, when dinner is over, and so you have to buckle down to do school work in the dining room instead of returning to the elementary school.

At eight o'clock your cell dings with a text. It's from Chelsea. Your chest tightens as you open it to read it.

She wants to meet at the municipal library to talk.

"I'm meeting some guys at the library to study," you tell Mrs. Chen as you pull on a jacket.

"No you're not, you're working here." Her mouth is very set.

"I have to, it's for a project!"

"Your project is here! You'll goof off if you go out!"

"I won't goof off! It's a serious project!"

"Who are you meeting?" Her eye goes to the phone in your hand.

No use lying about it, she'll just grab it from you and check. "This girl, Chelsea Cooper."

"So it's not studying! Sit down, work!"

"It is studying! I can't goof off with her! She's the head cheerleader, and her boyfriend would kill me if we were goofing off!"

"Head cheerleader? Like that girl Lin is a cheerleader?"

"Uh ... Yeah."

She studies you closely. "You talk to her about Lin?"

"I could."

She nods gravely. "Then talk to her about Lin. And study!"

"I will! Thanks!" You grab your stuff and run out the door before she can change her mind.

* * * * *

You've almost completed a circuit of the library before deciding that you've beaten Chelsea there, and then you get another text from her asking where you are. After a bit of texting cross-talk, she directs you to a distant corner in one of the wings behind lots and lots of stacks. There are two overstuffed chairs, and she's curled up in one of them. She looks at you gravely as you approach. "I didn't like the way you talked to me this afternoon," she says.

"Fuck you, I'm not taking it back. What's your answer?"

Her jaw tightens, but she keeps her composure. "I've decided I want you to give it to Kirkham," she says.

Her words are like a slap in the face. "Give what to him?" For a moment you think she's telling you to give Kirkham a blow job.

"My life. That mask you're wearing. Give it to him and let him take it over." She leans forward. "I want you to explain it all to him, tell him about these masks and what you and I have been doing. Then I want you to give him that mask so he can take over and start playing me. I'm firing you, Prescott," she says in a hard voice. "I'm hiring Kirkham in your place."

This is ridiculous in so many ways. You snort. "Why would Kirkham want to be you?"

"He would," she says confidently. "He's always been jealous of me. And when you tell him how much weed he'd be dealing, how much money he'd be pulling in, he'll grab it. He's always wanted my business, he's just too much of a stubborn prick to say so."

You have your doubts, but you ask a more pertinent question. "And what happens to me?"

"You can be my new boyfriend. Steve Patterson."
1112111311222221222222122121211212112211.title: The Ultimatum

1112111311222221222222122121211212112211.content:
You don't trust Chen, not for an instant. But there's nothing wrong with negotiating with him, pushing him, tricking him into revealing his intentions.

"So are you planning to stick it out as Chelsea?" you ask with a leering grin. "What do you think Kirkham's going to say about that?"

"He'll understand," Chelsea stammers. "He'll bust my balls about it -- " She stops in the middle of the inapposite image. "But fuck 'im if he does. I'll make him see it my way."

It might be unintentional, but she pushes her breasts out slightly.

"Uh huh. And he's gonna see things your way even after he catches you fucking Steve Patterson?" You snicker. "You know what he's going to say about that, especially if he knows it's really me you're sucking off."

"He won't give me any shit," Chelsea snarls, but the color of her face tells you even she doesn't believe it. "Look, I'm willing to put up with it, if that's what it takes to get you to do this. I'm really paying you a high price."

You laugh. "You sure as fuck are. And where does Patterson go? The real one. I know where I'm putting myself after I'm him." You click your tongue lasciviously.

"You turn him into Kirkham, right? Isn't that what you did with the fuckwits, you just switched their faces with -- "

"I can't really do that here." Well, you could, but you're not going to tell Chen about the rockbot paste. "See, the guys I switched the fuckwits with, they didn't kick at it. Like Chris Trantham, he wakes up in Tanner Evans's stupid face, but he's such a dipshit he doesn't know what to do with it, so he just plays along. But I don't see Patterson playing along with a body swap. I mean, you didn't, right? And once Patterson figures out there's body swapping going on, he'll do a little math -- he's taking Calculus, and this ain't even half as hard as calculus -- and figure out what happened to Gordon, that there was a body swap there. And if he talks to the guy who looks like Gordon, the way you did, he'll track it all back to whoever is wearing Gary Chen's face."

Chelsea is now looking very pinched, but you press on. "And he'll figure out where you are, too. 'Cos when he talks to Gordon he'll find Dane Matthias, and then he'll remember how screwy Dane Matthias has been acting lately. He'll go talk to that Dane, and when he does he'll find Chelsea Cooper."

"Well, what can he do about it?" Chelsea says shrilly.

"You did something about it."

Her lips compress so hard they disappear. "Isn't there something in your bag of magic tricks you can use to bluff him?"

"Nope. We can't use Patterson."

"Then think of someone else, someone who won't cause any problems if you swap 'em with Kirkham."

You lean back. "Who? It's gotta be someone big and mean, 'cos I ain't moving into any face where you can fuck me up. And anyone like that is gonna cause problems once he's inside Kirkham. And anyone who's be safe inside Kirkham wouldn't be safe for me."

"So change schools. If I scare you that much," she sneers, "maybe you oughta run."

"Look, I'm not going with your idea. I'm sticking to what I said before. Either you take your life back tonight, or you leave it with me forever." You check the time. "You got a little less than three hours to make up your mind."

She's shaking when you leave her. Probably she's been trembling all through your conversation, but by the time you get up from the table she can't hide it anymore.

* * * * *

You drop in at a nearby McDonald's and nurse a cheap coffee while waiting for a text from Chelsea. After an hour you send her one of your own. if i don't hear from u, means u stay that way.

Eleven o'clock comes, and there's no answer. You go home, to noodle around on some more homework. When midnight passes, and then a twelve-fifteen goes by, you decide that her answer has been given.

Gary Chen has traded his life permanently for that of Chelsea Cooper.

* * * * *

But that doesn't mean you've permanently traded your life for that of Gary Chen. Your excursions into the identities of Adam Karter and Jamie Rennerhoff have proven that you can take time off from being Chen, and there's no reason you can't manage Chen's operations from another identity, the way Chen himself has tried managing you from the identity of Chelsea Cooper. You're not keen on turning yourself into either Karter or Rennerhoff on a full-time basis, and would like to stay close to the Chenbot for the same reason that Chen wants to stay close to you: to make sure it doesn't make any blunders while operating on its own.

You could puzzle it out by yourself, but you'd feel better talking to someone. Besides, you want to see how much of a change there's really been in Caleb. So you text him, and he agrees to meet you at the elementary school late Sunday morning.

* * * * *

He's swaggering it just as hard as when you saw him last, driving his truck in fast and skidding to a halt just short of where you're standing. "Hola," he shouts as he hops out. "Or whatever guys like you say."

You're too subdued to take offense. "Haven't seen you in a while," you say.

"You just saw me the other day."

"I mean I haven't seen you, uh, Caleb." It feels very strange to be using his real first name. "But I guess you haven't much of me, either."

He shrugs. "Well, it's been awhile since we took these things off."

You decide it's best to be direct. "Look, I gotta talk to you about Chen. He pulled me out for a talk yesterday, and he scared me a little with what he said."

"Only a little?"

That lights a small flame in you. "I can handle him, fucker. I think I did. But I don't trust him not to make more trouble."

You tell it to him, all of it. He is very sober by the time you're done.

"Yeah, I don't blame you for being worried," he says. "He's gonna think up some kind of twist to hit you with." Caleb cocks his head. "But can't you figure out what he's going to do? You've got his head."

"That's no good, I can think up too many things for him that he could do. And he's more desperate than me, and he's got Chelsea's brain too. She's a schemer herself."

"True." His brow furrows. "This isn't going to fuck me up with her, is it?"

"Is that all you can think about, how it's going to screw up your fuck schedule? Worst that'll happen is she'll stop putting out.

"As if that's not bad enough."

You roll your eyes. "What I'm thinking about is moving someplace else," you say. "I figure I can leave the business to that Chenbot and hide somewhere Chen can't find me. He can't do anything to the 'bot, you know, and if he doesn't know where I am -- "

"Isn't the simplest thing to just turn Chen into a bot?" Caleb says. "Shit, we've talked about this before. Either way you're talking about doing to someone what we did to Javits. You're talking about turning someone into a 'bot while you take over their life. So who deserves that kind of thing more, Chen or some poor dumb asshole?"

That's a point. You've been resisting doing that, at least to take care of yourself. But you've already done it to bunch of other people, because Chen's business has forced you to. Andrea Varnsworth, Jelena Petrovich, Sienna Goldman, Fatima Zahedi, and Seth Javits. Not to mention the people who are walking around in a very confused and disoriented state: Gordon Black, Chelsea Cooper, Dane Matthias, Chris Trantham, and Kevin Hall.

The only person you're not protecting is yourself, and you're hurting lots of other people by not cutting things off at the root, with Chen.

You see all that, now that Caleb has laid it out. It would be simplicity itself to set things right. Get Chelsea's mask off Chen, and get the Chenbot mask onto him. Pull the masks off all the other people. Resume your life as Will Prescott. The Chenbot would secretly take your orders, and you could even collect those extra profits it would be making --

Ah. There's the temptation. To keep control of Chen's operations through the Chenbot. That would mean keeping most of the people where they are, since they are serving him. Oh, Chelsea and Gordon and Dane could go back to normal. But all the other changes you've made, all the other agents you've salted around --

"But if you're not going to listen to me," Caleb says, "why don't you turn yourself into Patterson, just without telling Chen about it?" His voice jerks you back to attention. He repeats the question when you ask him to. "That's where you could hide out, if you're too scared to actually do something to Chen. Put the Chenbot in play, turn yourself into Patterson. You could keep an eye on Chelsea from there, same as I could. And we'd be hanging out again. Hyah! Unh!" He punches you lightly in the shoulder a couple of times, and goes into a boxer's stance. "Hell, we could switch off, take turns being Stevie and Seth."

That's a variation on the idea, though one that wouldn't turn Chen into a 'bot of himself. But it would still leave him free.

And as you think of Chen acting outside your control, a sense of dread starts to oppress you. He got into the body of Chelsea Cooper by wrong-footing you in the first place, by finding some unlikely allies. He could do it again.

In fact, he's already mentioned the name of one person he could reach out to: David Kirkham. Maybe you could anticipate him by taking Kirkham's place.
1112111311222221222222122121211212112211.title: A Permanent Change of Footing

choices: Put everything back as best you can
1112111311222221222222122121211212112211.content:
"No, the simplest thing is to get rid of Chen," you say. "You were right the first time, you were right a long time ago. Just turn him into a Chenbot. That takes care of everything."

Caleb shrugs. "Sounds good to me. But you can still turn yourself into Patterson."

"No, we're gonna put everything back to the way it was." It's a hard thing to say, and the wish to keep Chen's occult operations as they are claws at your guts.

Maybe it claws at Caleb's too. "Does that include me? Are you telling me I have to give up this?" He flings his arms out, showing you the awesomeness that it is being Seth Javits and having Chelsea Cooper as a girlfriend.
1112111311222221222222122121211212112211.title: A Fork Into the Future

choices: Return to being yourself, Gordon, or Dane
Use someone else as a vacation spot
1112111311222221222222122121211212112211.content:
And that's where you find the excuse not to make any more change-backs than the one. If you're going to indulge Caleb by letting him stay as Seth Javits, then you can leave everything else more or less as it is now.

"Are you saying you wanna stay as Seth Javits forever?"

"Well, maybe," he says without hesitation. "It's pretty fucking sweet, you know. Seth's no dummy, and he's got moves I don't." He dances in place, his sneakers scuffing noisily in the turf, like he's playing basketball on a dance floor and marrying a three-point shot to an electro-shimmy. "Look, at least until the end of the year, okay? And then we can talk."

"Sure," you sigh.

"Sweet. And then you can be Patterson -- "

"Look, I know you're set on that, but this about letting people go back to normal, not screwing up more people."

"Oh." He looks surprised. "I thought it was about getting the upper hand, finally." His squints skeptically at you. "You know, I haven't been saying anything about it all since you got me into this position, 'cos I figured you having Chen's brain and all, you knew some reason we couldn't just pop a rockbot mask onto Chen. I didn't think it was because -- " He blinks hard. "So you wanna go back to being Will Prescott?"

"Why not?"

He snickers. "Dude."

You bridle. "What's wrong with being Will Prescott?"

He snickers again. "Nothing, I guess."

"He's not in any more trouble, is he? At school, I mean. Or at home."

"Fuck if I know. Point is -- Three-point point is -- " He laughs at his dumb attempted witticism. "Why settle for Prescott when you can be Patterson? Fuck, I'd trade up to that even from here."

"It's about putting people back, Caleb. Mostly."

He sobers up without losing the skepticism. "So Gordon goes back to being Gordon?"

"Sure."

"Fuck. That means I gotta fight with him up in the loft, 'cos he's definitely gonna want to come back up there. You gonna let Chelsea go too?"

"We'd have to, if we turn Chen into a 'bot."

"Great. And then she busts up with me and goes back to Gordon." His skeptical expression deepens into a glower. "You might as well put me back to normal, then."

You relent. Except for turning Chen into a 'bot, you'll leave everything as it already is, at least until the end of the school year. "So where do I go? If we turn Chen into a 'bot of himself -- "

"You turn into Patterson."

"Stop that. I'm not interested. Are you really that queer for him?"

"Then turn him into a Chelseabot," Caleb says. "That'd suit me. Turn Chen into a Chelseabot the way you turned Javits into a Caleb-bot. Then I don't have to worry about her breaking up with me, and I can make her suck me off any time I want."

"Doesn't answer the question, where do I go?"

"Stay the way you are now? Alright, if you don't want to make any more masks -- " He twists his toe into the grass and sucks in his cheeks. "Um. I guess one thing you could do is turn Chen into a Chenbot, and then you could slip into the Chelsea mask. I mean," he adds hurriedly, "Chen's having fun at it, so -- "

"Oh, Jesus! He has to have fun at it, you dumbass! Or at least he has to pretend he's having fun at it, since he's thinks you don't know what's going on!"

"Alright," Caleb grumbles. "I knew you wouldn't like it."

Except you kind of do. Chelsea is certainly very sexy, and Chen has already shown you by example how much fun it could be to play the part of Chelsea Cooper. If it weren't for Caleb and the leering satisfaction he would take in it, you might do it.

Well, you might still do it, if you can't think of an alternative.

The alternatives, unfortunately, consist mostly of people you're not keen on impersonating: Tanner Evans. George Mendoza. Joe Thomason. Jamie Rennerhoff. Joshua Call. Jeff Spencer.

It might be possible to impersonate the people that the real assholes are impersonating, but that would involve several layers of impersonation. Could you really pretend to be Joshua Call-pretending-to-be-Jelena-Petrovich?

There is one other possibility, though. You could turn Chelsea into a Chelseabot, and leave Chen under that mask. But you could take one of your original victims -- Gordon or Dane or Chelsea -- and turn them into the Chenbot. Then you could take over the position they currently hold: Turn yourself into Dane or Gordon, or go back to being yourself.

So there it is. You can turn yourself back into yourself, or into Dane or Gordon; you can take over as Chelsea Cooper; or you can make another victim and turn that person into the Chenbot while you take over that person's life.
1112111311222221222222122121211212112211.title: A Spider in His Web

1112111311222221222222122121211212112211.content:
You rub your forehead. All this thinking is getting to be a strain. You need to make a decision. And the easiest decision is to turn Chelsea into a Chelseabot for Caleb, and for you to push Gordon or Chelsea or Dane under the mask of the Chenbot while you take over one of the roles they are currently. So those three choices are:

Gordon Black, who has fallen completely out of the high school firmament, losing his girlfriend and his place on the basketball squad and attaching himself to a bunch of potheads. From what you hear, the guy who's playing Gordon is having fun, but is there really any point in being Gordon if you're not picking up what he used to have?

Then there's Dane, who is still hanging out with Dane's friends but has cleaned up his act and is studying hard. That's not your idea of fun, but you could always take him back to the way he was before. And he would be the ideal place from which to manage the Chenbot's affairs. No one would think twice about seeing Dane with Chen, and no one would suspect that genial Dane is the mastermind of a giant dope ring.

And finally there's yourself: Will Prescott. God only knows how Gordon has screwed up your life at home. Dare you return to find out?
1112111311222221222222122121211212112211.title: The Original Three

1112111311222221222222122121211212112211.content:
You rub your forehead. All this thinking is getting to be a strain. "Ah, fuckit," you exclaim. "I'll think of something later. Let's just go take care of Chelsea before it's too late." You look up at Caleb. "Text her, get her to meet you up in the fuck room. You remember how to get masks off?"

"Sure, you grab their face and pull."

"What are the magic words?"

"Uh ... "

You laugh mirthlessly, and tell them to him again. "I'll be waiting down in the school parking lot for when you've got the mask off him. Call me and I'll run in so we can finish it."

"You don't trust me to do it right?"

"I wanna be there anyway for when it's done. Sweet, sweet vengeance."

Caleb sets it up and leaves. You go down into the basement to pick up the rockbot paste -- a container containing Caleb's hair so the Chelseabot will obey him. Your eye falls on the unfinished masks you started to put together.

You shiver when you think that you don't have to have a single vacation spot away from the Chenbot. You could give yourself a couple.

* * * * *

The end of your apprenticeship to Gary Chen turns out to be anticlimactic. You park on a side street near the school so Chen won't see your Jeep. The wait is dull, and after thirty minutes you get a ping telling you that Chelsea has arrived for a Sunday afternoon date with her boyfriend. You're just pulling into the parking lot beside the gym when you get another text telling you the mask is off your old foe.

You tear into the gym and across the floor and up the stairs. Caleb pulls you inside the loft. Splayed out on the mat is an unconscious Gary Chen, looking very silly squeezed into Chelsea Cooper's clothes. "Gross, isn't it?" Caleb says. He hands you a mask.

You drop to the floor, peel open up the rockbot paste, and smear its insides. You blow it dry. It's only about then that your heart begins to race, as you bring things to a conclusion. You slide the mask onto Chen's face, and Chelsea's appears in its place. Her eyes flutter open.

There is some slight confusion and some talk after that. From the way the Chelseabot is talking, it remembers everything that Chen was doing while pretending to be Chelsea, and the 'bot seems inclined to keep pushing you around. You explain to Caleb how he can get it to change its mind and attitude, and after he's talked to it a bit, it settles down to being his faithful girlfriend and no more than that. Still, the thing grazes you with a quick glance, and inside that glance you think you can see a desire to keep managing your affairs. But --

"So, that's it, then," you say, hopping back a couple of feet to put your back against the door.

"That's it," says Caleb quietly. "What are you going to do now?"

You don't know. You're still unsettled, and are more unsettled the more you look at Chelsea. It feels like you've finished running a long and grueling race, but the moment of triumph has eluded you somehow. The moment isn't really a victory for you; it's only a complete defeat for Gary Chen.

"I dunno. I won't stick around, though. You don't want me here, and it'd be funny if anyone caught me up here. Besides, you'll want to teach your new toy how to do all those things she wouldn't ever do for you before."

Caleb, you notice, doesn't argue. "So, see you in school tomorrow?"

"Sure." You shift from foot to foot worriedly, then yank the door open and leave without another word. Your feet are heavy but your head is light as you take the stairs down two and three at time, and the sound of your boots hitting the floor echoes loudly around the gym. There's a fast, hard flutter in your chest, and you sprint over to the side door and out, and you tear around the corner to your Jeep and jump in.

You ran all the way as though something were chasing you.

In a sense, something was. And the moment you fall into the seat, it catches you.

A raging boner.

You throw your head back, groan, and open your trousers to let it out. You have no idea why you've got such a boner, but you do.

It's not the first you've had from inside Gary Chen's body, and you've had some pretty big ones before, ones that hurt. But you're never had one like this. You're pretty sure it has nothing to do with what happened up there in that loft. You sure don't feel a strong desire to fuck the Chelseabot, and you feel no swelling trumpets of triumph at beating Chen, finally, for that's a victory you could have had a long time ago. As you grip the headrest with both hands and blink rapidly you try thinking it through. Why here, why now, in this way, are you having the worst kind of boner when your strongest emotion isn't lust but dread?

Dread. Yes, that's what you're feeling. A deep, aching dread that tears at your chest and blackens your thoughts. And thinking about that dread, turning your attention to it, is only making the erection worse. You grind the heel of a hand into an eye and suppress a whimper.

The answer comes when your cell tweets. It's a note from your mother. And in that very familiar thought -- mom wants me -- you solve the puzzle.

That's not "Mrs. Chen" anymore. That's your mother. Same as the phone and this Jeep and the clothes and the life are now yours. They're not just yours, because there is now no one else to claim them.

You turned Gary Chen into a Chelseabot.

And that means that you are now Chen Guangli. For there is no one else to be him.

And why is this giving you a boner?

Because Gary Chen hasn't jacked off since you took control. Because you've been too gingerly with the fantasies and the physiology, too grossed out to touch yourself except when pissing into a urinal.

But you're Chen now, and your body is telling you what it wants, and telling you that you and it belong to each other now, so there will be no excuses.

With an anguished and reluctant groan, you clasp the shaft with one hand, and with the other pull a tissue from the glove compartment. A minute later you've blown a powerful load into the Kleenex, and still your cock won't relax. Not until you've pushed it back into your trousers, started the Jeep, and driven around for twenty minutes does it subside.

But it's still growling.

* * * * *

"Alright, fuckers, lemme see your prospects." That's what you say to Mendoza, Evans, and Thomason when you meet them at Hochstetter Park a little after three.

"Tell that Canfield fuck you want to meet him after school Monday out by the portables." That what you tell Kevin Hall when you talk to him at the comic book store a little after four.

"All your friends know that Karter's handling your shit now?" you ask Chris Trantham when you see him a little before five o'clock at a Wendy's for a fast bite. "Good. I want you back at Westside on Tuesday after school, I'm giving you a new spot. You and Mendoza are gonna starting working some of them AP fucks for me."

Maybe you're just trying to distract yourself, but you're busting with energy and determination, and in those series of fast, hard meetings you send your minions racing out to complete your orders. It occurs to you that maybe Chen was right, maybe you weren't actually handling his affairs as well as you could have been. You were, after all, acting only as his agent, and a reluctant agent, and even though you were proud of the job you were doing, you really weren't doing it as hard as you could have been. You were wasting time indecisively with Mendoza and Evans, for instance.

That stops now. Chen's business is yours, and there's no reason to run it the way he would.

Including turning some rich bitches into creamy milch cows

But even though you are now firmly in charge of the life and business of Gary Chen, that doesn't mean you are planning to work as him 24/7. You can still do it from the side, switching in and out as need arises. What you need is a place where you can meet with and work beside him without raising eyebrows.

You're thinking about two such places as you carve runes into a strip of metal. One is a recruit that you thought about using a long time ago: Justin Roth. He's good with guys and good with girls, and you thought about using him as a "marketing agent" before giving Andrea Varnsworth to Thomason. Chen already hangs out with him sometimes, and you could draw a larger sales circle around him the way you've drawn one around Andrea and Karter. In fact, you could use Justin's face to pull those two circles together so that they overlap and reinforce each other as well as whatever circle you draw around Roth himself.

And the obvious way to do the latter is to order Andrea to take Justin into her bed. That's another reason to make him your new headquarters.

That would satisfy you, but it wouldn't satisfy Chen. For him there's another possibility, one that the Chenbot has noticed but you haven't -- not directly -- at the country club. A new girl who has gone to work there as a greeter. She's Asian, so she'd satisfy your mom, and she's definitely cute. She also goes to Eastman, so the connection would not set off dissonances at Westside. Her name is Dana Pak.
1112111311222221222222122121211212112211.title: The Old Boss, Same as the New Boss

choices: Prepare more masks for more switches
1112111311222221222222122121211212112211.content:
Your plans for a new hiding place are somewhat long-range. You've got plans for Monday afternoon after school, and you've got to get things set up for that.

So after you finish up the one mask you'll use at school tomorrow, you send out four texts and go home. There you get the last of your homework done and are in bed by two o'clock. You put yourself to sleep instantly, for tomorrow is going to be a busy day.

* * * * *

Mendoza -- the fake one -- is already on his way back to the portables when you run into him at around seven-thirty. "Yo, how much you sell this weekend?"

"Uh, a little?"

"How the fuck much is a 'little'? Give me a number."

"An eighth?"

"Jesus." How the fuck could you think a fake Mendoza could handle a greater sales load when the real Mendoza probably couldn't? Well, you've got a solution to that. "We need to do it in the farthest portable on the right," you say.

"What do we need to do?"

"You need to shut up, that's what you need to do."

The rest of the walk passes in silence, and you let fake-Mendoza follow you in. You point to a chair. "Sit," you tell him.

Slowly, he complies. You drop your backpack and step behind him. You throw one arm around his chest, and with your right hand grab his face. He struggles a little, but taking the mask off him is like twisting his neck: he slumps like a lump of cold meat when it comes off. With the mask in your hand, you bend around to look at him.

Yep, it's still Kevin Hall. Like it could be anyone else.

Well, it won't be Kevin Hall for long. Not here, and not later. You open up your bag and take out the rockbot paste.

A few minutes later George Mendoza is back on his feet, straightening out his clothes. The puzzled look on his face has vanished after you told him not to worry about what just happened. "Be back here after class, this same portable," you tell him. He nods, and you let him leave while you repack your supplies.

The fake Mendoza now has no memories of ever having been anyone else, and he will follow your orders perfectly. He will follow your orders for the rest of the day, when it's Kevin Hall under the mask, and he will follow your orders perfectly this evening and afterward, when it will be Douglas Canfield under it.

It's so much simpler this way, and ten minutes later it's simpler still when you've also turned Tanner Evans' duplicate into a perfect rockbot.

It's five minutes until first bell when you close the portable door behind you and head off toward the main building. You glance over at the sound of raucous voices: Joshua Call, Jeff Spencer, Jamie Rennerhoff, Nicholas Horner, Brophy Maddox, and Alex Massey are clustered nearby.

It's a relief to see Call and Spencer, as you weren't sure the 'bots would obey the texts you sent them last night.

* * * * *

The rest of the day passes without incident, and you hustle a few eighths onto some of your regulars. At lunch you go looking for Roth, but he's not around. Not that it matters. Today is not the day for making any kind of replacement, and if he's decided to skip it won't upset any of your plans.

But you would have liked to hang out with him.

At the start of seventh period you finally get a reply from the real Mendoza, telling you that he's set up that meeting with Canfield. You give him a curt thx and shoot a text to your goon squad to be on hand. In fact, you tell them to skip last period altogether so there won't be any chance of a fuck up.

But then Kirkham delays you as you're coming out of the orchestra room last period. "Got me an invite to a sexy part of town after school," he tells you.

"It's your mom's night at the strip club?"

"Go ahead and laugh, fucker. I was going to tell you where, see if you wanted to come along. But if you'd rather bang my mom in your fantasies 'cos your mom don't give it to you no more, well -- " He shrugs.

"So where is this sexy place?"

"Beg for it."

"No."

"Fuck you, I'll tell you anyway. Andrea Varnsworth's."

Your blood chills. You cover it with a sneer. "Now who's bragging about having a vivid fantasy life?"

"I shit you not. Fucking Jelena Petrovich as'd me out there."

That chill deepens. "She's just fucking with you. I heard about what happened at the Warehouse last week. What almost happened."

A slow flush creeps over Kirkham's face. "Yeah? Well, I thought she was fucking with me, right up until she backed me into a wall in the library and put her hand down my shorts and touched my cock and told me some of the things she wanted me to do to her with it."

"She's queer, didn't you hear that?"

"That's what makes it hot. But it don't matter whether she's fucking with me or wants me to fuck her. Either way, I'm heading over there at four, because either way I'm gonna make her bend backward and feel it."

You know Kirkham well enough that you don't doubt he means to.

"And I'm asking if you wanna tag along, maybe pick up anything that falls offa my plate."

You look over his head toward the portables. "I gotta meet my man Mendoza on some business now. But I know where Andrea parks herself. If I show up, I show up."

"Don't be too late. I might not leave anything for you."

* * * * *

So Kevin Hall and Douglas Canfield are already at the corner of the Agricultural Annex when you get out to that side of campus, and Call and Spencer and Rennerhoff and Mendoza and Evans are squatting by the portables. You pass Shep Tsosie heading away from them with a scowl. Probably your goons told him to go fuck himself somewhere else. You told them to keep the portables clear of witnesses.

You give them a glance as you pass, and they watch you alertly, but you say nothing but instead steer over to the other two. Neither looks very comfortable.

For Kevin Hall, it's probably very awkward to be hanging out with a dillweed like Douglas Canfield. One look at Canfield tells you he's obviously uncomfortable at standing around with Kevin Hall. You get a nice, long look at him as you approach.

He's a tall and gangly kid and would look a little bit like Caleb if viewed in dim light from a bad angle. Like Caleb, he's got a big nose and a nervous grin, and his tightly curled hair is cut short. He's wearing a plain, long-sleeve sweater and cargo shorts. In other words, he's just the kind of fuckfaced dweeb that Will Prescott would hang out with, except that this fuckface is probably too rich and socially connected to soil himself with Will Prescott's company.

He can't dodge Gary Chen's company, though.

"Yo," you call to Kevin, and jerk your chin at Canfield. "This him?"

"Yeah."

"Hey, thanks for coming," you tell Canfield, and put your hand up for a clasp. He fumbles at it, and you go out of your way to make him feel lame and uncool by doing some complicated things with your fingers. "You know who I am, right?"

"Uh, Kevin says your name is Gary."

"Right. Do Garner or Gardinhire ever talk about me at your clambakes?"

"Uh, clambakes?"

"Yeah. You know, those monthly socials at Kelsey Blankenship's house, where all you guys with clams get really, really baked."

It takes him a moment to catch on to what you're saying, and his face both tightens and pales. You instantly feel 80% more contempt for him. You glance back at the goons, holding their gaze until they get up and start to come over.

Back to Canfield. "Listen, I know all about those things," you say, getting close and lowering your voice. "I wanna talk to you about them. Because I wanna know where you and your friends are getting your shit. I think I can give you a better deal."

"I don't know anything about that," he stammers.

"No? You calling me a fucking liar when I say I know what goes on out there?"

"No. I -- We -- I just -- "

"Ayiyi blah fucking blah. Listen to this guy," you say to Hall. To Canfield: "I got me an office over there in that portable. Let's go inside and talk."

"I don't -- " But all his resistance collapses as five hard-faced goons cluster around him. You step out of the way, and let them escort him into the portable. They are all very silent. Even Rennerhoff doesn't giggle.

And once you're inside and the door is locked, you jerk your chin at Call. "Hold this fucker down. Spencer, you hold him by the hair, I need to get at his face," you add when Canfield starts to struggle. You take the blank mask from your bag and advance on the terrified Canfield. "Don't worry, this won't hurt a bit."

* * * * *

Your goons are totally silent as they lay the unconscious Douglas Canfield across the teacher's table. They are so silent that they make even you a little nervous, and you glance up to find Kevin Hall giving you a rather wide-eyed stare. You affect not to notice.

"Okay, I need you out of your clothes, same as I need you -- " You jab a finger at the guy who looks like George Mendoza " -- out of yours. Call, get Canfield out of his shit." Silently and efficiently, Joshua Call strips your latest victim of his clothes.

As he works, you ponder whether to accept Kirkham's invitation.
1112111311222221222222122121211212112211.title: Rites of Obedience

1112111311222221222222122121211212112211.content:
You're pretty certain you know what's waiting for Kirkham over at Andrea Varnsworth's place: ritual humiliation of the sort that they bragged about practicing on Kevin Winkler. You don't need to see that.

Besides, you've got stuff you need to do. You need to make a couple of more masks before tomorrow afternoon.

Right now, though, you've got to finish up the latest switch. Kevin Hall is naked, and he's looking more than a little freaked out at the silent company he's keeping.

"Alright, I don't need you guys anymore," you announce to the crowd still huddled around the unconscious Douglas Canfield. "Mendoza and Hall, you stay. The rest of you, out. But keep your phones charged 'cos I might need you tomorrow." The acknowledgements are silent, and a small crowd turns its back on you and shuffles out.

If anything, Hall now looks even more freaked out, and he keeps licking his lips and looking over at the Mendozabot. "Don't worry about him," you say. "In fact, don't worry about anything, and don't say anything. Both of you just lay down on the floor." The Mendozabot instantly complies, but Hall is slower to stretch out. You can tell he desperately wants to say something, but there's nothing intelligent he can offer, so you move out of his eye line and sit next to Canfield.

Jesus. It sure doesn't seem like it takes this long for a mask to copy someone when you're not in a hurry for it to get done. You check your phone, and find that only four minutes have gone by. You go onto the internet to waste some time while waiting.

Finally, the mask appears on Canfield's face, and you quickly seal it up with some of the regular old paste. You crouch next to Hall. "I'm gonna pull Hall's face off of you," you tell him. "It's gonna knock you out, and when you wake up you're gonna be Dougie Canfield. People call him 'Dougie', don't they?"

"I dunno. I don't call him anything."

"Well, he looks like a 'Dougie' kind of a douche. Get ready. Just close your eyes." He shuts them and you lay your fingers across his brow. It's like pulling a thick scab away, and then there's George Mendoza lolling on the floor. You drop Canfield's mask onto him, and when that great honker materializes you grab it and pinch it closed.

He flies up, gasping and batting feebly at you. "Quit that," you snap at him. "Get up and get dressed. We have to get out of here."

But he doesn't move, not instantly. "Oh, fuck," he gasps, and rubs his face hard. "Jesus." He curls up.

"I said move it!"

"I heard you! But fuck!" He groans and coughs and sputters. "I feel like -- Gah! Like shit!"

"You'll feel better when you're dressed and moving. I can't do anything else until you're prepped."

He grabs at some striped boxers. "Prepped for what?"

"Prepped for your new life, what the fuck do you think I mean? You got any of his memories yet?"

"Jesus! No," he adds after a frown.

"Well come on, get up, get the blood going, see if that starts stirring them up."

He gets shakily to his feet. Canfield is lanky naked, and the long shorts and long sweatshirt only accentuate his lankiness. But it doesn't take Mendoza long to get into the clothes, and less than a minute after you prodded him awake, the 2.0 version of Douglas Canfield is ready to go into circulation.

Except he has no idea where he circulates.

"What the fuck am I supposed to do until I remember where this dipshit lives?" he whines while plucking at his shirt.

"Christ, check your driver's license if you can't remember. If you don't want to face the parents, go to the library. Text 'em and tell 'em you're studying. I bet this asshole is all about studying. He probably jacks off to his calculus homework."

"It'd be nice if he had a girlfriend," he says grumpily. "At least I got laid when I was being Hall."

"Who with?"

"Do I have to tell you?"

"Yeah, on second thought I'd pay you not to tell me."

With the ice broken, he apparently finds it easier to ask those questions that had been bothering him earlier. "So I guess you showed this stuff to Josh Call and them."

"Yeah, but don't go bothering them with it. They won't have anything to say to you, and you don't got any business with them. I got a special thing going there."

"What kind of thing? They seemed -- " He trails off.

"They seemed normal to me," you tell him boldly. "You know, not talkative."

"Uh huh," he says, not looking convinced. "What about, uh, that guy?" He flicks a finger at Mendoza, who is staring at the ceiling with a patient, bored look.

"You don't have to worry about him either."

"But isn't that -- "

"I said, you don't have to worry about it. Don't go bothering George Mendoza or Kevin Hall. Especially Hall. That's gonna be the real asshole after this afternoon, and he's gonna be plenty confused and we don't want to give him any ideas about what happened to him."

His face tightens further. "How much is he going to remember?"

"Enough. Not enough to get us into any trouble, but enough that you don't want to give him anything to chew on. Now get out of here. Text me Wednesday. I'll be busy tomorrow with Evans. Gonna put him someplace new as well."

"Where?"

"You'll find out. And you'll like it." You clap on the shoulder that is half friendly and half "get the fuck out of here like I've been telling you."

After he's gone you finish up with the other two. You peel the Mendoza mask off Hall and place it on Canfield; the 'bot instantly wakes, and you tell it to get dressed. After it's done, you and it sneak out the portable so that Kevin Hall can wake up, back in his old body, by himself.

* * * * *

It's four-thirty by now, and when you swing by Panda Garden for your usual you get it to go, telling your aunt that you've got some errands to run before heading off to work. "You not eat while driving," she cautions you, and you assure her you won't. And you don't except for the finger food that can be sucked down with one hand while steering the Jeep with the other.

Your "errands" consist in getting as much homework done at the elementary school as you can before splitting into yourself and the Chenbot. You concentrate on getting the hard stuff -- the stuff that needs Chen's brain -- done first, and you get most all of it done before deciding it's time to make the split.

Before doing that, though, you call Seth Javits's number. "Yo, how busy are you?"

"Getting ready to eat."

"Any chance I could hook up with you at the school. The club house, I mean."

"Hook up, you say? Doing what exactly?" he sniggers.

"Keeping me company. You're not eating with Chelsea, are you?"

"No, but I was thinking of going over to her house afterward."

"Okay. But think of me, will you?"

"I don't want to think of your greasy face while going down on my girlfriend."

"I meant -- " But the line goes dead.

* * * * *

You're through with the homework and halfway through some runework for a brain band when you hear a car outside. Too late you douse the lights and sit in the dark with a sick stomach and a hammering heart as you hear a car door open and close. A moment later the basement door opens with a creak. "Will?" a voice calls. "Uh, Chen?"

Christ. "Jesus, Johansson, why didn't you tell me you were coming?" You put on the light again and try to get back the five years of life he scared out of you.

"I thought I did," he says as he comes down the stairs. "I called you and I got you and -- Why'd take the mask off?"

"I put it on the Chenbot and sent it in to work for me. Oh fuck, you talked to it, didn't you?"

"I guess." His furrowed brow relaxes. "Anyway, you -- I mean, it -- said I was welcome to do whatever the fuck I wanted to do." He rolls his eyes. "It sounded just like you've been talking recently, so I assumed it was you."

"Huh." Then you add a "What?" as you see the gleeful grin spreading on Seth Javits's horsey face.

"You're nude," he says. "You're fucking little naked jaybird, and I could haul you outside and -- "

"Caleb, don't you fucking dare!"

He hoots. "Oh, listen to you. Little Will Prescott! Oo! Don't you fucking dare, don't you fucking dare!"

"I mean it! Once I'm back in that mask -- "

"I'm just busting your balls, man." He hops up on a desk. "It's easier to stay in character these days than to fall out of it." He shudders all over, and flinches. "Christ," he says in a low voice. "I guess you probably heard what I did to Tilley the other day."

"No. What?"

He shudders again, even more elaborately. "Fuck. I can't even look at him anymore without seeing what Javits sees, and that makes it -- Yugghh! I feel awful if I do anything to him and it's like a horrible, unscratched itch if I don't."

"What's wrong with Tilley? What's Javits's problem with him?"

"I think you asked me that before, and I still don't know how to describe it. It's just his overall 'Tilleyness'. He needs to have it scrubbed off him, but he's like an onion, he's Tilley all the way through, and if I really went to work on him there'd be nothing left." He shudders again. "Anyway, I don't want to talk about it. What are you doing?"

"Making a mask for my new identity." Actually, it's to use on Kelsey Blankenship tomorrow, but you're not going to tell him that.

"Oh yeah? Who'd you pick?"
1112111311222221222222122121211212112211.title: Rites of Obedience, Part 2

choices: You go along with Kirkham's idea
You explain to Kirkham why it won't work
1112111311222221222222122121211212112211.content:
"Justin Roth. You know him?"

You look up when Caleb doesn't answer. He's frowning, with a distant look in his eye. "Vaguely," he says after a moment. "He's always wearing, like, tie-dyed t-shirts?"

"That doesn't pick him out, and I don't think he's always wearing them."

"But he's a hard-core pothead, right? That's the picture I'm getting." Caleb laughs. "Jesus, Will, you're planning to become your own best customer?"

"He doesn't look weird hanging out with Chen," you retort. "But he's not always hanging around with him. I'm picking him because, well -- " You can't give Caleb your real reason, which is that he'll help you cement down the party scene at Andrea's house and the Warehouse. "He's just really laid back and no one gives him shit, and he can hang out with Chen's crowd without, actually, being part of that crowd." You shrug lamely.

"Huh. Well, whatever. Would it look funny if he hung out with me?" Caleb stretches in a way that suggests he's trying to flex without being obvious about it.

"We could try it and see. I don't know when I can get ahold of him, but I'll let you know when I do."

"Do that, and do it in a hurry. We got the real Chen out of our lives, I'd like to shake the fake one."

You say nothing, because anything you said that would be true would piss Caleb off, and anything that would make him happy would be a lie.

* * * * *

The Chenbot is surly when it gets back to the basement, but it's got a sour smile on its face. You're feeling very wary when you put the mask back on.

"Fucker," you mutter to yourself after you've recollected the parallel life at the country club. It's not the Chenbot you're cussing -- though it deserves a cussing even when it's behaving well -- but Kirkham. He was bombarding your cell with texts and is demanding a meeting. He doesn't say what it's about, but you can guess. Well, he's the one who insisted on laying his dick out on Jelena Petrovich chopping block. No less than the Chenbot, he deserves some pain in his life. talk tmorow, you reply, and turn the phone off before you've even gotten in the Jeep.

And you'd forgotten completely about his texts when you pull into the parking lot the next morning, flipping off all other drivers who are trying to cut you off. Not until you see Kirkham leaning against the wall of the gym and staring into the lot do you remember how he was pestering you. He's got one foot up against the wall, and his arms are folded and his chin raised. His upper lip curls into a sneer as you approach. "Thought you were gonna try ducking me," he says in that soft rasp.

"Why the fuck would I duck you, I don't even know what you want."

His eyebrows go up. "You didn't hear from Call?"

"Josh Call? Why the fuck would I want to hear from -- "

Kirkham grabs your shoulder. You knock him away with a snarl. "Keep your fucking fingers to yourself."

"I wanna talk, but not here. Back at the portables."

"About what, asshole?"

He shows you his teeth. "About masks."

* * * * *

In the instant after the instant when you realize what he's talking about, you think maybe you can bluff him. But that's just reflex. After loosing a curse, you jerk your head at the far corner of the gym. "Alright, portables it is."

Neither of you speak on the long walk around the gym, past the athletic fields and the agricultural annex back to the portables. In a stupid anticipatory echo of what you're planning, Justin Roth is one of a small cluster of students already crouching near them. They don't pay any attention to you, and you don't pay any attention to them as you lead Kirkham around to one of the portables in the back. Once you're inside and got the door closed, you turn on Kirkham with a weary sigh. "So what happened yesterday?"

He doesn't answer right away, but discards one toothpick and pulls a fresh one out of his pocket. He sucks on it thoughtfully a minute, and looks you up and down. "So they weren't shitting me," he finally says.

"Who?"

"Those bitches. Jelena and Fatima. You really do know what they were talking about."

A faint ray of hope gleams about you. "Maybe I don't."

"Don't bullshit me. I said 'masks' and you jumped."

"Maybe I just -- "

"Fuck you. I thought it was some psycho talk when those cunts were telling me about masks, about how they were in disguise and shit. Thought it was really fucked up when they started talking about shit that only me and Call would know about, that we'd never'a told those bitches or anyone else. Fuck, I came real close to caving their faces in and burying 'em in Andrea's back garden, the things they were saying."

"Where was Andrea in all this?" You can hardly believe that Thomason was a party to it, and if he was you're going to do something about it.

"Inside. We were talking out back. Jesus, you're a sick fuck, man. Dumb as fuck, too."

"Get to the fucking point. Which is what?"

"My fucking point?" He jabs you in the shoulder with his forefinger. "You show me what you're doing and how to do it, that's the fucking point."

"No."

"Are you telling me to go fuck myself?"

"Yes."

His eyes are hidden behind those tinted shades, but it's not hard to imagine them blazing with anger. "This is bigger than you, Chen -- "

"It's bigger than you, asshole."

"You're not doing it right."

"Not doing what right?"

"Whatever you're doing with these things. Turning Call and Rennerhoff and them into girls just so you can sell a little more weed. That's what they told me is going on. Christ. Please tell me you're just telling those fucks a tiny, tiny bit of what you got going, because I wanna fucking puke when I think of what you're leaving on the table if that's all you're doing."

"Oh, Jesus." You roll your eyes. "Are you gonna try selling me on something different, on something I haven't thought of? Lemme guess, you wanna turn yourself into the president of the First National Bank, or into some dumbass politician, or some celebrity. You wanna turn yourself into some fat-assed fucker who actually has to work for his money."

Kirkham turns stony. "I'm not talking about work. I'm talking about finding someone where it isn't work -- "

"Yeah, I figured that's what a dumb fuck like you would think. 'Cos that's a hell of a lot harder than it sounds." Actually, you don't know that it is, but it seems like a good guess. "So what do you want? They told you what these things could do, and you instantly thought what? That you could have some fun being someone else, or that you could get a fucking job and make some fucking money by being someone else?"

"Why not both? Or the money and the fun without the work?"

"Why do you need money for fun? Look where I put Call and Rennerhoff. They're having fun. Were you at the Warehouse Friday? Did you see Jelena up on stage? Didn't it look like Call was having fun?"

"Everything's more fun with money. And you just gotta know how to monetize these things, and it ain't hard to come up with a way. It took me about five minutes."

"Really? So tell me."

Kirkham rolls the toothpick around contemptuously. "Get yourself a couple of hundred million by selling new bodies to rich old fucks," he says. "You think some of these eighty-year-old billionaires wouldn't give you almost everything they got just so they could start over again as a twenty-year-old kid?"

You blink. "I don't know any billionaires."

"So use a mask to find one. I know where we could start."

"Where?"

"A couple of places. There's that tech company north of town that Mr. Peters is always talking about. Proteus? The guy who owns that's a billionaire, and he's old. We get him interested, and he could connect us with a bazillion other billionaires."

"And make a hell of a lot of trouble for us. Jesus, you tried threatening me, getting me to tell you how it's done. You think those guys wouldn't do that to us?"

"Only 'cos you're such a dumbass you're still walking around under your own face." Kirkham's sneer combines contempt and incredulity. "You gave Andrea Varnsworth's body to fucking Thomason instead of keeping it for yourself? Fuck. If we go see that guy, we do it under some new faces so he can't trace it back to us."
1112111311222221222222122121211212112211.title: A Delayed Demolition of Most of Your Plans

1112111311222221222222122121211212112211.content:
You have to admit that it's an interesting idea Kirkham is proposing. You've been concentrating so hard on Chen's business that you've not really thought through any other possibilities.

Oh, it's occurred to you, as it has to Chen, that you could use masks to steal from people. It's even occurred to you, as it has to Josh Call, that you could use masks to steal the identities of rich or famous people. Neither appealed to you. One seemed risky, the other radical.

But Kirkham's idea is high reward without a radical change. Basically, he's just suggesting that you sell a mask -- or a pair of them -- for thousands or even millions of dollars. And he's seen a way to manage the risk.

"So what are you thinking?" Kirkham asks after you've been silent for a very long time.

"I'm thinking you should shut up so I can figure out a way to pull this off. I like it, but I'm worried about the technical part. Who do we disguise ourselves as?"

"What does it fucking matter? Some jerkass -- "

"No, it's got to be done careful. It can't be anyone in school."

"Why not?"

"Because we want disguises no one can trace back to us. We can't go as someone in school. Look, he won't take us seriously if we go see him looking like ourselves, and he won't take us seriously even if we're looking like, I dunno, Pruitt or Mansfield. We gotta be adults. Young guys, because we want him to think we use our own product, but adults. But we also don't want someone real, because if we wind up talking to some badass, he'll put his goons onto us, and if they beat the shit out of the real guys we're pretending to be, they might remember something that sends them to us. I wanna be absolutely covered, man."

Kirkham's eyebrows have gone up during this speech, and he replies with a broad shrug. "If you're such a chickenshit about it, we're not gonna get -- "

"I'm not a -- !" You savagely kick a desk. "Watch your fucking mouth, asshole! You think you're so goddamned smart, but you're a dumbass if you piss me off! I wanna do this, but I wanna do it right! Besides, I know how this shit works, what's possible, and you don't, so keep your fucking mouth shut while I think!"

Kirkham turns red, but clamps his mouth shut. You pace the room.

Finally, an idea comes to you. You snap your fingers excitedly, and outline it to him. Kirkham stays at school, but you skip the whole day, returning to the clubhouse to make the four masks you need to pull your plan off.

* * * * *

Robbery still played a role in the scheme, despite your intentions. It wasn't so much that Kirkham insisted as that he seized the chances. Every time he knocked a guy out, he rifled his wallet.

It only took you two days to put the disguises together, and it was Kirkham who carried the ball. He took two of the masks and spent all his free time roaming from one coffee shop to another and ambushing any handsome assholes who were sitting by themselves. It takes guts to do something like that in public, and you warned Kirkham against it, but he did it anyway, and damn if he wasn't right. When people see something strange, he told you, they look away. All he had to do was get right in a guy's face, smush the mask into him, then sit with him while they slept. Only once did he come close to being caught, and that was when one of his guy's friends showed up. Kirkham shooed him from the sleeping form with a shushing gesture.

So now you have two masks, each containing three or four faces that the masks melded into a composite before you sealed them -- faces that belong to no one. You also have a couple of hundred dollars that Kirkham lifted off the victims. You don't need it -- you've got more than enough cash yourself -- but you're glad to use it instead of dipping into your own stash.

It's Thursday now, after school. You're at Nirdlinger's, in the changing room. Kirkham wanted to go along, but you put him off with an excuse, and he's changing separately. You took some jeans and a shirt into the stall with you, put them on, then changed Chen's mask for one of the new composite faces. You've still got Chen's personality with you, though. You made a special trip to see Chelsea, and after stripping the mask off the sleeping Chen you copied his brain into a brain band. Now you can still have his memories, his instincts, and his guts when you meet Kirkham and your potential customer.

The face that meets you in the mirror when you step back into the Men's Department is swarthy -- Kirkham put mostly Hispanics into it, and an Arab. It's hairy all over, and the short, curly hair has an oily sheen. But it's handsome and buff and looks about twenty-five years old. It looks good in the suit and shoes that you quickly pick out and buy. You wish you had the money for a good watch, but content yourself with adding a thin gold chain to your wrist. You probably look completely out of place inside Chen's Jeep, but you won't be driving it to meet the customer anyway.

Kirkham is sitting in his car in the parking lot of the municipal airport when you arrive. He's also got a young, buff look. This one, though, is Anglo through and through, with curly, golden hair that covers his ears and the back of his neck. He's got a good smile, too, though it leers a little too crookedly when he turns it on you. He's still wearing shades, though he's exchanged his own tinted lenses for regular sunglasses. "You got the stuff?" he asks as he gets out.

You pat the satchel. It contains two masks. Neither has a brain band in it, and only one has an image sealed inside it: another composite, this time of three high school guys you hauled off to the portables for some occult abuse. You don't even know their names, only that they were pretty good looking. Chen's mask you have left in the Jeep.

You pick up a taxi at the terminal, and tell the guy to take you to Proteus Technologies, which is nearby. You have him wait with the meter running. Even if you're there for hours, you have the cash to pay for it.

You were surprised that you got an appointment with Curt Straussler, the billionaire owner of Proteus, on such relatively short notice. The gray-haired receptionist has you waiting only a few minutes in his outer office. You spend the time looking out the window. Proteus has a large campus of sleek, modern buildings slung about a central plaza and parking lot, and the office is tastefully decorated with fine furniture and some good art reproductions. The corner office you're summoned into is dominated by a huge executive desk at one end and a conference table at the other. Curt Straussler is seated behind the former. "Good afternoon, gentlemen. I can give you fifteen minutes," he says gruffly.

He's a stocky man, in his sixties or early seventies, with a round head, bald on top; intense, flashing blue eyes; and a white, bristling moustache. His mouth is grim, and it's hard not to imagine him as a tyrant and a bully. He said "fifteen minutes," but you probably have only fifteen seconds to impress him with your sales spiel.

You and Kirkham had talked about what to say. You'd written, honed, cut, polished, reduced, and buffed your pitch endlessly. But it never worked to your satisfaction. It sounded coy when you tried working up to a slow reveal, and it sounded crazy when you launched straight into it.

Finally you decided: Screw the pitch.

From your satchel you take the sealed mask. Without warning, you thrust it at the man's face. He loses at least sixty years of age, and falls face forward onto the desk.

* * * * *

"It's not an illusion, Mr. Straussler," you tell the boy who's staring bug-eyed at himself in a hand mirror. "If it was only an illusion, or hypnosis, your pants wouldn't have fallen down when you stood up." Vaguely, you hope that your smile doesn't look like a smirk.


The owner of Proteus Technologies is very white. He studies and rubs his fingers, then gingerly runs them through his thick brown hair. He runs his tongue around the inside of his mouth.

Then he goes to his office door, holding his pants up with one hand as he opens it. "Lydia," he says in a voice that cracks. He clears this throat. "I mean, Ms. Richards. Er. Did, uh, did Mr. Straussler step outside?" He hurriedly shuts the door after an inaudible reply. He leans against it, shutting his eyes.

"So you believe it?" you ask him.

His eyes snap open. They are very alert, with the same flashing intensity that his old eyes showed. He hurries back to his desk, his loafers flopping off his feet. He drops into his chair. "What do you want?" he asks in a low voice.

"To sell you that face," Kirkham says.

"Or another one, if you don't like that one. We'll also sell you a second, uh, item, that you can use so you don't lose your company. I mean, you can't really run Proteus while you're looking like that."

He frowns. "Explain."

So you do, giving a very vague description of a golem, and how he can use it to make a fake version of himself while he gets to inhabit a much younger body.

He nods when you're done. "I like it." He opens a drawer. You think he's taking out a checkbook. Instead, he takes out a small metal tube. There's a flash of light, and you know no more.
1112111311222221222222122121211212112211.title: The Making of Two Salesmen

choices: Sure, that's a great punishment for Call
No, Call's so dumb he should stay right there
1112111311222221222222122121211212112211.content:
What's the difference is between Gary Chen and David Kirkham? The difference is that Kirkham really would try pulling off this dumb-ass idea about selling young bodies to billionaires. Because that's Kirkham all over, all mouth and swagger. He'd go see this tech billionaire and show him the masks and make him an offer, and then he'd find out real fast how easy it is for a billionaire to fuck him over, because no one -- you're pretty sure -- gets to be a billionaire by making square deals with dipshit high school seniors.

But Gary Chen has both brain and guts. That's how he got to be dealing tens of thousands of dollars of weed every month without even Kirkham guessing what was going on. Gary Chen is too smart to go for Kirkham's idea.

But Gary Chen is also too smart to piss Kirkham off by swatting his idea down completely. That's the thing about swaggering morons like David Kirkham: there's no telling how much they can fuck other people up, even accidentally.

So you play it cool.

"Not a bad idea," you say. "Not that different from the kind of thing I was thinking of. But I wasn't going to go jumping in on it right away."

"Why not?"

"Because I don't need to. Why do I need to go out this afternoon to see this tech guy?"

"I didn't say 'this afternoon'," Kirkham sneers.

"Then why do I gotta tomorrow, or next week, or even next month? Fuck, I got all the time in the world to get on it if I want to. If I don't do it this year I can do it next year or the year after that. I can do it in ten years time. And I can still do it from Westside if I want to. If I want, I can turn myself into some fucking kindergarten snot-baby who's not gonna be a senior for another ten years. Christ, man, I got years to figure out how to use this shit to make a fucking fortune or run the fucking world. So I'm not gonna run out and get my dick sliced off just because I can't wait to cash a check for ten thousand dollars."

Contempt shows in Kirkham's eyes. "You fucking scared to try, Gary."

Kirkham never uses your English name except when he's being especially obnoxious. He and you both remember your middle school years, when the jock-bullies like Cameron Huber were calling you "Girlie Chen." But you ignore it now.

"I ain't scared. I'm figuring out how to take over a fucking high school first, and how to get rich off a fucking high school. I'm figuring out tactics and strategies, man, and putting me a team together. Then I'll start taking it a little wider. You already met the first couple on my team. Fucking dumbasses." You spit on the floor. "You and me are gonna fuck Jelena up on account of her telling you about this."

"You didn't want me to know?"

"Didn't want you to know right away," you admit with a shrug. "'Cos I knew you'd lose your shit. But it's not that I mind Call telling you. But if he's gonna tell you, who else is he gonna tell? Let's say you and me are partners now. What would you do if Call went and told -- I dunno -- Rich Austin about this?"

Kirkham mutters a long string of bad words, which you take as agreement with your point.

"Exactly. But don't get any ideas just because you found out about this. We're not partners, not exactly."

Kirkham shows his teeth. "Why not?"

"'Cos what I can do, it ain't a talent that can be shared, that's why not." You waggle a forefinger at him. "It's all in here, and if I touch you the wrong way -- " You jab at him, and he jumps back. You grin. "Yeah. See, the trouble is that I'll trust you a lot farther than I'll trust the fucketeers and the dipshits, 'cos you're my bro and you're fucking smart, man. But there's gotta be one boss, and -- " You waggle your finger again. "It's gotta be me."

He glowers, but seems to accede. "So what's this fucking brilliant long-range plan you're working on?" he asks. Off in the distance, the bell rings, but you both ignore it.

"No long-range plan. I told you, I'm just figuring things out, and right now I'm just looking for ways to improve my position. Today, for instance, I got plans to make me a new agent."

"Who?"

"You'll like it, it's right up there with your billionaire idea, only safe. Kelsey Blankenship."

Kirkham's lip goes up in a puzzled sneer. "What, you're gonna tell that rich skank about the masks?"

"No, I'm gonna turn one of my players into that rich skank. Evans has been doing some work for me somewhere else -- "

"Evans knows about this? What's he been doing for you?"


"Fuckin' yo' mama, that's what he's been doing. Shut up, man. But he's done with it, and today I was gonna try turning him into Kelsey, just like I turned Call into Jelena." You grin. "That rich skank is gonna pay out so much coin."

"Like, she's gonna give you her ATM card, her credit cards?"

"Not like that, though I bet I can get her to carry some of my expenses once it's Evans fingering her pussy at night. But she throws reefer parties at her house. Did you know that? God Almighty, that's even what she calls them. Reefer parties." You shake your head.

"How'd you find out?"

"Never mind how, I just found it out. I found out I've been selling a bunch of weed to Martin Gardinhire without knowing where it was going, and I found out that's where it was going, to him and Kelsey and those country club fucks." You laugh nastily "They're gonna start buying a lot more from me when it's Evans playing with her titties, and I got another guy who's gonna channel some more customers into her group so she'll be buying more on top of the more she's gonna be buying from me. Gonna be party central at her digs come Christmas time."

Despite his reflexive skepticism, Kirkham's interest looks piqued. "How much would you be selling to her?"

"Dunno, depends on how quick we get more partygoers into her place. I'm hoping to start selling ten or twelve ounces a month to her and her friends."

Kirkham's eyes are hidden -- as always -- behind his shades, but you see his eyebrows go up and his mouth slacken. "That's about what you already sell around the school, isn't it?"

"Right. And I got a few other pots going on the stove. Never mind where and how. I'm only telling you about Kelsey because she's up your alley." You cock your head. "Or you could be up hers. Could be your fingers, not Evans, up her alley, I mean." You mime fingering an imaginary pussy. "Could be you driving that BMW, spending her money, going to the country club and talking snot shop with her friends. I ain't wedded to it being Evans."

Kirkham stares at you and stares at you, and you return his stare. Then he ducks his head and makes a slow, thoughtful circuit of the portable. It's dim, but you can see his face working as he bites on his lips and sucks in his cheeks. He finishes the circuit, gives you a sidelong look, then makes another slow circuit.

And when he's back again to where he started, he looks up at you. "You got any other possibilities?"

"I told you, I'm taking it slow. I haven't thought farther than her."

"What about Chelsea Cooper?"

You were afraid of that. "She doesn't do anything for us. She's not rich, and she can't help my operation any."

"What about one of Kelsey's country club friends?"

"I don't need any more on that end, not unless Kelsey's puppeteer winds up with ideas where I could seed someone."

"So it's Kelsey, or I wait until you get another idea?"

"Well, there's nothing wrong with waiting, man. That's why I'm telling you all this, so you'll understand the virtue of patience."

He cusses softly. "And what do you do with Evans if I'm the one handling Kelsey?"

"He goes back to playing himself until we find another spot for him."
