Your double has come and gone, leaving the last of Prescott's supplies inside the mausoleum, but it's not yet seven o'clock when you rear back from your work with the blood draining from your face. "Fuck," you mutter to yourself. "It's not the soul. It's anima!"

* * * * *

need to see chelsea. Your thumbs tremble as you tap the text to Johansson. tonite. loft or library or somwhere. IMPORTANT!!!

A few minutes later your cell rings. It's Javits, and he sounds worried. "What's wrong?"

"Like I said in the fucking text, I need to see Chelsea. I need to get under her mask."

"Will? Is that you?"

His question knocks you back. It takes you a second to remember that Johansson will think you're really Prescott, and that he's also hearing Prescott's whiny voice since you're not wearing a mask. "Yeah, I had to switch out for a little while. Chen's at work you know. The Chenbot. Come on, don't fuck around, I need -- "

"What's it about? Is it about what happened the other night when you took her face off?"

"Yeah, I needed to check on something. Everything's okay, I think, but I need to double-check."

"What is it? What have you been doing?"

You almost throw the cell through the French doors, but you keep your temper. Neither Seth Javits nor Caleb Johansson would be impressed by any fit that Will Prescott could pitch. "I needed to get a copy of Chen's brain," you say, throttling the impatience in your voice. "I needed to know what he's been up to while he was still running Chelsea. But I lost that brain band I made the other night, and I need to -- "

"Jesus, Will! You can't just leave those things lying around!"

"It's not lying around, you fuck! Sorry, I didn't mean it that way." What would Prescott say, how would Prescott act? "Come on, Caleb, I think it's at Kirkham's house, and I can't go back there. It's, like, scary over there, you know that."

He snorts, but says he'll meet you at Hochstetter Park in twenty minutes.

That'll work for you, though it's now almost eleven, and you risk cutting it close getting back to the professor's house in time for a midnight curfew. Into your pocket you slip the band you'd made after figuring out what the sigil does, and race out the front door.

* * * * *

Your heart's pounding and your stomach heaving on the drive out to Hochstetter Park. A couple of times you think you're going to have to pull over to throw up.

Anima. That's what's in the metal band that Prescott put onto his forehead. A copy of the anima of Gary Chen. A spasm in your arm jerks the wheel and the Jeep rocks.

Okay, you're not totally sure that's what's going on. Even with Blackwell's brain it was slow work deciphering the sigil. You didn't finish, not before shoving yourself back from the table when the symbol for anima came into focus. And when another thirty minutes of translation work showed that the advanced sigil was using a "copy" technique that Blackwell had noticed in the basic sigil, then you had to face the ghastly possibility that you completely misread what's been going on over the last few days.

Anima is not a person's soul. It's just their mind, memories, personality. It's all the stuff that you thought was being copied into those "brain bands." But those basic bands were only copying mentalia, the shadow that anima leaves inside the physical body. That's why you can wear someone's mentalia, like Aubrey Blackwell's or David Kirkham's, and control it. It's just a reflection of the real thing.

But to put someone's anima onto yourself is to let their living mind into yours, and it comes with its own kind of body and a hammer blow of force. With anima, every thought and experience that poured into a person comes into focus and creates a sense of identity -- the conviction that all those things happened to me, and that I am that person they happened to.

When you put someone's anima into yourself, you don't put their soul in you. But you might as well have.

Because having their anima will convince you that you are that person.

In other words, you're not really Gary Chen. You're really Will Prescott, and you've only magically convinced yourself that you're Gary Chen.
1112111311222221222222122121211212112211.title: A Vigil with Sigils

choices: Stay to see what's going on
1112111311222221222222122121211212112211.content:
You race the car engine as you wait at a red light. "Come on, turn green," you mutter, and glance around to see if you can safely jack rabbit across the intersection. It is, after all, after eleven o'clock.

But you stay where you are, even though there's hardly any traffic about. Because it may be "Turn green" coming out of your mouth, but it sounds more like Stay red in your head.

You really don't want to get to Hochstetter Park to see what's waiting for you there.

It will be Caleb Johansson (disguised as Seth Javits) and Chelsea Cooper (a faked-up 'bot). And when you get out there you will take the mask off Chelsea and underneath will be --

Your throat tightens. Will it be yourself? Or will it be Gary Chen?

Because the horrible truth may be that those are not the same people.

For the last few days you've been convinced that you are really Gary Chen, that that fuck-faced little shit Will Prescott sucked your soul out of your body and accidentally inserted it into his in a way that let you take control. But in studying the sigil for that spell you've discovered a much more hideous possibility: that you are and always have been Will Prescott, and that the most recent spell has only struck you with a much more powerful copy of Gary Chen's brain, one so powerful that it has overridden your perso --

Your lips peel back in a ghastly rictus. No, it's not possible. You can't even say it to yourself. You can't say, I am really Will Prescott and I've only fooled myself into thinking I'm Gary Chen. That way lies madness.

Because you feel it with all the force of memory and experience -- the guts and sinews of eighteen years of life and habit. I am Gary Chen.

And almost nothing would horrify Gary Chen more than finding out that he's really Will Prescott.

You rub your forehead with your fingertips. You clench the wheel and twitch; a spasm almost jerks your leg off the brake. Will this fucking light never turn?

Oh. It has. You just didn't notice. You punch the accelerator, and the tires scream against the asphalt.

What'll happen if you take off that metal band, the one that copies Gary Chen's anima? Nothing, really, if your worst fears are correct. You'd just lose that illusion of being Chen. Prescott would probably like that, Prescott would probably yell with relief.

Well, fuck him. Because you're Gary Chen, and you're in control, and you're not going to give it up. It would be like killing yourself.

Except you're not Gary Chen. You're almost certain that you're not, which is why you are so filled with horror. Gary Chen -- the guy you think you are -- is still under that mask of Chelsea.

And if you let him out, which one of you would be Gary Chen?

You know the answer to that question too. It would be him. You're probably just a deluded doppelganger of him. Your guts pitch about like a small boat in a heavy sea.

There is some chance that you're wrong. That's why you made the new anima band and are taking it out to Hochstetter Park. If the new band only copies anima, then it will make a second anima copy of him. That would confirm what's going on. But if it doesn't copy him, if something else happens, then you can maybe let out a sigh of relief, because you will have misread the sigil.

* * * * *

Johansson, thank God, arrives only a few minutes later than you, so you have time to calm yourself and to remind yourself that you're supposed to be "Will Prescott" around him; but he's not so late that you get irritated and worked up all over again. It's dark, but you can recognize the almost ghostly image of Chelsea Cooper in the passenger side of Javits's truck.

You run over to her window, but Javits hops up out of the driver's side. "Hey man," he says. "You look, uh -- Ah, fuck it, I don't what you look like."

"That's great. Can you tell Chelsea to roll down her window? I need to get the mask off her."

"Yeah, what's this about?"

"It's about me having a curfew, you fuh -- I have a curfew, Caleb, I can't really dick around."

"Tch tch tch," he says. "We don't have a curfew," he boasts. "It's Friday night, you know. Alright," he adds in a grumble when you don't reply, and ducks back into the cab of the car. Chelsea's window goes down, and the girl with a blank and puzzled expression looks out at you. You grab her face.

She shrieks and grabs your wrists. But a word from Javits is enough to get her to release you. "You probably don't want to see this," you tell Johansson before pulling at her face. "It's, uh, Chen under there, you know."

"Yeah, I know. I guess I'll go chuck rocks at the winos until you're done. Don't take forever, man, and I better not find your cock anywhere near her -- " He snaps his fingers and grins. "Oh wait, I forgot. It'll be Gary Chen you're molesting if -- "

"Just take off if you're gonna take off." You've got the mask off by now, and Gary Chen's face -- your face -- gleams in the harsh glow of a street lamp.

You put the anima band to his forehead and turn away, feeling sick when it vanishes into him.

Maybe it will come back out, having copied nothing, because there's nothing to copy. You crouch by the side of the car, craving a cigarette. But Prescott doesn't smoke, and you're in Kirkham's clothes still anyway, and he doesn't smoke either. You pull at the grass and glare into the darkness and hate everything that has happened since you got mixed up with Prescott.

Correction: Since you went into that damn used bookstore and walked out with the --

Again, you can't finish the thought. It just doesn't ring true.

"Are you done yet?" Seth Javits's voice rings out of the darkness.

You check. "No!"

The angry bees in your stomach -- they're not nearly as gentle as butterflies -- buzz harder. You bang your fist on your knee. One way or another, you want it to be over. One way or another you want to settle the issue. One way or another --

You can't take it anymore, and get up and lean against the door so you can see the band when it finally falls out of the guy's forehead. From somewhere in the dark you hear a knocking sound, and glance over. Dimly you can make out Seth Javits. He's chucking rocks at the trees. You turn back to the guy wearing Chelsea's clothes. It makes you sick, to see yourself dressed up like that, and to know that it's not really you, but that it's person you wish you were.

And just as a particularly hard crack sounds behind you, the band appears and falls into the wearer's lap. You pick it up.

In Chinese characters, the name Chen Guangli burns redly.

* * * * *

You didn't even stop to talk to Johansson, you just slapped the mask back onto Chen's face and legged it back to the Jeep with a shouted "Got it, tell you about it later when I know something!" You get back to Blackwell's with maybe four minutes to spare, and lock yourself in one of the spare bedrooms just before the clocks downstairs start to chime midnight.

When the last chime fails, something downstairs falls with a sound like shattering glass.

You crouch in the middle of the room, staring hard at the door. You're sure the protective spells on the door frame will hold. But if they don't --

From down the hall faintly comes a scraping noise, as of long, hard claws dragging across the woodwork. You can almost see where they touch the walls as they approach your door. The floor outside groans and pops, as of something immensely heavy settling outside it. The knob rattles, and from the crack at the bottom you think you can hear breathing. Something raps sharply at the door.

You hold your breath.

Nothing more happens, and the thing moves away.

It's active all night, though, and even though you've the mind and stamina of Gary Chen -- manfully you have to face up to the dreadful fact that you're only imitating him, despite the unshakeable certainty that you are him -- you pass a very sleepless night.

Only when you jump awake at the sound of a giant anvil being dropped on the ceiling -- at least, that's what it sounds like -- and look over at the clock to see that it's three in the morning do you realize that things could be a lot worse. You fall back onto the mattress and force your eyes back together.

But the gwarcheidwad wouldn't be what it was if it didn't make a lot of noise on Halloween night.

* * * * *

Yes. Halloween. You've cause to marvel over the rapid shift that your fortunes took over the past twenty-four hours as you look blearily out the bedroom window into the pale pre-dawn. Twenty-four hours ago you were "Gary Chen," for all intents and purposes, but you were trapped inside Will Prescott's body and pretending to be your blood brother David Kirkham. That was bad enough. Now you know you're really Will Prescott and that you've only got a really powerful version of Gary Chen's mind lodged in your head. You've also got a copy of Professor Aubrey Blackwell's mind in there with you. And you just spent the night in Blackwell's house.

Jesus.

As the fingernail of the sun slips over the eastern horizon, you turn to the bedroom door. With the advent of dawn, it will be safe to leave the bedroom. You have a mask to put on, and --

From downstairs comes a terrific crash. Voices shout.

It sounds like a police raid.
1112111311222221222222122121211212112211.title: Will the Real Gary Chen Please Stand Up?

1112111311222221222222122121211212112211.content:
You're too shocked and surprised by the noises outside to do anything but stare dumbly at the bedroom door. It can't be the gwarcheidwad -- that occult monster that guards Blackwell's house -- making the noise, even though that's what it sounds like. That creature can only walk the grounds between midnight and dawn, and it is at its weakest in the early hours of the day. But the noises outside -- It's the sound of heavy, booted feet kicking in doors and overturning furniture.

You back up against the window and stare hard at the door as the footsteps sound closer, coming up the stairs. They pass your door. A voice shouts hoarsely, and from the hall comes a resounding blow and the cracking and splintering of wood. Someone bellows -- the Blackwell 'bot.

Whatever is going on, you want to be dressed for it. You're in Kirkham's t-shirt and socks, but you quickly pull on his trousers. They are a bad fit for you, but they're better than nothing.

But though dressed to meet someone, you're not prepared to meet them, not based on the noises you're hearing. Blackwell's voice abruptly cuts out, and something heavy thumps on the floor. More footsteps sound. A voice says something, and then another voice says something else. You brace backwards against the window as fast footsteps run down the hall. Doors bang open.

You lift the window behind you and throw a leg over it, grasp the sill in both hands, and drop a dozen feet. The ground jars your feet and ankles hard, but you roll away and are up and across the grounds to the wall in just a few seconds. You vault the outer barrier and drop into a crouch behind it. With your knees practically bumping into your chin, you hunch and run to the back of the house where you put Kirkham's car.

There's no time to find out what's going on. You make the car and turn the motor over and take off away from Blackwell's house without doing any kind of recon to see who was making noise inside it. Only after you're away do you make a long loop around so as to approach it again from another angle, as though you're just a guy driving around town who happens to be passing.

You expected squad cars out front, but there's only a white truck, one that looks a lot like Prescott's. That gives you momentary pause, until you see that it's got a different license plate. Also, a bumper sticker that says "I Brake for Meteors." You drive past, and continue up Orlando Road until you hit McDonalds, where you hang out with the early morning old folks while nursing a small coffee and thinking intently about things.

* * * * *

The first problem is that you have no good idea about what is going on back at Blackwell's house. You still have a copy of Blackwell's memories lodged in your brain, but although they can suggest some possibilities -- black magicians always have enemies and rivals -- you can't decisively connect any names to the events of the morning. All you can say for sure is that today would be the perfect day for someone to stage a raid on his villa. On Halloween the charms and protections he has woven about it are at their most potent. But in the early hours of the following morning -- All Hallows Day -- those protections practically vanish, and will only regain most of their strength with the coming of night. That suggests that whoever is staging that raid knows the house and knows Blackwell and has carefully planned this morning's events, timing the attack for the only day of the year when those protections can be breached. You doubt those charms will still be in place when the afternoon comes, though.

Without knowing who has invaded the house, you don't know when or how you can return to it. That wouldn't be a problem except that you left both the grimoire and David Kirkham's mask in the house. Kirkham can spend the day, and probably the night, out of his family's sight, but at some point he will have to reappear. Worse, you'll be walking around with Will Prescott's face while there's another Prescott -- the erstwhile Gordon Black -- in circulation. You've got other masks you could hide under, but that would just release people like Andrea Varnsworth or Adam Karter that you don't want back in circu --

Lucy, you gasp to yourself, and you must have said it out loud, for one of the old guys at the next table shoots you a glance.

Lucy Vredenburg. That's where you can hide for the next day. And it's a good spot to scope things out from.

* * * * *

"Hey Cindy," you casually greet her, and nod like she's a good friend that you just happened to run into. "I'm waiting for your sister."

"Uh huh." Your classmate -- the cheerleader who was going with Seth Javits before a couple of people had their faces switched -- looks puzzled but not contemptuous when she sees you leaning in at her front door. Cindy's a cunt, but she's not a cunt to the degree that Chelsea Cooper is, and she sometimes hangs out with girls who hang out with Prescott, so that even though Cindy has never smiled at Prescott she is at least used to his presence and doesn't automatically react with disgust. "How do you know Lucy?" she bluntly asks.

"I don't really," you admit, because that's what Lucy will tell her when she asks. "I'm dropping off a message from her boss."

"Lucy hasn't got a boss."

"Some university professor." You hold up the envelope containing the note that you quickly scrawled in Blackwell's hand after making a quick stop at the drugstore for writing supplies. "I'm doing work for him too, so he asked me to pass it along."

A look of incredulity passes over Cindy's face, but she hasn't time to question you further before her older sister, Lucy, appears behind her, having been summoned by Mrs. Vredenburg. She shows no trace of recognition when she looks at you. Not that she should. Lucy Vredenburg, in none of her manifestations, has ever had anything to do with Will Prescott.

"Hey, I have a letter for you, from Professor Blackwell," you tell her, and hand over the envelope. "He told me to wait for your answer."

Lucy looks even more puzzled than Cindy, and opens the envelope slowly. She catches her sister looking over her shoulder, and nudges her back rudely with an elbow. Cindy rolls her eyes and departs. You also take a few steps back and wait as Lucy leans against the doorway to read the note.

There wasn't time to seal the envelope, or to give it any of the rigmarole that you used to bluff the Blackwell-bot. You're counting on Lucy being obedient to the person who supposedly sent it, and force yourself not to tense as you wait.

She reads the note a couple of times, and looks at you with questions in her eyes. She hesitates over them, but doesn't ask them. "Let me get my purse," she says and ducks back into the house. You let out a breath.

At your suggestion you drive out separately to Gary Chen's place -- with her following in her SUV -- and you order the Chenbot to look after Kirkham's car until you get back. Then you hop into the SUV that Lucy is driving, and give her directions to the elementary school in Acheson. "What's going on?" she asks.

"Beats me," you lie. "He only gave me the note and said to either bring you back there or to come back myself if you didn't come."

"How long have you been working for the professor?"

"Since yesterday."

She gives you a sidelong look. "You don't look like the kind of person he'd employ."

"Maybe that's why he hired me."

She regards the school and the basement with obvious distaste when you get there, and gives you a dubious look after you've got the lock off and invited her in with "Ladies first." But she passes through.

That's just what you wanted. You step quickly behind her, grasp her about the torso, and grab at her face.

She elbows you in the gut. All the air goes out of you.

But though you weren't prepared, that blow didn't interrupt anything, and her motion stops as suddenly as it had started. When you straighten up again, wheezing hard, Lucy has vanished. There's only a pillar of rock there, albeit one with crude legs and arms and even a lump for a head. It is wearing Lucy Vredenburg's clothes.

It is, of course, the golem that Professor Blackwell made when he still had the grimoire earlier in the year. He had made a couple of masks, and a golem, and he had trapped Lucy Vredenburg and replaced her with a doppelganger. He even went out as her, hanging the mask of himself on the golem to keep his place warm while he had fun pretending to be a popular but snooty, leggy, blonde college cheerleader. It was lucky that you caught him in his own from at the college, and lucky that he has this hiding place for you to use. You only have to put her mask on and stash the golem next to the one Prescott made.

And you're very tingly all over as you think of who you're going to get to be.

* * * * *

There is some work to be done first. You have to get the golem down the steps without breaking it. Then you have to get it undressed. You have to get Blackwell's mentalia band out of yourself without accidentally pulling the Chen anima out, for you are still deeply averse to "killing" yourself by reverting to Prescott's personality. That last stumps you for a bit, for the same magic phrase releases both bands from the wearer. At last, Blackwell's mind suggests concentrating on the band you want to release, and that works. It feels good to get his mind out from yours.

But now you're ready for Lucy's mask. You can't resist glancing at the dusty mirror in the corner, and anticipating what you'll see when you wake.
1112111311222221222222122121211212112211.title: A Fast Retreat

choices: Use the anima band on this guy
1112111311222221222222122121211212112211.content:
You're too shocked and surprised by the noises outside to do anything but stare dumbly at the bedroom door. It can't be the gwarcheidwad -- the occult monster that guards Blackwell's house -- making the noise, even though that's what it sounds like. That creature can only walk the grounds between midnight and dawn, and it is at its weakest in the early hours of the day. But the noises outside -- It's the sound of heavy, booted feet kicking in doors and overturning furniture.

You back up against the window and stare hard at the door as the footsteps sound closer, coming up the stairs. They pass your door. A voice shouts hoarsely, and from the hall comes a resounding blow and the cracking and splintering of wood. Someone bellows -- the Blackwell 'bot.

Whatever is going on, you want to be dressed for it. You're in Kirkham's t-shirt and socks, but you quickly pull on his trousers. They are a bad fit for you, but they're better than nothing.

But though dressed to meet someone, you're not prepared to go out to meet them, not based on the noises you're hearing. Blackwell's voice abruptly cuts out, and something heavy thumps on the floor. More footsteps sound. A voice says something, and then another voice says something else. You brace backwards against the window as fast footsteps run down the hall. Doors bang open.

You lift the window behind you, but still you don't go out it.

Then the door to your room flies open, banging hard off the wall behind.

In the doorway stands a kid about your age. Your age, but he's quite a bit bigger, with rounded shoulders and powerful arms. His blonde hair shines like gold in the early morning light.

His eyes widen, and he grins. "Yo Frank," he shouts. "Blackwell's got a catamite!"

He moves so fast you've hardly time to blink before he's in your face, and then something like a bag goes over your head and you know no more.

* * * * *

When the world comes back, it's with all the brutality of a TV being switched on. You gasp and blink as cold air and pale sunlight hits your face. Something drops in your lap. It's a small, silver-colored sack. You blink at it dumbly.

"It's called a nightcap, asshole," says a cheery voice. You look up. That same blonde kid is looming over you, wearing the same manic grin as when he assaulted you. "And it's called a nightcap 'cos it goes over your head and makes you go beddy-bye for a bit. Makes it a lot easier dealing with people when they don't want to be dealt with." He drops onto his haunches. "Now that I've answered a question you didn't ask, care to answer a question I haven't asked? It'll be an obvious question, so you shouldn't have any trouble giving me the answer."

But your head is spinning too hard for you to even come up with questions of your own. You're sitting on something cold and hard, propped up against something equally cold and hard, and from the look and feel of things, you're outside. You glance around.

It's the bed of a pickup truck, you're outside sitting in the bed of a goddamn pickup truck.

Oh, and your arms are tied behind your back.

Your captor shifts on his feet, but his smile doesn't fade. "Is it really that hard? Here, we'll start with an easy one. How many fingers am I holding up?"

This is too much for you to take. "What?"

"How many fingers am I holding up?"

"What the fuck do I care?"

"You don't care, but I do. It's just to make sure that your head and eyes are working. How many fingers am I holding up?"

"Twelve," you exclaim impatiently, and struggle with your bonds. "Now what the fuck?"

"I'll go with the hypothesis that you employ a non-standard counting system when it comes to fingers," says the kid. "Now, what's your name, and why are you wearing David Kirkham's clothes?"

Your jaw drops, but you quickly recover. "Fuck you."

"Maybe you think I'm blowing smoke up your ass," says the kid. "Maybe you're thinking, How does he know I'm not David Kirkham, and why does he think these aren't my clothes? Well, for a start, those things you're wearing don't really fit you all that well, which might only indicate that you're really bad at dressing yourself, except that -- " He shows you something he's been holding. It's a wallet. "This was in your pocket." He opens it. "Sure, you might be David Kirkham, on account of you've got a couple of club IDs in his name, along with a bunch of coupons and even some cash." He takes each of these items out, one by one, and tosses them carelessly away. "But you also got his driver's license, and no matter how I try -- " He squinches up his face to peer at the license and then at you. "I can't get the face here to go with the face there." He throws the license over one shoulder, and the wallet over the other. "So come on, cupcake, I know it's early and you ain't had your coffee yet. But who are you, and why are you wearing David Kirkham's clothes?"

"And I said, Fuck you." There's nothing else you can tell him. You've got Prescott's face and Chen's brain, but the bodies associated with those names are still in circulation elsewhere.

Your captor doesn't cease looking merry. "Ooh, tough guy. Or just likes to act tough. Cool. Now I get to act tough." He cracks his knuckles one by one.

But your nerves are like flint. You don't care how much or how badly he hits you. In fact, since it'd be Prescott's body getting the blows, you'd kind of enjoy it.

The kid finishes cracking and pulling at his fingers. "I think I'll start at eleven with you," he says. "That way you know I'm not fucking around." He sits on one of your ankles, and you grimace. He grabs your other ankle, and with a wink at you pulls your sock off. "Now, if this spot doesn't work for you, just let me know." His grin sharpens, and he traces a fingertip down the sole of your foot.

Fuck! You didn't realize Prescott was so ticklish down there.

* * * * *

He's got a grip of iron, and no matter how hard you kick and squirm you can't break free of him. You shout and curse and bang your head against the cab of the truck, but your tormentor only laughs and keeps asking you for your name. "Come on," he says, "even captured spies are allowed to give their name, rank and serial number."

Relief, when it comes, appears from an unexpected place. Another voice sounds near you. Where the blonde kid has a rich tenor, this one is a gruff baritone. "Joe, what are you doing?"

"Interrogating."

"Are you getting anywhere?"

"No, but the journey's always half the fun." He winks at you. "Isn't it?"

"Fuck you!"

"Well, when you run out of questions of your own, ask him about this." A heavy item drops into the bed of the truck by your captor's feet. He stops torturing you long enough to pick it up and open it, though he keeps a hard grip on your bare foot.

"Whew," he says after reading the first page. "The Libra Personae," he says softly. "I definitely want to ask you about this. We totally gave up on finding this -- " He looks up at you sharply. "How long have you been taking off your shoes inside one of Professor Blackwell's bedrooms?"

"I don't know what you're talking about!"

"No?" He casts the book aside and resumes tickling you.

Tears finally burst from your eyes: that brief respite makes it all the worse when he starts torturing you again. But now you've got an idea on how to get out of this. "I don't know my name, okay? That's why I can't tell you!"

"How can you not know your name?"

"I don't know, that's why it's so -- ! Look, I think it's Will Prescott, but there's already a kid at school called that, and he looks just like me! But I also think my name is Gary Chen, except -- "

"Except what?"

"Except, do I look Chinese?"

The kid shrugs. "When the Libra is involved, anybody can look like anything, no matter their name. Where does David Kirkham fit into this mystery of yours?"

"Beats the fuck out of me! I woke up this morning in that bedroom, and I don't know how I got there. Only I looked in the mirror -- " You'd be proud of all this improvising if you weren't terrified that it'll get you into even worse trouble eventually. "And it wasn't my face, it was that Prescott kid's face. And I was wearing Kirkham's clothes, and I don't know why 'cos Kirkham's a friend of mine, but Prescott isn't, and -- "

"Okay, you're getting hysterical, but you're actually starting to make a little sense, believe it or not." He stops tickling you, and even gives the sole of your foot a good scratch, as though to rub away even the memory of the torture. "We'll take it back inside," he continues, and grasps your elbow to help you up. "It'll be more comfortable and you can talk about it from the beginning."

Talk about it from the beginning. What a dreadful threat. You have no idea what you'll say when the questions begin again.

The kid helps you out of the truck, which is parked just outside Blackwell's. "Frank'll get us some breakfast," he says. "Won't you?" he shouts.

"Won't I what?" comes a shouted reply.

"Go get us some breakfast. I ain't touching the shit Blackwell's got in his kitchen."

Then you're inside. You're taken into the library, and the kid pulls the bonds off you -- silver ropes. You hadn't noticed how numb your arms were, but they quickly come back to life. "I'll get you some water, though," says the kid. "Don't run off."

You rub yourself all over to get the circulation going. That's when you find the anima band in your pocket.
1112111311222221222222122121211212112211.title: Surprise Interrogation

1112111311222221222222122121211212112211.content:
It's a really dangerous idea you're playing with -- slapping the anima band onto the kid who took you prisoner. The band contains a copy of Gary Chen's anima, and if you put it on the kid then he'll be convinced that he's Gary Chen, the way you're convinced of it. Can the world stand to contain two assholes who think they're Gary Chen? You don't even want to let the real Gary Chen out.

Too late. It's like your hand has a mind of its own. "I brought you some water," says the kid as he comes back in. "And this box of cookies was still sealed up. Made by Keebler elves, but if they're bad for you its for solid nutri -- "

As he bends over the table, you strike like snake, slapping the band to his forehead. He reels back in surprise.

For one, two, five, seven seconds you hold your breath as the kid stares at you with wide, bright blue eyes. His mouth slackens, and his brow furrows slightly. You can see thoughts -- of what kind you're not sure -- rapidly coming together and coming apart again behind his gaze.

Then with something like an Apache war cry, he grabs you by the collar and throws you to the floor.

His eyes blaze and his color reddens as he sits on you, and he smashes you across the face with a blow that jars a couple of teeth loose. "You have ten seconds to explain what you just did to me, Prescott, before I fuck you up so hard -- "

"It's alright, man!" you shout. "I'm on your side! I had to do it because this guy you're in, he was trying to fuck things up for us!"

"You don't know the half of how he could fuck things up," the kid says, and his chest heaves. He pulls you up by your collar. "So explain it before it totally lose my shit and kick in your guts so hard your spleen'll stick to the ceiling!"

"It's complicated! But I know it's you, man! Chen Guangli! I know it's you! Look in the book!"

"The Libra!" he snarls, and drops you so he can grab the book off the table. He sits on you, though, pinning you to the floor while he flips through the grimoire. "Which fucking spell? Hurry. Frank'll be back in a few minutes and I need to know what's going on before -- "

"The eighth spell or something, I think. There's no explanation for what it does -- "

"A sigil'll be enough for me. Which one?"

"It's on a loose sheet of paper, stuck into the book. Lemme see it and I'll tell you -- Yeah, that's it," you tell him when he flashes the sheet at you. "What I did, see -- "

"Shtt!" He pinches your lips shut with his fingers and glares at the sheet, turning his head this way and that as he studies it intently. It looks like he's trying to drill holes in the paper with his eyes. His eyebrows work, and his lips move. A couple of times he swallows.

"Oh Christ," he says after a few moments. "Anima. It duplicates anima."

"Matsh what I wuzh zhaying," you mumble. He releases your lips. "Prescott trapped you under that mask he made of Chelsea. He made up a kind of goop that puts people to sleep, and he knocked you out with it. Then he made an anima band."

"Why are you talking about yourself in the third fucking person? I hate that, and it's not like I don't already hate you enough."

"I'm getting to that! Prescott made an anima band, but he didn't know what it did. So he tested it out on -- " You swallow. "On Chen. He took the mask off Chen and he put the anima band on him, so that -- "

"So that it copied my anima," the kid says impatiently. Then he freezes. "My anima," he repeats in a voice soft with horror.

"Yeah, he copied Chen's anima.Then he put it on himself. So you see what happened, right?" Your tone turns pleading, which you hate. "I've got Chen's anima in me. So I think I'm Chen Guangli, just like you do!"

"Fuck," the kid sneers.

"I can't help it, I do! Well, some other things happened after that, and after I figured out what the anima bands were, I went back and made another anima copy of Chen. I had it with me when, uh, what's this kid's name?"

"Fuck you, that's his name as far as you're concerned."

"Well, he busted in. I got the band onto him, and that's where we are now."

The kid stares at you, then straightens up and stares at the wall behind you. He puts his hand to his forehead, then covers your forehead with his other hand. "Shit," he says when he drops both hands. "I'm not who I think I am."

"No, and I'm not either. But you can't stop thinking of yourself that way. I've tried. You're thinking you're Chen Guangli."

"And neither of us is really -- " He makes a face. "The real guy is still -- Where -- ?" He swallows. "Where is he?"

"Stuck under that mask of Chelsea. He's asleep. He has no idea what's happening."

The kid groans. "What a fucking mess. Oh, God damn it, Prescott, if you had any idea -- " He looks around. "What are you doing in Aubrey Blackwell's house?"

"It's a long story. But who's this guy and -- ?"

"That's a long story too." He gets off you, though he doesn't help you up. "He's got a partner, God damn it, and it's gonna be fucking awkward when he gets back." He glances quickly about the room, as though the answer to the problem might be found written on the spines of the books. "We need to get alone, just the two of us, until we can compare notes better. Can you look like you're about to shit yourself? Oh, look who I'm asking, you always look like you're -- "

That's enough, and you get in his face. "I know who you think you're talking to, asshole, but I'm not gonna let you -- "

"I think you will," he says, and punches you in the chest so hard it almost feels like something has broken. "But leave the talking to me when Frank comes back. And sit down." He shoves you into a chair.

* * * * *

It's another five minutes before the front door opens, and in that time your alter ego has you quickly relate Prescott's history with the Libra from the beginning. You tell about his -- you still have to think of Prescott as "him" and not "me" -- finding the book at Arnholm's, of his and Caleb's early experiments, and how one really stupid conversation led first to the body swap with Gordon Black, then one with Dane Matthias, and finally one with Gary Chen. That's as far as you get when a tall, dark-haired youth appears in the doorway of the library. His gaze is very watchful as he looks between you and the blonde one. "I got you coffee and an Egg McMuffin," he says.

"And a third one for yourself?"

"No, I got a second one for myself."

"So the third one's for me?"

"The first one's for you and the second's for me. What are you talking about?"

"I'm talking about how I want two of them. Prescott here gets the fourth."

"I only got two, Joe."

"Then that gives me the excuse I need." He hauls you up by your shirt collar. "I'm taking him out to breakfast, since we both need more than what you brought."

"Is that wise?" says the one called Frank.

"He's harmless, Frank, I've already established that much. And it'll go friendlier over a Formica countertop with junk food between us." He pulls you to the doorway.

"You just want donuts," Frank grumbles as you press past him.

"No, I want to interrogate him in a friendlier environment. And I want donuts." A moment later you're at the door and outside. "Cocksucking asswipe," your alter ego mutters to himself. "Sanctimonious prick." Then he adds a few more phrases in a language you don't recognize.

"So this kid's name is Joe?"

"No, but you might as well call me that." He makes a face. "If I'm gonna call you 'Prescott' 'cos that's your face, you can call me 'Joe'." He says it glumly. "Alright, pick it up where you stopped."

So you continue the history as he drives you back into town to the nearest donut shop, and after you're seated there. And since you are for all practical purposes talking to yourself you share everything that Prescott was doing while under the mask of Gary Chen. "He was doing a pretty good job," you observe. "Better than I thought he could do."

"Don't break your arm patting yourself on the back. He had, well, our brain, while he was doing it." His whole manner during your talk has been pinched and irritable, and he now gives you a look of ill-disguised loathing. "I oughta tear that band off your forehead, put it on someone more deserving. Put it on Kirkham or -- I dunno." He sips his coffee, and his expression turns hooded.

A chill runs through you. "If you take it off me, I go back to thinking I'm Prescott."

"Like I give a shit. I'm on the loose now, cocksucker, and you're not doing anything about it. If I make you my bitch -- "

"Look, we're on the same team, motherfucker." You return his hot glare with one of your own. "I can't help it if I'm stuck in the body of this piece of shit -- "

"You are that piece of shit, you're just deluded enough to think -- "

"So are you!" You realize you're shouting, and though the shop is empty except for the help in the back, you lower your voice. "Think of it as the perfect revenge, that's all." You punch yourself in the chest. "We've been wanting to put this cocksucker in his place ever since he blindsided us. Well, he's fucked now, well and truly. He even thinks he's us!"

A gleam comes into Joe's eye, and a smirk to his lips. But you're not sure if he's laughing with you or at you.
1112111311222221222222122121211212112211.title: Another You, So to Speak

1112111311222221222222122121211212112211.content:
This kid called "Joe" -- who you've infected with the brain of Gary Chen, the same way you've infected Will Prescott's body with Chen's brain -- gives you a long and penetrating look. "I need you to make another anima band," he says when you warily ask what he's thinking.

"What for?"

"My partner. That fuckwit back at the professor's house. We can't leave him running loose."

"Who is he? Who are you?"

"You know who I am, you fuck. Let's keep it at that." His face is very hard.

You feel your own face muscles tighten. "Then fuck you, if you're gonna be like that. You can make a fucking anima band on your own. Why should I suck you off?"

Fire briefly flares in his eyes, then dies back. It doesn't go away entirely, though, and you can still see it behind his smile when he shows you his teeth. "You need this thing too, asshole," he says. "But let's pretend you're doing it for me as a favor. Do it for me, and you can name your price."

You'd double-cross this asshole in a second; and since he's basically you, you know he'll double-cross you too, so this "name your price" line is just bullshit.

But you don't dare show weakness in front of him. "Pay me first," you retort. "Then maybe I'll help."

"So what's your price?"

"You tell me what the fuck is going on here. Who the fuck these guys are" -- you jab a finger at his chest -- "and why you need to plant another copy of 'us' into that guy back at the professor's. That's who you want it for, right? That one who brought back the food?"

Joe fixes you with a long, intense stare. His smile isn't pretty when he shows it to you again.

"Okay, you wanna know who these assholes are? Watch this." He snaps a finger, then holds out his palm.

You blink and do a double take. There's an open flame dancing there.

It's four inches tall, and it dances lightly in the draught. There is no wick or fuel that you can see, and when you put out a finger he lets you touch it. But it's hot, and you quickly pull your hand away. "How the fuck are you doing that?"

"Magic," he says. "Real, fucking magic. Don't tell me you don't believe in it."

"Fuck, you expect me to shit myself over some parlor trick after what I've seen? What I've done?"

His smile is cold and thin. "It's not a parlor trick and it's not book magic, not like what Prescott found or what that dumbass professor plays around with. This is Harry Potter, magic-in-the-blood stuff. As in, this kid here and that asshole we left back at Blackwell's, are natural-born warlocks. If I wanted to fuck you up, I could, easily, and the other one could fuck you up even worse."

You've no reason to believe him, but it's easiest to go along with him. "Okay, so what?"

"So like I say, the other guy could fuck you up worse, fuck me up too, if he finds out I'm running another operating system now." He douses the flame. "I can't take that risk, and you can't either. So we can't leave him running loose."

"Alright, but who are these guys?"

"They call themselves Frank and Joe." He snorts. "Like a the dumb fucks they are, they named themselves after the Hardy Boys. They're a couple of juvenile do-gooders, go around stomping on fuckers like Professor Aubrey Blackwell, and like you. In fact, they came to town trying to find that magic book you picked up, even enrolled at Eastman. That's how dumb they are, they hit the wrong school. Anyway, they gave up after a while, but not until after they'd latched onto Blackwell, and they decided to take him out as long as they were here." He snorts. "Your fucking bad luck that they had to wait until the morning after Halloween before they could get past his defenses, and Jesus, why the fuck am I telling you all this?" He punches himself in the side of the head, and says a string of harsh words in a language you don't understand.

"Anyway," he continues, "as long as I'm in this fucker's head, there's no fucking way I can go back home, back to this guy's home, I mean. They'll spot me hiding out in his head. So I gotta get undercover, and get undercover before any of his friends come looking for me. I could try to ditch Frank, but it'd be better if he came along too. Safer. Besides, he could be useful." He flashes you a brief glare. "More useful than you."

He settles back again. "And that's all you need to know. So, payment granted. Now you're gonna go make me that anima band, right?" A dangerous light comes into his eye.

You shrug. "And after you get it onto this other fuck, what're you gonna do? Bust outta town?"

"Sure," he says. You don't believe him, and from the smirk on his face it looks like he doesn't care that you don't believe him.

You doubt you've got much choice but to agree.

But that doesn't mean you have to go along with him to the end.

In fact, you're already getting an idea. "You say you wanna hide. You want me to make a couple of masks for you while I'm at it?"

He freezes, and you can practically read his thoughts: What's the planned double-cross? "We can talk about that later," he says. "You just need to get that band made while keep Frank distracted."

"I need the sigil."

"Wait here and I'll go get it."

He rises, but he only takes two steps before whirling on you. "It's that loose sheet of paper you showed me, right?" His eyes narrow when you nod. "Did you rip it out of the book?"

"No, it was -- " In a halting way you describe how it seemed to form under the moonlight when you laid a blank sheet of paper on the spell. Joe's eyes harden as you talk.

"Huh," he says when you're done. "That's interesting." He gives you an appraising look. "Don't go anywhere until I get back. Because I will find you if you run off."

You tamp down a desire to flip him off, and just lift your lip to show him a tooth. His eyes glitter in reply.

* * * * *

"Look, I just need to see Chelsea one more time," you tell Johansson. "Chen, I mean."

"You need to get me a mask for Patterson," he snarls back. "That's what you promised me. And why do I need to meet you back at the old clubhouse?"

"Because that's where I am, and I'm stuck here for the duration," you yell into the cell phone. That Joe guy had dropped you off here after picking you back up from the donut shop. "Don't ask me why, it's complicated."

"If you're out there, you can make me a mask, can't you?"

"Bring me the supplies," you tell him, and kick angrily at an old, broken desk. It's cold -- foully cold -- in the basement, and you can barely keep yourself warm by pacing the concrete floor. "No, tell you what, do it this way. You bring Chelsea out here, drop her off, and then you go buy some supplies. I'll be done with Chelsea by the time you get back."

"What do you need her for?"

"I told you, I need to copy -- "

"I mean, why does she have to stay there with you?"

"Don't be a jealous prick, Johansson. She's under your control, I can't do anything with her."

"You'll wanna try," he grumbles, but agrees to your plan.

Once you're done with him, you call that Joe guy at the number he gave you -- you need him to bring over the Libra so you can shut up Johansson with a new mask. But he refuses your request: "You just call me when you get that anima band made. After that, we'll talk. I got a character to maintain out here, and bringing you the Libra ain't part of it. I had to talk real fast to explain why I even let you go."

So you have to make some fast talk yourself when Johansson -- in the guise of Seth Javits -- shows up later with Chelsea on his arm. He's incredulous at the excuse you give him. "A gang of Chen clones?" he gasps. "Why the fuck do we want a gang of that fucker running around?"

"Because he's got a full life," you bridle. "We split things up -- Look, just go get the shit on this list so I can make you that mask for Patterson." You give him a scrap of paper that has as much written on it as you can remember for making masks.

When he's gone, you get the Chelsea mask off the guy who's underneath it, and put the newly made anima band on him. You have ten minutes to contemplate him, and how sexy he is, even squeezed into the Chelsea's clothes -- which is a fucking embarrassing look for him. When you've got it out, you replace the Chelsea mask and call Joe. This time you tell him of the trouble you're in with Johansson. "Once I got the band on Frank, I'll get you the Libra," he says. "I'm on my way now."

It's like an airport terminal or something for the next little bit. Joe comes and goes, and then Johansson comes back. You pretend to have forgotten a key ingredient and send him away again, taking Chelsea with him.

Then Joe shows up again. You eye him warily as he comes down the stairs. He's got a book under his arm, but you don't like the smile on his face. "Just stay right there," you tell him. "Drop the book there and get out."

"You really think you can stop me?" he chortles. "Fuck, not even Frank could stop me when I went at him."

He vanishes.

You just have time to register the blur where he'd been standing before the world goes dark.
1112111311222221222222122121211212112211.title: Just Enough Back Story to Motivate Your Ass

1112111311222221222222122121211212112211.content:
The light returns just as abruptly as it had vanished. You blink and shake your head, then glower at the grinning blonde guy. "Got ya again, asshole," Joe says, and tosses the silver bag over his shoulder.

You look around. You're sitting in a living room you've never seen before. It's a dingy room lit only by a small window covered with some Venetian blinds. There's a sour smell in the air, and the sofa you're sitting on is soft in the way that old pieces of furniture with saggy springs get soft.

You shift, and find that your hands are bound in those same silver ropes. Your arms are completely numb, and feel like they weigh a couple of tons when you try to lift them. "Okay, you made your fucking point," you growl. "And thanks for getting me away from Johansson. What now?"

He laughs. "Now we -- "

He breaks off and looks over as a new figure comes in. It's the dark-haired kid you'd glimpsed earlier. He's taller than the blonde kid, and though his muscles aren't obviously bigger, something in the way he prowls into the room makes him seem a lot bigger. You flinch at the sight of the bulging biceps, and can't bring yourself to look him in his dark eyes.

He stares down at you. "Do I get to twist his fucking head off now," he says in a baritone rumble.

"Not yet," says Joe. "I told you, it depends on what I find out about him. Give it here," he snaps, and from the other one he takes a large sketchpad and a pencil. "Now take the dingus off him."

You rear back as the dark-haired one -- Frank, Joe had called him -- leans over you with a cold and merciless expression on his face. He pauses, though, when Joe says, "Hold it."

The blonde kid now leans in to leer at you. His expression is a lot merrier than the other's, but it is just as lacking in pity. "We're gonna take that anima band off you, Prescott," he says softly. "The thing that makes you think you're Gary Chen."

"The fuck," you gasp, and try to backpedal.

"We're gonna take it off you, and then I'm gonna ask you some questions. You don't want to lie to me. It wouldn't do you any good, and it might do you a lot of bad. You know who you're dealing with, so you -- Wait, you don't, really, do you. Hit him, bro."

Frank doesn't have to be told twice. His fist smashes into your gut.

In the agony that ensues -- it's like a wave of hot, acidic blood has engulfed you -- you don't even feel the anima band come off your forehead. You do feel the departure of Gary Chen's mind, however. And with it goes all the false courage you had borrowed from him. When you open your eyes again, the faces of the other two are like the faces of hungry, sadistic animals. And when Frank seizes your hair by its roots and pulls your head up, you burst into tears.

Joe only laughs, then asks you your name.

* * * * *

As awful as it was to be hit by Frank, you'd let him hit you a dozen more times if it let you escape the five hours that ensued. The one called Joe -- an Eastman student possessed by the awful mind and will and personality of Gary Chen -- interrogated you hard, and when you failed to give him a quick enough answer, he'd smash you hard across the face. Most of the afternoon, Frank stood behind him, arms folded, with a cold scowl on his face.

That was bad enough, but the questions were worse. They made no sense. Three times Joe probed you, starting each new session with the same biographical questions: Where were you born, and when; where were your parents and brother born, and when; and where have you spent your life? After that the questions would diverge, though into parallel channels having to do with your school life, your hobbies, your likes and dislikes; and then they would diverge further into strange and nonsensical questions that seem to have no purpose but to baffle and anger you: Which do you prefer, strawberries or the number five? Would you rather have a sixth finger on each hand, or a third eye? What would be a good name for the first city established on Pluto?

As you answer these questions, he sketches and doodles on that pad. Three times he completes some kind of sketch, glares at it, tears it from his pad and crumples it up, and starts over again with you. His temper becomes shorter and nastier as he proceeds. Frank leaves once to run an errand, and on his return disappears into another room.

There is only one thing to be said in favor of this stupid and exhausting routine: It keeps you from dwelling on the horrible position you have trapped yourself in. What were you thinking, playing around with dangerous magic without instructions? Obviously, you weren't thinking. But you've been well and truly repaid for your folly. You shudder and fight down the vomit when you remember the loathsome feeling of having Gary Chen's soul crawl and swarm over yours, and the way he moved your limbs and thought your thoughts. He was inside you, being you, making you do and think things and thoughts that weren't yours. And the worst part of it was that he hated being inside you even worse than you hated having him there.

And now he's inside these other two guys, guys who apparently have magical powers without needing to use books or spells. The glittering malice in Joe's eyes is unmistakable, and Frank looks like he can barely keep himself from stomping you into a puddle of blood and shit. With Chen's personality in control of them, and without anything like the faux brotherhood that came from having his personality inside you, there will be nothing to save you from them.

The end, when it comes, will be long and painful, you are sure.

Only Joe's slaps keep you from passing out from fear and despair.

* * * * *

The third time Joe rips a sheet off that pad, he doesn't start the questions up again. Instead, he yells for the other: "Frank!"

A dark silhouette appears in the entryway. "Don't call me that, fucker."

"The fuck am I supposed to call you, then?" Joe retorts. "If you don't like that name, go put on Kirkham's mask. Here, I'll get his clothes for you." He tears at your jeans, and you are powerless to stop him.

"What's the verdict?" Frank asks as he advances. You draw a deep breath to keep the vomit of panic down.

"The bad news is you can't kill him. Or, I guess you could," says Joe as he finishes pulling Kirkham's pants and underwear off you. "But he's more useful alive."

"You mean -- ?"

"Yep. Sulva and Kenandandra."

Frank turns cold, murderous eyes on you. "Can't be," he says. "A dribbling little cum-pump like him?"

"It makes sense when you add the probabilities up. God knows I'd cut my own balls off before betting on him being -- " Joe draws a ragged breath. "But that's what the charts say." He shrugs. "Of course, if you want to kill him anyway -- "

"Could we?" You hate the hopeful tone in his voice.

"Sure. But it's better to, um -- " Joe picks up the anima band from an end table and waggles it in the air.

"Shit. Fuck. I'd kill myself if I got stuck inside that -- "

"I would too," says Joe. "I'm sure he almost did. But I don't think he will, not if we put it back on him and explain the situation."

A hard, slow hiss saws in and out over Frank's teeth. "He's your pet, then." He hocks up a loogie, and splats it all over your face. "And I guess I got him to thank for being here -- " He raises a fist, and gives his arm and bicep a glance-over, and a ray of admiration flickers briefly on his face. "But I'll leave it you to look at him. Keep him away from me." He snatches up the underwear and jeans from the floor and leaves the room with them.

"Don't mind Frank," Joe says when he's gone. "You gotta understand that he's -- Oh, hell." He laughs nastily. "Let me put this back on you, and you'll understand totally why he feels that way about you." With a numbing blow slams the anima band back onto your forehead.

* * * * *

It sinks into you and opens up and Gary Chen flows out into every crack and crevice in your body, filling and numbing you with his poisonous contempt. "Oh, fuck," you sigh. "Here we are again."

"Yeah, here we are again," Joe says. "I'm gonna take those ropes off you now. Don't get any ideas, because I can punch you in the balls so fast you won't even see me making a fist."

"Just tell me what the fuck all that was about," you retort as he undoes the ropes. Feeling instantly returns to your arms, and you wince as you rub them. They're so uselessly skinny.

"It was a test, and you passed. Well, Prescott passed. You'd probably be happier if he flunked. I don't know how I feel." He cocks his head. "But you'll have your uses."

"So explain it to me." You sit up, and that bile that Prescott had been pushing down comes roaring up. You choke on it, swallow it down again, and make a face.

"Well, the short version is that Prescott is magic too, the same way these two fuckers are." Joe points to himself and back over his shoulder. "It was one shot in billion," he continues as you stare at him, "but Prescott's shit body is a lottery winner."
1112111311222221222222122121211212112211.title: The Worst Test of Your Life

1112111311222221222222122121211212112211.content:
As you sit up, Joe pushes you back down and puts a strong knee into your chest. "Okay, first of all, you need to remember who you are and where you are."

"And where's that?" you snarl back at him. "Remember who and where you are, cocksucker."

"I know exactly who and where I am," he retorts, and doesn't bother to hide the gloat in his voice. "That's what makes me awesome. You, on the other hand, are stuck in that shit Prescott. Frank and I, on the other hand -- "

He sniggers, and he presses that knee down harder on you as he leans over you, putting his face near yours.

"So here's the situation," he says. "For ten years, these two cocksuckers have been training to use their mutant powers. We ain't hardly showed you what they can do, but they got ten years of training behind them. Remember that, because you got shit inside you, Prescott. Yeah, you got Chen Guangli inside you," he snorts, "same as me and Frank. But it's stuck inside Prescott, and Prescott's just a snot-nosed little shit. So maybe you got something special going on with that carcass, but you don't know what it is or how to use it. You're starting with shit, man, and you'll learn shit and do shit if you cop an attitude with me. You heard what Frank said? How'd he'd rather twist your head off? There's only one thing between you and him, and that's me. And don't count on me being on your side, either."

He gets off you, and his lip curves so hard with contempt it looks like it hurts. "I told you earlier I should tear that thing off you and put it on someone else. I might yet, just so I don't have to look at you."

"So go ahead and do it," you snarl back. "I don't like being here. Put me in someone so I'll -- "

Joe reaches for your forehead. But instead of letting him grab you, you grab his wrist. You lock eyes.

Very long moments pass, and you have plenty of time to feel the sweat popping out all over your body -- your bare legs and hips and crotch, and the torso still wrapped in Kirkham's shirt. It's a horrifying thought, the idea of being ripped out of a body, even out of Prescott's. You couldn't bring yourself to do it earlier, and now you're stopping Joe from doing it. You stare back at him without blinking, and strain to keep his arm immobile. Your face feels very cold and pale.

His grin is a pale and loathsome thing. "Yeah, I figured you'd fight me," he says softly. "That's the nature of the thing. Anyway, all other things being equal, I'd rather leave it on you, because like I say, you'll be useful in the long run." He pulls back. "But you didn't stop me from pulling it off you. I can get that thing off you any time I want. So just remember, I am totally the boss of you, and you will totally do what I say. You'll want to anyway," he continues, and you can barely hear him over the roar of blood in your ears. "That's the way you'll learn."

"Learn what?" Your breath comes in painful gasps.

"How to use your own mutant powers. Prescott's, I mean. That's another reason you don't want me pulling that thing off you. In the long run, you'll be better off with that body than inside another. And we can hide you so we don't have to be constantly reminded of what a little shit you actually are." He glances over his shoulder with a frown. "The fuck is keeping my brother?"

"What's he doing with Kirkham's mask?"

"Going home as him. Someone has to keep his place warm, and I need to talk to you here." He stabs at you with a finger. "Stay. I'll be back with some clothes." He looks you up and down speculatively, then with a muttered "Fuck" leaves the room.

* * * * *

He'd gone for awhile, which leaves you plenty of time to think in a painfully useless way about what he said about Prescott having "mutant" powers. When he returns, a shirtless Kirkham is with him. The latter snaps his fingers at you, and you take off the shirt and toss it to him. Joe and the replacement for David Kirkham exchange some muttered words, then the latter goes out the front door.

Joe tosses you some athletic shorts and a t-shirt, both very large and sloppy on Prescott's scarecrow frame. Then he sits down as you pull them on, and with a lot more patience than he'd shown earlier in the day he explains things to you.

It's a long story, but you pick up on it pretty quickly. It's all mixed up with astrology and the planets, he says. Each of the planets -- which include the Sun and the Moon as well as the rest of them, from Mercury through Pluto -- are capable of conferring spooky supernatural powers upon human beings. For whatever reason, though, only a few dozen people ever get them, and they always get a unique combination from being born under a conjunction of two of them. This Joe character, for instance, gets his mutant magic powers from the Sun and from Mercury; the Frank guy gets his from Mars and Saturn; and Prescott for whatever bizarre reason gets some powers from Pluto and the Moon.

"It must be the Moon that explains why no one ever found him," Joe muses. "There aren't any 'Moon' members in the club, and the Moon is associated with eclipses. So Prescott was invisible, blacked out."

That "club"? It's called the Stellae Errantes, and he snorts over it. "Buncha fucking do-gooders," he says. "There's all kinds of cool things out there. Vampires, and witches, and warlocks. Monsters. Demons. Great shit like that, and these ass-clowns are always trying to stamp 'em out. There's a couple of dozen guys in that club, and Joe and Frank have been studying for ten years so they could join it." He laughs. "Their first mission, they mess up so bad." He thumps his chest. "Now we're in charge of them. Fuck, we're gonna have some fun with the Libra once we get to a safe space."

That's the real trick, apparently. The Chen-possessed Frank and Joe can't return to their colleagues without the change being spotted, so they are going to have to go into hiding. "Some masks will help," he says. "But we can't count on them for sure. They're supposed to head back home sometime this coming week. We'll probably do that, lay a false trail, stage a disappearance, then come back here. You can have some masks ready for us by then."

"Then what?"

"Then we'll start training you. With your 'eclipse' ability, we might be able to hide out in the shadows until we figure out what we're going to do."

"What was all that stuff with the sketch pad about?"

"That was just me figuring out if you were legit or not. Yeah." He peers at you keenly. "I guess I'm glad I tried that. Now that I think about it, it really is best that you stick it out inside Prescott."

"Why's that?"

"On account of all those fakes you made. Christ. Ten of them. That's what put me onto you. Didn't you -- ?" He breaks off, and his eyes widen. "Oh, Jesus, what a waste of fucking time this has been. Didn't the professor's shadow mind tell you what was going on?"

"Shadow mind?" you ask, but you're asking it of Joe's retreating back, for he's leaped up and run from the room. He's back a moment later, though, with a silver band in his hand. He hands it to you, and you see it's the brain-band you made of Professor Aubrey Blackwell. You hadn't noticed it had gone missing from your own head.

"Yeah, I took that off you right after I put the nightcap on you," he says. "If you put it back on, you'd probably be better able to follow this shit I'm explaining to you."

"I think I'm following okay. I didn't like having it in me, anyway." You drop the band onto the end table, as though it's a spider.

Joe snickers. "Have it your way. But didn't you wonder how come you managed to make so many fakes?"

"Yeah, I did," you reply. "I was concentrating on other things, though."

"Uh huh. Well, so was I, but this fucker has a good brain and can think about lots of things simultaneously, so that was one of the things I thought about. Also, the way you got the anima to imprint itself under moonlight onto a blank sheet of paper. Prescott's got some pretty powerful essentia if he can make that many golems, and the moonlight was pretty suggestive of Sulva. That's these fucks' name for the Moon, by the way. So I told Frank, after I got him straightened out, that I wanted to test you out. Even then I didn't really think you'd turn out to be a Stellae, and I ran the test three different times, just to make sure I wasn't getting a false positive. Jesus." He shakes his head. "Although it's kind of obvious in retrospect." He doesn't amplify on that last remark.

* * * * *

"So what's the plan?" you ask when you've been over things a couple of times with him, just to make sure you understand what's up. You're feeling a lot more patient with him, and he seems more patient with you, too.

"Frank's taken over for Kirkham, at least until tomorrow," Joe says. "We need to pack up the shit we took out of Blackwell's, and then we need to make a quick trip out of town in a couple of days before doubling back here."

"I've got Johansson riding my ass," you tell him. "What should I do about that?" You have to explain the situation to Joe before he can say anything useful.

And even then he's not sure what to say or do about Prescott's old friend. "I should go talk to Frank about it," he says.
1112111311222221222222122121211212112211.title: The Story Behind the Test

1112111311222221222222122121211212112211.content:
You make to follow Joe out the door, but he turns and shoves you back. "You stay here. Remember what I told you about Frank not liking your face."

"He better get used to it, 'cos I'm not going anywhere!"

"I don't like it a lot either, don't forget. But stay here. There's some cash in the kitchen drawer," he adds. "Order in a pizza if you want. We're not gonna be hurting for cash once we get started."

You storm back into the living room after he's gone. There's got to be some way of dealing with these assholes.

Except you didn't get where you are by losing your temper needlessly. (On the other hand, losing your temper when it's useful has done you a lot of good.) It sounds like you'd be in a worse place if you hadn't slapped that anima band onto this "Joe" person, and these magic mutant powers they're bragging about could be useful to you. If they can teach some of them to you, you'll be in great shape.

The trouble is that they think you're useless. And because they know -- as well as you do -- that you're really Prescott, they hate you as much as you hate ... well, you.

The thing to do, then, is to stay on their good side -- without being a total pussy -- until you can figure out a way of getting on top of them.

So you leave the cash where Joe said it was. Instead, you ransack the place for clues about who they are.

* * * * *

You don't find much. Some school books and papers and a computer with a password you can't get around. Their names are Frank and Joe Durras, and they go to Eastman High. Based on the scores of the papers you find, Frank was apparently the smart one; Joe's scores, on the other hand, are unbelievably bad.

There are two bedrooms, only one of which looks like it's been used, which suggests one of two things. Either one of them is tidy in a super-anal way, or the "brother" thing is just an act and they're actually gay lovers. Both possibilities leave you snickering.

You find a few more books in the bedrooms, including some of the sort that Blackwell had in his library, but Joe took his brain-band with him so there's not much use in trying to read them.

Oh, and speaking of Blackwell -- you find his petrified corpse in the garage, still dressed. The mask is nowhere to be found. Apparently while they were taking care of you, they took the mask off the golem and ditched it someplace.

You do find one useful book, though. It's called Shadows of the Planets, and it turns out to be a fairly non-technical history of this Stellae Errantes organization and how it works. You take it into the living room and start reading it.

It tells you, in some more detail, what Joe had told you. According to the book, every human being is to some degree or another bound up with the planets -- the Sun, Mercury, Venus, the Moon, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, Neptune, and Pluto -- each of which has a specific and individual set of virtues. These virtues contribute, to a greater or lesser degree, to the personality, dispositions and talents of the people that they are entangled with. (The actual entanglement, so near as you can tell, has to do with the essentia of each person being "alchemically oriented" to one or more planets.) When certain conjunctions occur, this either has the effect of orienting some newborn's essentia to two of the planets, or if someone is already so oriented, of casting a beneficent glow upon them. This means that there is always at least, and at most, one person in the world who gets the maximum benefits from being born under that conjunction.

With forty-five possible conjunctions, there are thus only forty-five people in the world with such magical mutant powers. Each conjunction bestows a unique set of powers upon the person born under it, and these powers tend to change and shift in each generation of holder. Eagerly, you turn to the sections that describe the planets that Joe said belong to Prescott -- and thus to you.

The Moon, or "Sulva" as the book has it: "Associated with restless, undirected energies. Adepts tend to be wanderers and daydreamers. They disappear for long stretches of time, drifting they themselves know not where, or retreating into inner spaces so deep that they vanish from sight without leaving home. They are also sometimes mirrors, creating solitude for themselves by reflecting back what others wish to see, thus creating a protective shell around themselves. Personalities associated with Sulva tend to be introverted, quiet, dreamy, footloose. Their prodigies will be associated with silences and vacuums: invisibility, stealth, disguise."

Restless, dreamy, footloose? Yeah, you'd call that a super-charitable description of Prescott. Cocksucker needs a good slap. But the "stealth and disguise" bit sounds promising.

Of Pluto, or "Kenandandra" the book says: "Associated with industry and organization. Adepts are able to craft or disassemble spells, sigils or magical items with great ease and skill. They also have a tremendous worth ethic, and will pursue a problem as a Malacandran will pursue an enemy. They are also great organizers. Personalities tend to be somewhat metallic and rigid, in the way that engineers often are, and they sometimes exhibit obsessive-compulsive or neurotic tendencies."

Fuck. That sounds like something good, and you're pretty god damn sure Prescott hasn't got it. You wonder if Joe did the horoscope right.

But in the back of your mind there drifts a thought: A "great organizer"? Prescott whipped your business into smart shape when he had control of it. But you savagely kick the thought around until it dies. It was your brain, you tell yourself, that got all that done. Not his.

Well, if Joe is right about Prescott's talent, maybe some of this training will bring it out.

* * * * *

While you are reading this book, Joe calls you, asking for some info about the masks you've been scattering about: Who is wearing what masks, and where are they?

It takes you awhile to remember and communicate it all, and you snarl back when he snaps at you. But bit by bit you get it all listed for him.

You have six people working for you: George Mendoza, Joe Thomason, Tanner Evans, Joshua Call, Jeff Spencer, and Jamie Rennerhoff. Associated with each is a golem, and these golems collectively are hiding the original persons of Douglas Canfield, Andrea Varnsworth, Craig Wyatt, Jelena Petrovich, Sienna Goldman, and Fatima Zahedi. In addition, you have a mask of Adam Karter sitting on Adam Karter, of Grandpa Chen sitting on Grandpa Chen, and of Gary Chen sitting on David Kirkham.

What about Johansson? What masks and golems has he got? Joe asks you.

He's wearing a mask of Seth Javits. Javits is wearing a mask of Johansson.

Does that mask of Johansson have any of that golem paste inside it?

You have to think about that question. Then you remember some comment Caleb said not too long ago -- he made some paste and used it to put that mask under his control. He's also got some of that paste inside the mask of Chelsea Cooper, which is sitting on ... sigh ... Gary Chen's face. And his essentia is inside a spare golem that's hidden in the basement.

Finally, Joe asks about what other masks are on the loose.

There's a mask of Dane Matthias sitting on Chelsea Cooper's face. It hasn't got a brain-band in it, though. Same as the mask of Gordon Black that's sitting on Matthias's face, and the mask of Will Prescott that's sitting on Black.

Any others?

No. Wait, there's a mask of Martin Gardinhire sitting around somewhere.

Joe hangs up without answering any of your questions, and you go back to reading. An hour later he calls back, and tells you that they've got three ideas, and they can't agree on which one to go for, and they want you to be the tie-breaker. You listen to the proposals, talk them through, consult your gut, and render a decision. Joe hangs up, and tells you that he'll be back home soon to help you get set up for another move.

* * * * *

Because that's what the consultation was about: You need a new hiding place.

That's because the "Frank" member of your three-body brain will be taking permanent control of David Kirkham. You resent the plan, but the two of them insist: Frank is the natural to act as an enforcer for your gang, and since he is being managed by the same Chen-intelligence that is running all three of these bodies, he will know exactly what to do.

That leaves you and Joe needed a hiding place. He definitely needs a long-term hiding place, and with Kirkham's mask riding around on Frank's face, so do you. He was quite blunt about the fate of whoever the two of you replace -- they will be turned into golems, the same as your grandpa, and hidden away. These will, more or less, be your new permanent faces. And they will have to be faces that put you and Joe close together -- living with each other, so he can help you train without it looking weird.

One possibility, he explained, was for you to return to being Will Prescott, while Joe took the part of Caleb Johansson -- or you could take the part of Johansson while Joe found another personality to play. Johansson's mom would be turned into another golem, so that your group would have complete control of a small house to hide out in.

Another possibility was to replace two guys who were either dropouts or on the verge of dropping out, and renting a place as roommates. Here, the best choice would be Matt Reston, the bass player you've got running the weed supply into the Warehouse. Once you -- or Joe -- was in for Reston, you'd figure out another person who could room with him, and replace that person.

Finally, you could find a pair of siblings to replace. The obvious choice here would be the Garner girls, Eva and Jessica.

After talking it over, you settled on --
1112111311222221222222122121211212112211.title: A Little Snooping of Your Own

1112111311222221222222122121211212112211.content:
You chose the last option -- the "Garner Gambit" -- for a couple of reasons. One was that Joe did not sound real enthusiastic about it, and you liked the idea of needling him.

But at bottom it was just the most appealing of the ideas. Turning yourself and this dumb partner into Reston and a friend of his would put you and him too close to each other.
